39:3:15: But Jesus answering said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Suffer it now: for thus it becometh us to fulfil all righteousness</font>. Then he suffereth him.
39:4:4: But he answered and said, <font color="#CC0000">It is written, Man shall not live by bread alone, but by every word that proceedeth out of the mouth of God</font>.
39:4:7: Jesus said unto him, Again <font color="#CC0000">it is written, Thou shalt not make trial of the Lord thy God</font>.
39:4:10: Then saith Jesus unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Get thee hence, Satan: for it is written, Thou shalt worship the Lord thy God, and him only shalt thou serve</font>.
39:4:17: From that time began Jesus to preach, and to say, <font color="#CC0000">Repent ye; for the kingdom of heaven is at hand</font>.
39:4:19: And he saith unto them, Come ye after <font color="#CC0000">me, and I will make you fishers of men</font>.
39:5:3: <font color="#CC0000">Blessed are the poor in spirit: for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.</font>
39:5:4: <font color="#CC0000">Blessed are they that mourn: for they shall be comforted.</font>
39:5:5: <font color="#CC0000">Blessed are the meek: for they shall inherit the earth.</font>
39:5:6: <font color="#CC0000">Blessed are they that hunger and thirst after righteousness: for they shall be filled.</font>
39:5:7: <font color="#CC0000">Blessed are the merciful: for they shall obtain mercy.</font>
39:5:8: <font color="#CC0000">Blessed are the pure in heart: for they shall see God.</font>
39:5:9: <font color="#CC0000">Blessed are the peacemakers: for they shall be called sons of God.</font>
39:5:10: <font color="#CC0000">Blessed are they that have been persecuted for righteousness’ sake: for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.</font>
39:5:11: <font color="#CC0000">Blessed are ye when men shall reproach you, and persecute you, and say all manner of evil against you falsely, for my sake.</font>
39:5:12: <font color="#CC0000">Rejoice, and be exceeding glad: for great is your reward in heaven: for so persecuted they the prophets that were before you.</font>
39:5:13: <font color="#CC0000">Ye are the salt of the earth: but if the salt have lost its savor, wherewith shall it be salted? it is thenceforth good for nothing, but to be cast out and trodden under foot of men.</font>
39:5:14: <font color="#CC0000">Ye are the light of the world. A city set on a hill cannot be hid.</font>
39:5:15: <font color="#CC0000">Neither do men light a lamp, and put it under the bushel, but on the stand; and it shineth unto all that are in the house.</font>
39:5:16: <font color="#CC0000">Even so let your light shine before men; that they may see your good works, and glorify your Father who is in heaven.</font>
39:5:17: <font color="#CC0000">Think not that I came to destroy the law or the prophets: I came not to destroy, but to fulfil.</font>
39:5:18: <font color="#CC0000">For verily I say unto you, Till heaven and earth pass away, one jot or one tittle shall in no wise pass away from the law, till all things be accomplished.</font>
39:5:19: <font color="#CC0000">Whosoever therefore shall break one of these least commandments, and shall teach men so, shall be called least in the kingdom of heaven: but whosoever shall do and teach them, he shall be called great in the kingdom of heaven.</font>
39:5:20: <font color="#CC0000">For I say unto you, that except your righteousness shall exceed the righteousness of the scribes and Pharisees, ye shall in no wise enter into the kingdom of heaven.</font>
39:5:21: <font color="#CC0000">Ye have heard that it was said to them of old time, Thou shalt not kill; and whosoever shall kill shall be in danger of the judgment:</font>
39:5:22: <font color="#CC0000">but I say unto you, that every one who is angry with his brother shall be in danger of the judgment; and whosoever shall say to his brother, Raca, shall be in danger of the council; and whosoever shall say, Thou fool, shall be in danger of the hell of fire.</font>
39:5:23: <font color="#CC0000">If therefore thou art offering thy gift at the altar, and there rememberest that thy brother hath aught against thee,</font>
39:5:24: <font color="#CC0000">leave there thy gift before the altar, and go thy way, first be reconciled to thy brother, and then come and offer thy gift.</font>
39:5:25: <font color="#CC0000">Agree with thine adversary quickly, while thou art with him in the way; lest haply the adversary deliver thee to the judge, and the judge deliver thee to the officer, and thou be cast into prison.</font>
39:5:26: <font color="#CC0000">Verily I say unto thee, Thou shalt by no means come out thence, till thou have paid the last farthing.</font>
39:5:27: <font color="#CC0000">Ye have heard that it was said, Thou shalt not commit adultery:</font>
39:5:28: <font color="#CC0000">but I say unto you, that every one that looketh on a woman to lust after her hath committed adultery with her already in his heart.</font>
39:5:29: <font color="#CC0000">And if thy right eye causeth thee to stumble, pluck it out, and cast it from thee: for it is profitable for thee that one of thy members should perish, and not thy whole body be cast into hell.</font>
39:5:30: <font color="#CC0000">And if thy right hand causeth thee to stumble, cut it off, and cast it from thee: for it is profitable for thee that one of thy members should perish, and not thy whole body go into hell.</font>
39:5:31: <font color="#CC0000">It was said also, Whosoever shall put away his wife, let him give her a writing of divorcement:</font>
39:5:32: <font color="#CC0000">but I say unto you, that every one that putteth away his wife, saving for the cause of fornication, maketh her an adulteress: and whosoever shall marry her when she is put away committeth adultery.</font>
39:5:33: <font color="#CC0000">Again, ye have heard that it was said to them of old time, Thou shalt not forswear thyself, but shalt perform unto the Lord thine oaths:</font>
39:5:34: <font color="#CC0000">but I say unto you, Swear not at all; neither by the heaven, for it is the throne of God;</font>
39:5:35: <font color="#CC0000">nor by the earth, for it is the footstool of his feet; nor by Jerusalem, for it is the city of the great King.</font>
39:5:36: <font color="#CC0000">Neither shalt thou swear by thy head, for thou canst not make one hair white or black.</font>
39:5:37: <font color="#CC0000">But let your speech be, Yea, yea; Nay, nay: and whatsoever is more than these is of the evil one.</font>
39:5:38: <font color="#CC0000">Ye have heard that it was said, An eye for an eye, and a tooth for a tooth:</font>
39:5:39: <font color="#CC0000">but I say unto you, Resist not him that is evil: but whosoever smiteth thee on thy right cheek, turn to him the other also.</font>
39:5:40: <font color="#CC0000">And if any man would go to law with thee, and take away thy coat, let him have thy cloak also.</font>
39:5:41: <font color="#CC0000">And whosoever shall compel thee to go one mile, go with him two.</font>
39:5:42: <font color="#CC0000">Give to him that asketh thee, and from him that would borrow of thee turn not thou away.</font>
39:5:43: <font color="#CC0000">Ye have heard that it was said, Thou shalt love thy neighbor, and hate thine enemy:</font>
39:5:44: <font color="#CC0000">but I say unto you, Love your enemies, and pray for them that persecute you;</font>
39:5:45: <font color="#CC0000">that ye may be sons of your Father who is in heaven: for he maketh his sun to rise on the evil and the good, and sendeth rain on the just and the unjust.</font>
39:5:46: <font color="#CC0000">For if ye love them that love you, what reward have ye? do not even the publicans the same?</font>
39:5:47: <font color="#CC0000">And if ye salute your brethren only, what do ye more than others? do not even the Gentiles the same?</font>
39:5:48: <font color="#CC0000">Ye therefore shall be perfect, as your heavenly Father is perfect.</font>
39:6:1: <font color="#CC0000">Take heed that ye do not your righteousness before men, to be seen of them: else ye have no reward with your Father who is in heaven.</font>
39:6:2: <font color="#CC0000">When therefore thou doest alms, sound not a trumpet before thee, as the hypocrites do in the synagogues and in the streets, that they may have glory of men. Verily I say unto you, They have received their reward.</font>
39:6:3: <font color="#CC0000">But when thou doest alms, let not thy left hand know what thy right hand doeth:</font>
39:6:4: <font color="#CC0000">that thine alms may be in secret: and thy Father who seeth in secret shall recompense thee.</font>
39:6:5: <font color="#CC0000">And when ye pray, ye shall not be as the hypocrites: for they love to stand and pray in the synagogues and in the corners of the streets, that they may be seen of men. Verily I say unto you, They have received their reward.</font>
39:6:6: <font color="#CC0000">But thou, when thou prayest, enter into thine inner chamber, and having shut thy door, pray to thy Father who is in secret, and thy Father who seeth in secret shall recompense thee.</font>
39:6:7: <font color="#CC0000">And in praying use not vain repetitions, as the Gentiles do: for they think that they shall be heard for their much speaking.</font>
39:6:8: <font color="#CC0000">Be not therefore like unto them: for your Father knoweth what things ye have need of, before ye ask him.</font>
39:6:9: <font color="#CC0000">After this manner therefore pray ye: Our Father who art in heaven, Hallowed be thy name.</font>
39:6:10: <font color="#CC0000">Thy kingdom come. Thy will be done, as in heaven, so on earth.</font>
39:6:11: <font color="#CC0000">Give us this day our daily bread.</font>
39:6:12: <font color="#CC0000">And forgive us our debts, as we also have forgiven our debtors.</font>
39:6:13: <font color="#CC0000">And bring us not into temptation, but deliver us from the evil one.</font>
39:6:14: <font color="#CC0000">For if ye forgive men their trespasses, your heavenly Father will also forgive you.</font>
39:6:15: <font color="#CC0000">But if ye forgive not men their trespasses, neither will your Father forgive your trespasses.</font>
39:6:16: <font color="#CC0000">Moreover when ye fast, be not, as the hypocrites, of a sad countenance: for they disfigure their faces, that they may be seen of men to fast. Verily I say unto you, They have received their reward.</font>
39:6:17: <font color="#CC0000">But thou, when thou fastest, anoint thy head, and wash thy face;</font>
39:6:18: <font color="#CC0000">that thou be not seen of men to fast, but of thy Father who is in secret: and thy Father, who seeth in secret, shall recompense thee.</font>
39:6:19: <font color="#CC0000">Lay not up for yourselves treasures upon the earth, where moth and rust consume, and where thieves break through and steal:</font>
39:6:20: <font color="#CC0000">but lay up for yourselves treasures in heaven, where neither moth nor rust doth consume, and where thieves do not break through nor steal:</font>
39:6:21: <font color="#CC0000">for where thy treasure is, there will thy heart be also.</font>
39:6:22: <font color="#CC0000">The lamp of the body is the eye: if therefore thine eye be single, thy whole body shall be full of light.</font>
39:6:23: <font color="#CC0000">But if thine eye be evil, thy whole body shall be full of darkness. If therefore the light that is in thee be darkness, how great is the darkness!</font>
39:6:24: <font color="#CC0000">No man can serve two masters: for either he will hate the one, and love the other; or else he will hold to one, and despise the other. Ye cannot serve God and mammon.</font>
39:6:25: <font color="#CC0000">Therefore I say unto you, Be not anxious for your life, what ye shall eat, or what ye shall drink; nor yet for your body, what ye shall put on. Is not the life more than the food, and the body than the raiment?</font>
39:6:26: <font color="#CC0000">Behold the birds of the heaven, that they sow not, neither do they reap, nor gather into barns; and your heavenly Father feedeth them. Are not ye of much more value than they?</font>
39:6:27: <font color="#CC0000">And which of you by being anxious can add one cubit unto the measure of his life?</font>
39:6:28: <font color="#CC0000">And why are ye anxious concerning raiment? Consider the lilies of the field, how they grow; they toil not, neither do they spin:</font>
39:6:29: <font color="#CC0000">yet I say unto you, that even Solomon in all his glory was not arrayed like one of these.</font>
39:6:30: <font color="#CC0000">But if God doth so clothe the grass of the field, which to-day is, and to-morrow is cast into the oven, shall he not much more clothe you, O ye of little faith?</font>
39:6:31: <font color="#CC0000">Be not therefore anxious, saying, What shall we eat? or, What shall we drink? or, Wherewithal shall we be clothed?</font>
39:6:32: <font color="#CC0000">For after all these things do the Gentiles seek; for your heavenly Father knoweth that ye have need of all these things.</font>
39:6:33: <font color="#CC0000">But seek ye first his kingdom, and his righteousness; and all these things shall be added unto you.</font>
39:6:34: <font color="#CC0000">Be not therefore anxious for the morrow: for the morrow will be anxious for itself. Sufficient unto the day is the evil thereof.</font>
39:7:1: <font color="#CC0000">Judge not, that ye be not judged.</font>
39:7:2: <font color="#CC0000">For with what judgment ye judge, ye shall be judged: and with what measure ye mete, it shall be measured unto you.</font>
39:7:3: <font color="#CC0000">And why beholdest thou the mote that is in thy brother’s eye, but considerest not the beam that is in thine own eye?</font>
39:7:4: <font color="#CC0000">Or how wilt thou say to thy brother, Let me cast out the mote out of thine eye; and lo, the beam is in thine own eye?</font>
39:7:5: <font color="#CC0000">Thou hypocrite, cast out first the beam out of thine own eye; and then shalt thou see clearly to cast out the mote out of thy brother’s eye.</font>
39:7:6: <font color="#CC0000">Give not that which is holy unto the dogs, neither cast your pearls before the swine, lest haply they trample them under their feet, and turn and rend you.</font>
39:7:7: <font color="#CC0000">Ask, and it shall be given you; seek, and ye shall find; knock, and it shall be opened unto you:</font>
39:7:8: <font color="#CC0000">for every one that asketh receiveth; and he that seeketh findeth; and to him that knocketh it shall be opened.</font>
39:7:9: <font color="#CC0000">Or what man is there of you, who, if his son shall ask him for a loaf, will give him a stone;</font>
39:7:10: <font color="#CC0000">or if he shall ask for a fish, will give him a serpent?</font>
39:7:11: <font color="#CC0000">If ye then, being evil, know how to give good gifts unto your children, how much more shall your Father who is in heaven give good things to them that ask him?</font>
39:7:12: <font color="#CC0000">All things therefore whatsoever ye would that men should do unto you, even so do ye also unto them: for this is the law and the prophets.</font>
39:7:13: <font color="#CC0000">Enter ye in by the narrow gate: for wide is the gate, and broad is the way, that leadeth to destruction, and many are they that enter in thereby.</font>
39:7:14: <font color="#CC0000">For narrow is the gate, and straitened the way, that leadeth unto life, and few are they that find it.</font>
39:7:15: <font color="#CC0000">Beware of false prophets, who come to you in sheep’s clothing, but inwardly are ravening wolves.</font>
39:7:16: <font color="#CC0000">By their fruits ye shall know them. Do men gather grapes of thorns, or figs of thistles?</font>
39:7:17: <font color="#CC0000">Even so every good tree bringeth forth good fruit; but the corrupt tree bringeth forth evil fruit.</font>
39:7:18: <font color="#CC0000">A good tree cannot bring forth evil fruit, neither can a corrupt tree bring forth good fruit.</font>
39:7:19: <font color="#CC0000">Every tree that bringeth not forth good fruit is hewn down, and cast into the fire.</font>
39:7:20: <font color="#CC0000">Therefore by their fruits ye shall know them.</font>
39:7:21: <font color="#CC0000">Not every one that saith unto me, Lord, Lord, shall enter into the kingdom of heaven; but he that doeth the will of my Father who is in heaven.</font>
39:7:22: <font color="#CC0000">Many will say to me in that day, Lord, Lord, did we not prophesy by thy name, and by thy name cast out demons, and by thy name do many mighty works?</font>
39:7:23: <font color="#CC0000">And then will I profess unto them, I never knew you: depart from me, ye that work iniquity.</font>
39:7:24: <font color="#CC0000">Every one therefore that heareth these words of mine, and doeth them, shall be likened unto a wise man, who built his house upon the rock:</font>
39:7:25: <font color="#CC0000">and the rain descended, and the floods came, and the winds blew, and beat upon that house; and it fell not: for it was founded upon the rock.</font>
39:7:26: <font color="#CC0000">And every one that heareth these words of mine, and doeth them not, shall be likened unto a foolish man, who built his house upon the sand:</font>
39:7:27: <font color="#CC0000">and the rain descended, and the floods came, and the winds blew, and smote upon that house; and it fell: and great was the fall thereof.</font>
39:8:3: And he stretched forth his hand, and touched him, saying, <font color="#CC0000">I will; be thou made clean</font>. And straightway his leprosy was cleansed.
39:8:4: And Jesus saith unto him, <font color="#CC0000">See thou tell no man; but go, show thyself to the priest, and offer the gift that Moses commanded, for a testimony unto them</font>.
39:8:7: And he saith unto him, <font color="#CC0000">I will come and heal him</font>.
39:8:10: And when Jesus heard it, he marvelled, and said to them that followed, <font color="#CC0000">Verily I say unto you, I have not found so great faith, no, not in Israel</font>.
39:8:11: <font color="#CC0000">And I say unto you, that many shall come from the east and the west, and shall sit down with Abraham, and Isaac, and Jacob, in the kingdom of heaven:</font>
39:8:12: <font color="#CC0000">but the sons of the kingdom shall be cast forth into the outer darkness: there shall be the weeping and the gnashing of teeth.</font>
39:8:13: And Jesus said unto the centurion, <font color="#CC0000">Go thy way; as thou hast believed, so be it done unto thee. And the servant was healed in that hour</font>.
39:8:20: And Jesus saith unto him, <font color="#CC0000">The foxes have holes, and the birds of the heaven have nests; but the Son of man hath not where to lay his head</font>.
39:8:22: But Jesus saith unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Follow me; and leave the dead to bury their own dead</font>.
39:8:26: And he saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Why are ye fearful, O ye of little faith? Then he arose, and rebuked the winds and the sea; and there was a great calm</font>.
39:8:32: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Go</font>. And they came out, and went into the swine: and behold, the whole herd rushed down the steep into the sea, and perished in the waters.
39:9:2: And behold, they brought to him a man sick of the palsy, lying on a bed: and Jesus seeing their faith said unto the sick of the palsy, <font color="#CC0000">Son, be of good cheer; thy sins are forgiven</font>.
39:9:4: And Jesus knowing their thoughts said, <font color="#CC0000">Wherefore think ye evil in your hearts</font>?
39:9:5: <font color="#CC0000">For which is easier, to say, Thy sins are forgiven; or to say, Arise, and walk?</font>
39:9:6: <font color="#CC0000">But that ye may know that the Son of man hath authority on earth to forgive sins</font> (then saith he to the sick of the palsy), <font color="#CC0000">Arise, and take up thy bed, and go unto thy house</font>.
39:9:9: And as Jesus passed by from thence, he saw a man, called Matthew, sitting at the place of toll: and he saith unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Follow me</font>. And he arose, and followed him.
39:9:12: But when he heard it, he said, <font color="#CC0000">They that are whole have no need of a physician, but they that are sick</font>.
39:9:13: <font color="#CC0000">But go ye and learn what this meaneth, I desire mercy, and not sacrifice: for I came not to call the righteous, but sinners.</font>
39:9:15: And Jesus said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Can the sons of the bridechamber mourn, as long as the bridegroom is with them? but the days will come, when the bridegroom shall be taken away from them, and then will they fast</font>.
39:9:16: <font color="#CC0000">And no man putteth a piece of undressed cloth upon an old garment; for that which should fill it up taketh from the garment, and a worse rent is made.</font>
39:9:17: <font color="#CC0000">Neither do men put new wine into old wine-skins: else the skins burst, and the wine is spilled, and the skins perish: but they put new wine into fresh wine-skins, and both are preserved.</font>
39:9:22: But Jesus turning and seeing her said, <font color="#CC0000">Daughter, be of good cheer; thy faith hath made thee whole</font>. And the woman was made whole from that hour.
39:9:24: he said, <font color="#CC0000">Give place: for the damsel is not dead, but sleepeth</font>. And they laughed him to scorn.
39:9:28: And when he was come into the house, the blind men came to him: and Jesus saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Believe ye that I am able to do this</font>? They say unto him, Yea, Lord.
39:9:29: Then touched he their eyes, saying, <font color="#CC0000">According to your faith be it done unto you</font>.
39:9:30: And their eyes were opened. And Jesus strictly charged them, saying, <font color="#CC0000">See that no man know it</font>.
39:9:37: Then saith he unto his disciples, <font color="#CC0000">The harvest indeed is plenteous, but the laborers are few</font>.
39:9:38: <font color="#CC0000">Pray ye therefore the Lord of the harvest, that he send forth laborers into his harvest.</font>
39:10:5: These twelve Jesus sent forth, and charged them, saying, <font color="#CC0000">Go not into any way of the Gentiles, and enter not into any city of the Samaritans</font>:
39:10:6: <font color="#CC0000">but go rather to the lost sheep of the house of Israel.</font>
39:10:7: <font color="#CC0000">And as ye go, preach, saying, The kingdom of heaven is at hand.</font>
39:10:8: <font color="#CC0000">Heal the sick, raise the dead, cleanse the lepers, cast out demons: freely ye received, freely give.</font>
39:10:9: <font color="#CC0000">Get you no gold, nor silver, nor brass in your purses;</font>
39:10:10: <font color="#CC0000">no wallet for your journey, neither two coats, nor shoes, nor staff: for the laborer is worthy of his food.</font>
39:10:11: <font color="#CC0000">And into whatsoever city or village ye shall enter, search out who in it is worthy; and there abide till ye go forth.</font>
39:10:12: <font color="#CC0000">And as ye enter into the house, salute it.</font>
39:10:13: <font color="#CC0000">And if the house be worthy, let your peace come upon it: but if it be not worthy, let your peace return to you.</font>
39:10:14: <font color="#CC0000">And whosoever shall not receive you, nor hear your words, as ye go forth out of that house or that city, shake off the dust of your feet.</font>
39:10:15: <font color="#CC0000">Verily I say unto you, It shall be more tolerable for the land of Sodom and Gomorrah in the day of judgment, than for that city.</font>
39:10:16: <font color="#CC0000">Behold, I send you forth as sheep in the midst of wolves: be ye therefore wise as serpents, and harmless as doves.</font>
39:10:17: <font color="#CC0000">But beware of men: for they will deliver you up to councils, and in their synagogues they will scourge you;</font>
39:10:18: <font color="#CC0000">yea and before governors and kings shall ye be brought for my sake, for a testimony to them and to the Gentiles.</font>
39:10:19: <font color="#CC0000">But when they deliver you up, be not anxious how or what ye shall speak: for it shall be given you in that hour what ye shall speak.</font>
39:10:20: <font color="#CC0000">For it is not ye that speak, but the Spirit of your Father that speaketh in you.</font>
39:10:21: <font color="#CC0000">And brother shall deliver up brother to death, and the father his child: and children shall rise up against parents, and cause them to be put to death.</font>
39:10:22: <font color="#CC0000">And ye shall be hated of all men for my name’s sake: but he that endureth to the end, the same shall be saved.</font>
39:10:23: <font color="#CC0000">But when they persecute you in this city, flee into the next: for verily I say unto you, Ye shall not have gone through the cities of Israel, till the Son of man be come.</font>
39:10:24: <font color="#CC0000">A disciple is not above his teacher, nor a servant above his lord.</font>
39:10:25: <font color="#CC0000">It is enough for the disciple that he be as his teacher, and the servant as his lord. If they have called the master of the house Beelzebub, how much more them of his household!</font>
39:10:26: <font color="#CC0000">Fear them not therefore: for there is nothing covered, that shall not be revealed; and hid, that shall not be known.</font>
39:10:27: <font color="#CC0000">What I tell you in the darkness, speak ye in the light; and what ye hear in the ear, proclaim upon the house-tops.</font>
39:10:28: <font color="#CC0000">And be not afraid of them that kill the body, but are not able to kill the soul: but rather fear him who is able to destroy both soul and body in hell.</font>
39:10:29: <font color="#CC0000">Are not two sparrows sold for a penny? and not one of them shall fall on the ground without your Father:</font>
39:10:30: <font color="#CC0000">but the very hairs of your head are all numbered.</font>
39:10:31: <font color="#CC0000">Fear not therefore: ye are of more value than many sparrows.</font>
39:10:32: <font color="#CC0000">Every one therefore who shall confess me before men, him will I also confess before my Father who is in heaven.</font>
39:10:33: <font color="#CC0000">But whosoever shall deny me before men, him will I also deny before my Father who is in heaven.</font>
39:10:34: <font color="#CC0000">Think not that I came to send peace on the earth: I came not to send peace, but a sword.</font>
39:10:35: <font color="#CC0000">For I came to set a man at variance against his father, and the daughter against her mother, and the daughter in law against her mother in law:</font>
39:10:36: <font color="#CC0000">and a man’s foes shall be they of his own household.</font>
39:10:37: <font color="#CC0000">He that loveth father or mother more than me is not worthy of me; and he that loveth son or daughter more than me is not worthy of me.</font>
39:10:38: <font color="#CC0000">And he that doth not take his cross and follow after me, is not worthy of me.</font>
39:10:39: <font color="#CC0000">He that findeth his life shall lose it; and he that loseth his life for my sake shall find it.</font>
39:10:40: <font color="#CC0000">He that receiveth you receiveth me, and he that receiveth me receiveth him that sent me.</font>
39:10:41: <font color="#CC0000">He that receiveth a prophet in the name of a prophet shall receive a prophet’s reward: and he that receiveth a righteous man in the name of a righteous man shall receive a righteous man’s reward.</font>
39:10:42: <font color="#CC0000">And whosoever shall give to drink unto one of these little ones a cup of cold water only, in the name of a disciple, verily I say unto you he shall in no wise lose his reward.</font>
39:11:4: And Jesus answered and said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Go and tell John the things which ye hear and see</font>:
39:11:5: <font color="#CC0000">the blind receive their sight, and the lame walk, the lepers are cleansed, and the deaf hear, and the dead are raised up, and the poor have good tidings preached to them.</font>
39:11:6: <font color="#CC0000">And blessed is he, whosoever shall find no occasion of stumbling in me.</font>
39:11:7: And as these went their way, Jesus began to say unto the multitudes concerning John, <font color="#CC0000">What went ye out into the wilderness to behold? a reed shaken with the wind</font>?
39:11:8: <font color="#CC0000">But what went ye out to see? a man clothed in soft raiment? Behold, they that wear soft raiment are in kings’ houses.</font>
39:11:9: <font color="#CC0000">But wherefore went ye out? to see a prophet? Yea, I say unto you, and much more than a prophet.</font>
39:11:10: <font color="#CC0000">This is he, of whom it is written,  Behold, I send my messenger before thy face, Who shall prepare thy way before thee.</font>
39:11:11: <font color="#CC0000">Verily I say unto you, Among them that are born of women there hath not arisen a greater than John the Baptist: yet he that is but little in the kingdom of heaven is greater than he.</font>
39:11:12: <font color="#CC0000">And from the days of John the Baptist until now the kingdom of heaven suffereth violence, and men of violence take it by force.</font>
39:11:13: <font color="#CC0000">For all the prophets and the law prophesied until John.</font>
39:11:14: <font color="#CC0000">And if ye are willing to receive it, this is Elijah, that is to come.</font>
39:11:15: <font color="#CC0000">He that hath ears to hear, let him hear.</font>
39:11:16: <font color="#CC0000">But whereunto shall I liken this generation? It is like unto children sitting in the marketplaces, who call unto their fellows</font>
39:11:17: <font color="#CC0000">and say, We piped unto you, and ye did not dance; we wailed, and ye did not mourn.</font>
39:11:18: <font color="#CC0000">For John came neither eating nor drinking, and they say, He hath a demon.</font>
39:11:19: <font color="#CC0000">The Son of man came eating and drinking, and they say, Behold, a gluttonous man and a winebibber, a friend of publicans and sinners! And wisdom is justified by her works.</font>
39:11:21: <font color="#CC0000">Woe unto thee, Chorazin! woe unto thee, Bethsaida! for if the mighty works had been done in Tyre and Sidon which were done in you, they would have repented long ago in sackcloth and ashes.</font>
39:11:22: <font color="#CC0000">But I say unto you, it shall be more tolerable for Tyre and Sidon in the day of judgment, than for you.</font>
39:11:23: <font color="#CC0000">And thou, Capernaum, shalt thou be exalted unto heaven? thou shalt go down unto Hades: for if the mighty works had been done in Sodom which were done in thee, it would have remained until this day.</font>
39:11:24: <font color="#CC0000">But I say unto you that it shall be more tolerable for the land of Sodom in the day of judgment, than for thee.</font>
39:11:25: At that season Jesus answered and said, <font color="#CC0000">I thank thee, O Father, Lord of heaven and earth, that thou didst hide these things from the wise and understanding, and didst reveal them unto babes</font>:
39:11:26: <font color="#CC0000">yea, Father, for so it was well-pleasing in thy sight.</font>
39:11:27: <font color="#CC0000">All things have been delivered unto me of my Father: and no one knoweth the Son, save the Father; neither doth any know the Father, save the Son, and he to whomsoever the Son willeth to reveal him.</font>
39:11:28: <font color="#CC0000">Come unto me, all ye that labor and are heavy laden, and I will give you rest.</font>
39:11:29: <font color="#CC0000">Take my yoke upon you, and learn of me; for I am meek and lowly in heart: and ye shall find rest unto your souls.</font>
39:11:30: <font color="#CC0000">For my yoke is easy, and my burden is light.</font>
39:12:3: But he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Have ye not read what David did, when he was hungry, and they that were with him</font>;
39:12:4: <font color="#CC0000">how he entered into the house of God, and ate the showbread, which it was not lawful for him to eat, neither for them that were with him, but only for the priests?</font>
39:12:5: <font color="#CC0000">Or have ye not read in the law, that on the sabbath day the priests in the temple profane the sabbath, and are guiltless?</font>
39:12:6: <font color="#CC0000">But I say unto you, that one greater than the temple is here.</font>
39:12:7: <font color="#CC0000">But if ye had known what this meaneth, I desire mercy, and not sacrifice, ye would not have condemned the guiltless.</font>
39:12:8: <font color="#CC0000">For the Son of man is lord of the sabbath.</font>
39:12:11: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">What man shall there be of you, that shall have one sheep, and if this fall into a pit on the sabbath day, will he not lay hold on it, and lift it out</font>?
39:12:12: <font color="#CC0000">How much then is a man of more value than a sheep! Wherefore it is lawful to do good on the sabbath day.</font>
39:12:13: Then saith he to the man, <font color="#CC0000">Stretch forth thy hand</font>. And he stretched it forth; and it was restored whole, as the other.
39:12:25: And knowing their thoughts he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Every kingdom divided against itself is brought to desolation; and every city or house divided against itself shall not stand</font>:
39:12:26: <font color="#CC0000">and if Satan casteth out Satan, he is divided against himself; how then shall his kingdom stand?</font>
39:12:27: <font color="#CC0000">And if I by Beelzebub cast out demons, by whom do your sons cast them out? therefore shall they be your judges.</font>
39:12:28: <font color="#CC0000">But if I by the Spirit of God cast out demons, then is the kingdom of God come upon you.</font>
39:12:29: <font color="#CC0000">Or how can one enter into the house of the strong man, and spoil his goods, except he first bind the strong man? and then he will spoil his house.</font>
39:12:30: <font color="#CC0000">He that is not with me is against me; and he that gathereth not with me scattereth.</font>
39:12:31: <font color="#CC0000">Therefore I say unto you, Every sin and blasphemy shall be forgiven unto men; but the blasphemy against the Spirit shall not be forgiven.</font>
39:12:32: <font color="#CC0000">And whosoever shall speak a word against the Son of man, it shall be forgiven him; but whosoever shall speak against the Holy Spirit, it shall not be forgiven him, neither in this world, nor in that which is to come.</font>
39:12:33: <font color="#CC0000">Either make the tree good, and its fruit good; or make the tree corrupt, and its fruit corrupt: for the tree is known by its fruit.</font>
39:12:34: <font color="#CC0000">Ye offspring of vipers, how can ye, being evil, speak good things? for out of the abundance of the heart the mouth speaketh.</font>
39:12:35: <font color="#CC0000">The good man out of his good treasure bringeth forth good things: and the evil man out of his evil treasure bringeth forth evil things.</font>
39:12:36: <font color="#CC0000">And I say unto you, that every idle word that men shall speak, they shall give account thereof in the day of judgment.</font>
39:12:37: <font color="#CC0000">For by thy words thou shalt be justified, and by thy words thou shalt be condemned.</font>
39:12:39: But he answered and said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">An evil and adulterous generation seeketh after a sign; and there shall no sign be given to it but the sign of Jonah the prophet</font>:
39:12:40: <font color="#CC0000">for as Jonah was three days and three nights in the belly of the whale; so shall the Son of man be three days and three nights in the heart of the earth.</font>
39:12:41: <font color="#CC0000">The men of Nineveh shall stand up in the judgment with this generation, and shall condemn it: for they repented at the preaching of Jonah; and behold, a greater than Jonah is here.</font>
39:12:42: <font color="#CC0000">The queen of the south shall rise up in the judgment with this generation, and shall condemn it: for she came from the ends of the earth to hear the wisdom of Solomon; and behold, a greater than Solomon is here.</font>
39:12:43: <font color="#CC0000">But the unclean spirit, when he is gone out of the man, passeth through waterless places, seeking rest, and findeth it not.</font>
39:12:44: <font color="#CC0000">Then he saith, I will return into my house whence I came out; and when he is come, he findeth it empty, swept, and garnished.</font>
39:12:45: <font color="#CC0000">Then goeth he, and taketh with himself seven other spirits more evil than himself, and they enter in and dwell there: and the last state of that man becometh worse than the first. Even so shall it be also unto this evil generation.</font>
39:12:48: But he answered and said unto him that told him, <font color="#CC0000">Who is my mother? and who are my brethren</font>?
39:12:49: And he stretched forth his hand towards his disciples, and said, <font color="#CC0000">Behold, my mother and my brethren</font>!
39:12:50: <font color="#CC0000">For whosoever shall do the will of my Father who is in heaven, he is my brother, and sister, and mother.</font>
39:13:3: And he spake to them many things in parables, saying, <font color="#CC0000">Behold, the sower went forth to sow</font>;
39:13:4: <font color="#CC0000">and as he sowed, some seeds fell by the way side, and the birds came and devoured them:</font>
39:13:5: <font color="#CC0000">and others fell upon the rocky places, where they had not much earth: and straightway they sprang up, because they had no deepness of earth:</font>
39:13:6: <font color="#CC0000">and when the sun was risen, they were scorched; and because they had no root, they withered away.</font>
39:13:7: <font color="#CC0000">And others fell upon the thorns; and the thorns grew up and choked them:</font>
39:13:8: <font color="#CC0000">and others fell upon the good ground, and yielded fruit, some a hundredfold, some sixty, some thirty.</font>
39:13:9: <font color="#CC0000">He that hath ears, let him hear.</font>
39:13:11: And he answered and said unto them, Unto you <font color="#CC0000">it is given to know the mysteries of the kingdom of heaven, but to them it is not given</font>.
39:13:12: <font color="#CC0000">For whosoever hath, to him shall be given, and he shall have abundance: but whosoever hath not, from him shall be taken away even that which he hath.</font>
39:13:13: <font color="#CC0000">Therefore speak I to them in parables; because seeing they see not, and hearing they hear not, neither do they understand.</font>
39:13:14: <font color="#CC0000">And unto them is fulfilled the prophecy of Isaiah, which saith,  By hearing ye shall hear, and shall in no wise understand; And seeing ye shall see, and shall in no wise perceive:</font>
39:13:15: <font color="#CC0000">For this people’s heart is waxed gross, And their ears are dull of hearing, And their eyes they have closed; Lest haply they should perceive with their eyes, And hear with their ears, And understand with their heart, And should turn again, And I should heal them.</font>
39:13:16: <font color="#CC0000">But blessed are your eyes, for they see; and your ears, for they hear.</font>
39:13:17: <font color="#CC0000">For verily I say unto you, that many prophets and righteous men desired to see the things which ye see, and saw them not; and to hear the things which ye hear, and heard them not.</font>
39:13:18: <font color="#CC0000">Hear then ye the parable of the sower.</font>
39:13:19: <font color="#CC0000">When any one heareth the word of the kingdom, and understandeth it not, then cometh the evil one, and snatcheth away that which hath been sown in his heart. This is he that was sown by the way side.</font>
39:13:20: <font color="#CC0000">And he that was sown upon the rocky places, this is he that heareth the word, and straightway with joy receiveth it;</font>
39:13:21: <font color="#CC0000">yet hath he not root in himself, but endureth for a while; and when tribulation or persecution ariseth because of the word, straightway he stumbleth.</font>
39:13:22: <font color="#CC0000">And he that was sown among the thorns, this is he that heareth the word; and the care of the world, and the deceitfulness of riches, choke the word, and he becometh unfruitful.</font>
39:13:23: <font color="#CC0000">And he that was sown upon the good ground, this is he that heareth the word, and understandeth it; who verily beareth fruit, and bringeth forth, some a hundredfold, some sixty, some thirty.</font>
39:13:24: Another parable set he before them, saying, <font color="#CC0000">The kingdom of heaven is likened unto a man that sowed good seed in his field</font>:
39:13:25: <font color="#CC0000">but while men slept, his enemy came and sowed tares also among the wheat, and went away.</font>
39:13:26: <font color="#CC0000">But when the blade sprang up and brought forth fruit, then appeared the tares also.</font>
39:13:27: <font color="#CC0000">And the servants of the householder came and said unto him, Sir, didst thou not sow good seed in thy field? whence then hath it tares?</font>
39:13:28: <font color="#CC0000">And he said unto them, An enemy hath done this. And the servants say unto him, Wilt thou then that we go and gather them up?</font>
39:13:29: <font color="#CC0000">But he saith, Nay; lest haply while ye gather up the tares, ye root up the wheat with them.</font>
39:13:30: <font color="#CC0000">Let both grow together until the harvest: and in the time of the harvest I will say to the reapers, Gather up first the tares, and bind them in bundles to burn them; but gather the wheat into my barn.</font>
39:13:31: Another parable set he before them, saying, <font color="#CC0000">The kingdom of heaven is like unto a grain of mustard seed, which a man took, and sowed in his field</font>:
39:13:32: <font color="#CC0000">which indeed is less than all seeds; but when it is grown, it is greater than the herbs, and becometh a tree, so that the birds of the heaven come and lodge in the branches thereof.</font>
39:13:33: Another parable spake he unto them; <font color="#CC0000">The kingdom of heaven is like unto leaven, which a woman took, and hid in three measures of meal, till it was all leavened</font>.
39:13:37: And he answered and said, <font color="#CC0000">He that soweth the good seed is the Son of man</font>;
39:13:38: <font color="#CC0000">and the field is the world; and the good seed, these are the sons of the kingdom; and the tares are the sons of the evil one;</font>
39:13:39: <font color="#CC0000">and the enemy that sowed them is the devil: and the harvest is the end of the world; and the reapers are angels.</font>
39:13:40: <font color="#CC0000">As therefore the tares are gathered up and burned with fire; so shall it be in the end of the world.</font>
39:13:41: <font color="#CC0000">The Son of man shall send forth his angels, and they shall gather out of his kingdom all things that cause stumbling, and them that do iniquity,</font>
39:13:42: <font color="#CC0000">and shall cast them into the furnace of fire: there shall be the weeping and the gnashing of teeth.</font>
39:13:43: <font color="#CC0000">Then shall the righteous shine forth as the sun in the kingdom of their Father. He that hath ears, let him hear.</font>
39:13:44: <font color="#CC0000">The kingdom of heaven is like unto a treasure hidden in the field; which a man found, and hid; and in his joy he goeth and selleth all that he hath, and buyeth that field.</font>
39:13:45: <font color="#CC0000">Again, the kingdom of heaven is like unto a man that is a merchant seeking goodly pearls:</font>
39:13:46: <font color="#CC0000">and having found one pearl of great price, he went and sold all that he had, and bought it.</font>
39:13:47: <font color="#CC0000">Again, the kingdom of heaven is like unto a net, that was cast into the sea, and gathered of every kind:</font>
39:13:48: <font color="#CC0000">which, when it was filled, they drew up on the beach; and they sat down, and gathered the good into vessels, but the bad they cast away.</font>
39:13:49: <font color="#CC0000">So shall it be in the end of the world: the angels shall come forth, and sever the wicked from among the righteous,</font>
39:13:50: <font color="#CC0000">and shall cast them into the furnace of fire: there shall be the weeping and the gnashing of teeth.</font>
39:13:51: <font color="#CC0000">Have ye understood all these things</font>? They say unto him, Yea.
39:13:52: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Therefore every scribe who hath been made a disciple to the kingdom of heaven is like unto a man that is a householder, who bringeth forth out of his treasure things new and old</font>.
39:13:57: And they were offended in him. But Jesus said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">A prophet is not without honor, save in his own country, and in his own house</font>.
39:14:16: But Jesus said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">They have no need to go away; give ye them to eat</font>.
39:14:18: And he said, <font color="#CC0000">Bring them hither to me</font>.
39:14:27: But straightway Jesus spake unto them, saying, <font color="#CC0000">Be of good cheer; it is I; be not afraid</font>.
39:14:29: And he said, <font color="#CC0000">Come</font>. And Peter went down from the boat, and walked upon the waters to come to Jesus.
39:14:31: And immediately Jesus stretched forth his hand, and took hold of him, and saith unto him, <font color="#CC0000">O thou of little faith, wherefore didst thou doubt</font>?
39:15:3: And he answered and said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Why do ye also transgress the commandment of God because of your tradition</font>?
39:15:4: <font color="#CC0000">For God said, Honor thy father and thy mother: and, He that speaketh evil of father or mother, let him die the death.</font>
39:15:5: <font color="#CC0000">But ye say, Whosoever shall say to his father or his mother, That wherewith thou mightest have been profited by me is given to God;</font>
39:15:6: <font color="#CC0000">he shall not honor his father. And ye have made void the word of God because of your tradition.</font>
39:15:7: <font color="#CC0000">Ye hypocrites, well did Isaiah prophesy of you, saying,</font>
39:15:8: <font color="#CC0000">This people honoreth me with their lips; But their heart is far from me.</font>
39:15:9: <font color="#CC0000">But in vain do they worship me, Teachingas theirdoctrines the precepts of men.</font>
39:15:10: And he called to him the multitude, and said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Hear, and understand</font>:
39:15:11: <font color="#CC0000">Not that which entereth into the mouth defileth the man; but that which proceedeth out of the mouth, this defileth the man.</font>
39:15:13: But he answered and said, <font color="#CC0000">Every plant which my heavenly Father planted not, shall be rooted up</font>.
39:15:14: <font color="#CC0000">Let them alone: they are blind guides. And if the blind guide the blind, both shall fall into a pit.</font>
39:15:16: And he said, <font color="#CC0000">Are ye also even yet without understanding</font>?
39:15:17: <font color="#CC0000">Perceive ye not, that whatsoever goeth into the mouth passeth into the belly, and is cast out into the draught?</font>
39:15:18: <font color="#CC0000">But the things which proceed out of the mouth come forth out of the heart; and they defile the man.</font>
39:15:19: <font color="#CC0000">For out of the heart come forth evil thoughts, murders, adulteries, fornications, thefts, false witness, railings:</font>
39:15:20: <font color="#CC0000">these are the things which defile the man; but to eat with unwashen hands defileth not the man.</font>
39:15:24: But he answered and said, <font color="#CC0000">I was not sent but unto the lost sheep of the house of Israel</font>.
39:15:26: And he answered and said, <font color="#CC0000">It is not meet to take the children’s bread and cast it to the dogs</font>.
39:15:28: Then Jesus answered and said unto her, <font color="#CC0000">O woman, great is thy faith: be it done unto thee even as thou wilt. And her daughter was healed from that hour</font>.
39:15:32: And Jesus called unto him his disciples, and said, <font color="#CC0000">I have compassion on the multitude, because they continue with me now three days and have nothing to eat: and I would not send them away fasting, lest haply they faint on the way</font>.
39:15:34: And Jesus said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">How many loaves have ye? And they said, Seven, and a few small fishes</font>.
39:16:2: But he answered and said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">When it is evening, ye say, It will be fair weather: for the heaven is red</font>.
39:16:3: <font color="#CC0000">And in the morning, It will be foul weather to-day: for the heaven is red and lowering. Ye know how to discern the face of the heaven; but ye cannot discern the signs of the times.</font>
39:16:4: An evil <font color="#CC0000">and adulterous generation seeketh after a sign; and there shall no sign be given unto it, but the sign of</font> Jonah. And he left them, and departed.
39:16:6: And Jesus said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Take heed and beware of the leaven of the Pharisees and Sadducees</font>.
39:16:8: And Jesus perceiving it said, <font color="#CC0000">O ye of little faith, why reason ye among yourselves, because ye have no bread</font>?
39:16:9: <font color="#CC0000">Do ye not yet perceive, neither remember the five loaves of the five thousand, and how many baskets ye took up?</font>
39:16:10: <font color="#CC0000">Neither the seven loaves of the four thousand, and how many baskets ye took up?</font>
39:16:11: <font color="#CC0000">How is it that ye do not perceive that I spake not to you concerning bread? But beware of the leaven of the Pharisees and Sadducees.</font>
39:16:13: Now when Jesus came into the parts of Cæsarea Philippi, he asked his disciples, saying, Who <font color="#CC0000">do men say that the Son of man</font> is?
39:16:15: He saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">But who say ye that I am</font>?
39:16:17: And Jesus answered and said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Blessed art thou, Simon Bar-Jonah: for flesh and blood hath not revealed it unto thee, but my Father who is in heaven</font>.
39:16:18: <font color="#CC0000">And I also say unto thee, that thou art Peter, and upon this rock I will build my church; and the gates of Hades shall not prevail against it.</font>
39:16:19: <font color="#CC0000">I will give unto thee the keys of the kingdom of heaven: and whatsoever thou shalt bind on earth shall be bound in heaven; and whatsoever thou shalt loose on earth shall be loosed in heaven.</font>
39:16:23: But he turned, and said unto Peter, <font color="#CC0000">Get thee behind me, Satan: thou art a stumbling-block unto me: for thou mindest not the things of God, but the things of men</font>.
39:16:24: Then said Jesus unto his disciples, <font color="#CC0000">If any man would come after me, let him deny himself, and take up his cross, and follow me</font>.
39:16:25: <font color="#CC0000">For whosoever would save his life shall lose it: and whosoever shall lose his life for my sake shall find it.</font>
39:16:26: <font color="#CC0000">For what shall a man be profited, if he shall gain the whole world, and forfeit his life? or what shall a man give in exchange for his life?</font>
39:16:27: <font color="#CC0000">For the Son of man shall come in the glory of his Father with his angels; and then shall he render unto every man according to his deeds.</font>
39:16:28: <font color="#CC0000">Verily I say unto you, There are some of them that stand here, who shall in no wise taste of death, till they see the Son of man coming in his kingdom.</font>
39:17:7: And Jesus came and touched them and said, <font color="#CC0000">Arise, and be not afraid</font>.
39:17:9: And as they were coming down from the mountain, Jesus commanded them, saying, <font color="#CC0000">Tell the vision to no man, until the Son of man be risen from the dead</font>.
39:17:11: And he answered and said, Elijah indeed cometh, and <font color="#CC0000">shall restore all things</font>:
39:17:12: <font color="#CC0000">but I say unto you, that Elijah is come already, and they knew him not, but did unto him whatsoever they would. Even so shall the Son of man also suffer of them.</font>
39:17:17: And Jesus answered and said, <font color="#CC0000">O faithless and perverse generation, how long shall I be with you? how long shall I bear with you? bring him hither to me</font>.
39:17:20: And he saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Because of your little faith: for verily I say unto you, If ye have faith as a grain of mustard seed, ye shall say unto this mountain, Remove hence to yonder place; and it shall remove; and nothing shall be impossible unto you</font>.
39:17:22: And while they abode in Galilee, Jesus said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">The Son of man shall be delivered up into the hands of men</font>;
39:17:23: <font color="#CC0000">and they shall kill him, and the third day he shall be raised</font> up. And they were exceeding sorry.
39:17:25: He saith, Yea. And when he came into the house, Jesus spake first to him, saying, <font color="#CC0000">What thinkest thou, Simon? the kings of the earth, from whom do they receive toll or tribute? from their sons, or from strangers</font>?
39:17:26: And when he said, From strangers, Jesus said unto him, Therefore the sons <font color="#CC0000">are free</font>.
39:17:27: <font color="#CC0000">But, lest we cause them to stumble, go thou to the sea, and cast a hook, and take up the fish that first cometh up; and when thou hast opened his mouth, thou shalt find a shekel: that take, and give unto them for me and thee.</font>
39:18:3: and said, <font color="#CC0000">Verily I say unto you, Except ye turn, and become as little children, ye shall in no wise enter into the kingdom of heaven</font>.
39:18:4: <font color="#CC0000">Whosoever therefore shall humble himself as this little child, the same is the greatest in the kingdom of heaven.</font>
39:18:5: <font color="#CC0000">And whoso shall receive one such little child in my name receiveth me:</font>
39:18:6: <font color="#CC0000">but whoso shall cause one of these little ones that believe on me to stumble, it is profitable for him that a great millstone should be hanged about his neck, and that he should be sunk in the depth of the sea.</font>
39:18:7: <font color="#CC0000">Woe unto the world because of occasions of stumbling! for it must needs be that the occasions come; but woe to that man through whom the occasion cometh!</font>
39:18:8: <font color="#CC0000">And if thy hand or thy foot causeth thee to stumble, cut it off, and cast it from thee: it is good for thee to enter into life maimed or halt, rather than having two hands or two feet to be cast into the eternal fire.</font>
39:18:9: <font color="#CC0000">And if thine eye causeth thee to stumble, pluck it out, and cast it from thee: it is good for thee to enter into life with one eye, rather than having two eyes to be cast into the hell of fire.</font>
39:18:10: <font color="#CC0000">See that ye despise not one of these little ones: for I say unto you, that in heaven their angels do always behold the face of my Father who is in heaven.</font>
39:18:12: <font color="#CC0000">How think ye? if any man have a hundred sheep, and one of them be gone astray, doth he not leave the ninety and nine, and go unto the mountains, and seek that which goeth astray?</font>
39:18:13: <font color="#CC0000">And if so be that he find it, verily I say unto you, he rejoiceth over it more than over the ninety and nine which have not gone astray.</font>
39:18:14: <font color="#CC0000">Even so it is not the will of your Father who is in heaven, that one of these little ones should perish.</font>
39:18:15: <font color="#CC0000">And if thy brother sin against thee, go, show him his fault between thee and him alone: if he hear thee, thou hast gained thy brother.</font>
39:18:16: <font color="#CC0000">But if he hear thee not, take with thee one or two more, that at the mouth of two witnesses or three every word may be established.</font>
39:18:17: <font color="#CC0000">And if he refuse to hear them, tell it unto the church: and if he refuse to hear the church also, let him be unto thee as the Gentile and the publican.</font>
39:18:18: <font color="#CC0000">Verily I say unto you, What things soever ye shall bind on earth shall be bound in heaven; and what things soever ye shall loose on earth shall be loosed in heaven.</font>
39:18:19: <font color="#CC0000">Again I say unto you, that if two of you shall agree on earth as touching anything that they shall ask, it shall be done for them of my Father who is in heaven.</font>
39:18:20: <font color="#CC0000">For where two or three are gathered together in my name, there am I in the midst of them.</font>
39:18:22: Jesus saith unto him, <font color="#CC0000">I say not unto thee, Until seven times; but, Until seventy times seven</font>.
39:18:23: <font color="#CC0000">Therefore is the kingdom of heaven likened unto a certain king, who would make a reckoning with his servants.</font>
39:18:24: <font color="#CC0000">And when he had begun to reckon, one was brought unto him, that owed him ten thousand talents.</font>
39:18:25: <font color="#CC0000">But forasmuch as he had not wherewith to pay, his lord commanded him to be sold, and his wife, and children, and all that he had, and payment to be made.</font>
39:18:26: <font color="#CC0000">The servant therefore fell down and worshipped him, saying, Lord, have patience with me, and I will pay thee all.</font>
39:18:27: <font color="#CC0000">And the lord of that servant, being moved with compassion, released him, and forgave him the debt.</font>
39:18:28: <font color="#CC0000">But that servant went out, and found one of his fellow-servants, who owed him a hundred shillings: and he laid hold on him, and took him by the throat, saying, Pay what thou owest.</font>
39:18:29: <font color="#CC0000">So his fellow-servant fell down and besought him, saying, Have patience with me, and I will pay thee.</font>
39:18:30: <font color="#CC0000">And he would not: but went and cast him into prison, till he should pay that which was due.</font>
39:18:31: <font color="#CC0000">So when his fellow-servants saw what was done, they were exceeding sorry, and came and told unto their lord all that was done.</font>
39:18:32: <font color="#CC0000">Then his lord called him unto him, and saith to him, Thou wicked servant, I forgave thee all that debt, because thou besoughtest me:</font>
39:18:33: <font color="#CC0000">shouldest not thou also have had mercy on thy fellow-servant, even as I had mercy on thee?</font>
39:18:34: <font color="#CC0000">And his lord was wroth, and delivered him to the tormentors, till he should pay all that was due.</font>
39:18:35: <font color="#CC0000">So shall also my heavenly Father do unto you, if ye forgive not every one his brother from your hearts.</font>
39:19:4: And he answered and said, <font color="#CC0000">Have ye not read, that he who made them from the beginning made them male and female</font>,
39:19:5: <font color="#CC0000">and said, For this cause shall a man leave his father and mother, and shall cleave to his wife; and the two shall become one flesh?</font>
39:19:6: <font color="#CC0000">So that they are no more two, but one flesh. What therefore God hath joined together, let not man put asunder.</font>
39:19:8: He saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Moses for your hardness of heart suffered you to put away your wives: but from the beginning it hath not been so</font>.
39:19:9: <font color="#CC0000">And I say unto you, Whosoever shall put away his wife, except for fornication, and shall marry another, committeth adultery: and he that marrieth her when she is put away committeth adultery.</font>
39:19:11: But he said unto them, Not <font color="#CC0000">all men can receive this saying, but they to whom it is given</font>.
39:19:12: <font color="#CC0000">For there are eunuchs, that were so born from their mother’s womb: and there are eunuchs, that were made eunuchs by men: and there are eunuchs, that made themselves eunuchs for the kingdom of heaven’s sake. He that is able to receive it, let him receive it.</font>
39:19:14: But Jesus said, <font color="#CC0000">Suffer the little children, and forbid them not, to come unto me: for to such belongeth the kingdom of heaven</font>.
39:19:17: And he said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Why askest thou me concerning that which is good? One there is who is good: but if thou wouldest enter into life, keep the commandments</font>.
39:19:19: <font color="#CC0000">Honor thy father and thy mother; and, Thou shalt love thy neighbor as thyself.</font>
39:19:21: Jesus said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">If thou wouldest be perfect, go, sell that which thou hast, and give to the poor, and thou shalt have treasure in heaven: and come, follow me</font>.
39:19:23: And Jesus said unto his disciples, <font color="#CC0000">Verily I say unto you, It is hard for a rich man to enter into the kingdom of heaven</font>.
39:19:24: <font color="#CC0000">And again I say unto you, It is easier for a camel to go through a needle’s eye, than for a rich man to enter into the kingdom of God.</font>
39:19:26: And Jesus looking upon them said to them, <font color="#CC0000">With men this is impossible; but with God all things are possible</font>.
39:19:28: And Jesus said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Verily I say unto you, that ye who have followed me, in the regeneration when the Son of man shall sit on the throne of his glory, ye also shall sit upon twelve thrones, judging the twelve tribes of Israel</font>.
39:19:29: <font color="#CC0000">And every one that hath left houses, or brethren, or sisters, or father, or mother, or children, or lands, for my name’s sake, shall receive a hundredfold, and shall inherit eternal life.</font>
39:19:30: <font color="#CC0000">But many shall be last that are first; and first that are last.</font>
39:20:1: <font color="#CC0000">For the kingdom of heaven is like unto a man that was a householder, who went out early in the morning to hire laborers into his vineyard.</font>
39:20:2: <font color="#CC0000">And when he had agreed with the laborers for a shilling a day, he sent them into his vineyard.</font>
39:20:3: <font color="#CC0000">And he went out about the third hour, and saw others standing in the marketplace idle;</font>
39:20:4: <font color="#CC0000">and to them he said, Go ye also into the vineyard, and whatsoever is right I will give you. And they went their way.</font>
39:20:5: <font color="#CC0000">Again he went out about the sixth and the ninth hour, and did likewise.</font>
39:20:6: <font color="#CC0000">And about the eleventh hour he went out, and found others standing; and he saith unto them, Why stand ye here all the day idle?</font>
39:20:7: <font color="#CC0000">They say unto him, Because no man hath hired us. He saith unto them, Go ye also into the vineyard.</font>
39:20:8: <font color="#CC0000">And when even was come, the lord of the vineyard saith unto his steward, Call the laborers, and pay them their hire, beginning from the last unto the first.</font>
39:20:9: <font color="#CC0000">And when they came that were hired about the eleventh hour, they received every man a shilling.</font>
39:20:10: <font color="#CC0000">And when the first came, they supposed that they would receive more; and they likewise received every man a shilling.</font>
39:20:11: <font color="#CC0000">And when they received it, they murmured against the householder,</font>
39:20:12: <font color="#CC0000">saying, These last have spent but one hour, and thou hast made them equal unto us, who have borne the burden of the day and the scorching heat.</font>
39:20:13: <font color="#CC0000">But he answered and said to one of them, Friend, I do thee no wrong: didst not thou agree with me for a shilling?</font>
39:20:14: <font color="#CC0000">Take up that which is thine, and go thy way; it is my will to give unto this last, even as unto thee.</font>
39:20:15: <font color="#CC0000">Is it not lawful for me to do what I will with mine own? or is thine eye evil, because I am good?</font>
39:20:16: <font color="#CC0000">So the last shall be first, and the first last.</font>
39:20:18: <font color="#CC0000">Behold, we go up to Jerusalem; and the Son of man shall be delivered unto the chief priests and scribes; and they shall condemn him to death,</font>
39:20:19: <font color="#CC0000">and shall deliver him unto the Gentiles to mock, and to scourge, and to crucify: and the third day he shall be raised up.</font>
39:20:21: And he said unto her, <font color="#CC0000">What wouldest thou</font>? She saith unto him, Command that these my two sons may sit, one on thy right hand, and one on thy left hand, in thy kingdom.
39:20:22: But Jesus answered and said, <font color="#CC0000">Ye know not what ye ask. Are ye able to drink the cup that I am about to drink</font>? They say unto him, We are able.
39:20:23: He saith unto them, My cup indeed <font color="#CC0000">ye shall drink: but to sit on my right hand, and on my left hand, is not mine to give; but it is for them for whom it hath been prepared of my Father</font>.
39:20:25: But Jesus called them unto him, and said, <font color="#CC0000">Ye know that the rulers of the Gentiles lord it over them, and their great ones exercise authority over them</font>.
39:20:26: <font color="#CC0000">Not so shall it be among you: but whosoever would become great among you shall be your minister;</font>
39:20:27: <font color="#CC0000">and whosoever would be first among you shall be your servant:</font>
39:20:28: <font color="#CC0000">even as the Son of man came not to be ministered unto, but to minister, and to give his life a ransom for many.</font>
39:20:32: And Jesus stood still, and called them, and said, <font color="#CC0000">What will ye that I should do unto you</font>?
39:21:2: saying unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Go into the village that is over against you, and straightway ye shall find an ass tied, and a colt with her: loose them, and bring them unto me</font>.
39:21:3: <font color="#CC0000">And if any one say aught unto you, ye shall say, The Lord hath need of them; and straightway he will send them.</font>
39:21:13: and he saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">It is written, My house shall be called a house of prayer: but ye make it a den of</font> robbers.
39:21:16: and said unto him, Hearest thou what these are saying? And Jesus saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Yea: did ye never read, Out of the mouth of babes and sucklings thou hast perfected praise</font>?
39:21:19: And seeing a fig tree by the way side, he came to it, and found nothing thereon, but leaves only; and he saith unto it, <font color="#CC0000">Let there be no fruit from thee henceforward for ever</font>. And immediately the fig tree withered away.
39:21:21: And Jesus answered and said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Verily I say unto you, If ye have faith, and doubt not, ye shall not only do what is done to the fig tree, but even if ye shall say unto this mountain, Be thou taken up and cast into the sea, it shall be done</font>.
39:21:22: <font color="#CC0000">And all things, whatsoever ye shall ask in prayer, believing, ye shall receive.</font>
39:21:24: And Jesus answered and said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">I also will ask you one question, which if ye tell me, I likewise will tell you by what authority I do these things</font>.
39:21:25: <font color="#CC0000">The baptism of John, whence was it? from heaven or from men</font>? And they reasoned with themselves, saying, If we shall say, From heaven; he will say unto us, Why then did ye not believe him?
39:21:27: And they answered Jesus, and said, We know not. He also said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Neither tell I you by what authority I do these things</font>.
39:21:28: <font color="#CC0000">But what think ye? A man had two sons; and he came to the first, and said, Son, go work to-day in the vineyard.</font>
39:21:29: <font color="#CC0000">And he answered and said, I will not: but afterward he repented himself, and went.</font>
39:21:30: <font color="#CC0000">And he came to the second, and said likewise. And he answered and said, I go, sir: and went not.</font>
39:21:31: Which <font color="#CC0000">of the two did the will of his father</font>? They say, The first. Jesus saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Verily I say unto you, that the publicans and the harlots go into the kingdom of God before you</font>.
39:21:32: <font color="#CC0000">For John came unto you in the way of righteousness, and ye believed him not; but the publicans and the harlots believed him: and ye, when ye saw it, did not even repent yourselves afterward, that ye might believe him.</font>
39:21:33: <font color="#CC0000">Hear another parable: There was a man that was a householder, who planted a vineyard, and set a hedge about it, and digged a winepress in it, and built a tower, and let it out to husbandmen, and went into another country.</font>
39:21:34: <font color="#CC0000">And when the season of the fruits drew near, he sent his servants to the husbandmen, to receive his fruits.</font>
39:21:35: <font color="#CC0000">And the husbandmen took his servants, and beat one, and killed another, and stoned another.</font>
39:21:36: <font color="#CC0000">Again, he sent other servants more than the first: and they did unto them in like manner.</font>
39:21:37: <font color="#CC0000">But afterward he sent unto them his son, saying, They will reverence my son.</font>
39:21:38: <font color="#CC0000">But the husbandmen, when they saw the son, said among themselves, This is the heir; come, let us kill him, and take his inheritance.</font>
39:21:39: <font color="#CC0000">And they took him, and cast him forth out of the vineyard, and killed him.</font>
39:21:40: <font color="#CC0000">When therefore the lord of the vineyard shall come, what will he do unto those husbandmen?</font>
39:21:42: Jesus saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Did ye never read in the scriptures,  The stone which the builders rejected, The same was made the head of the corner; This was from the Lord, And it is marvellous in our eyes</font>?
39:21:43: <font color="#CC0000">Therefore say I unto you, The kingdom of God shall be taken away from you, and shall be given to a nation bringing forth the fruits thereof.</font>
39:21:44: <font color="#CC0000">And he that falleth on this stone shall be broken to pieces: but on whomsoever it shall fall, it will scatter him as dust.</font>
39:22:2: <font color="#CC0000">The kingdom of heaven is likened unto a certain king, who made a marriage feast for his son,</font>
39:22:3: <font color="#CC0000">and sent forth his servants to call them that were bidden to the marriage feast: and they would not come.</font>
39:22:4: <font color="#CC0000">Again he sent forth other servants, saying, Tell them that are bidden, Behold, I have made ready my dinner; my oxen and my fatlings are killed, and all things are ready: come to the marriage feast.</font>
39:22:5: <font color="#CC0000">But they made light of it, and went their ways, one to his own farm, another to his merchandise;</font>
39:22:6: <font color="#CC0000">and the rest laid hold on his servants, and treated them shamefully, and killed them.</font>
39:22:7: <font color="#CC0000">But the king was wroth; and he sent his armies, and destroyed those murderers, and burned their city.</font>
39:22:8: <font color="#CC0000">Then saith he to his servants, The wedding is ready, but they that were bidden were not worthy.</font>
39:22:9: <font color="#CC0000">Go ye therefore unto the partings of the highways, and as many as ye shall find, bid to the marriage feast.</font>
39:22:10: <font color="#CC0000">And those servants went out into the highways, and gathered together all as many as they found, both bad and good: and the wedding was filled with guests.</font>
39:22:11: <font color="#CC0000">But when the king came in to behold the guests, he saw there a man who had not on a wedding-garment:</font>
39:22:12: <font color="#CC0000">and he saith unto him, Friend, how camest thou in hither not having a wedding-garment? And he was speechless.</font>
39:22:13: <font color="#CC0000">Then the king said to the servants, Bind him hand and foot, and cast him out into the outer darkness; there shall be the weeping and the gnashing of teeth.</font>
39:22:14: <font color="#CC0000">For many are called, but few chosen.</font>
39:22:18: But Jesus perceived their wickedness, and said, <font color="#CC0000">Why make ye trial of me, ye hypocrites</font>?
39:22:19: Show <font color="#CC0000">me the tribute money</font>. And they brought unto him a denarius.
39:22:20: And he saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Whose is this image and superscription</font>?
39:22:21: They say unto him, Cæsar’s. Then saith he unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Render therefore unto Cæsar the things that are Cæsar’s; and unto God the things that are</font> God’s.
39:22:29: But Jesus answered and said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Ye do err, not knowing the scriptures, nor the power of God</font>.
39:22:30: <font color="#CC0000">For in the resurrection they neither marry, nor are given in marriage, but are as angels in heaven.</font>
39:22:31: <font color="#CC0000">But as touching the resurrection of the dead, have ye not read that which was spoken unto you by God, saying,</font>
39:22:32: <font color="#CC0000">I am the God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob? God is not the God of the dead, but of the living.</font>
39:22:37: And he said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Thou shalt love the Lord thy God with all thy heart, and with all thy soul, and with all thy mind</font>.
39:22:38: <font color="#CC0000">This is the great and first commandment.</font>
39:22:39: <font color="#CC0000">And a second like unto it is this, Thou shalt love thy neighbor as thyself.</font>
39:22:40: <font color="#CC0000">On these two commandments the whole law hangeth, and the prophets.</font>
39:22:42: saying, <font color="#CC0000">What think ye of the Christ? whose son is he? They say unto him, The son of David</font>.
39:22:43: He saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">How then doth David in the Spirit call him Lord, saying</font>,
39:22:44: <font color="#CC0000">The Lord said unto my Lord, Sit thou on my right hand, Till I put thine enemies underneath thy feet?</font>
39:22:45: <font color="#CC0000">If David then calleth him Lord, how is he his son?</font>
39:23:2: saying, <font color="#CC0000">The scribes and the Pharisees sit on Moses’ seat</font>:
39:23:3: <font color="#CC0000">all things therefore whatsoever they bid you, these do and observe: but do not ye after their works; for they say, and do not.</font>
39:23:4: <font color="#CC0000">Yea, they bind heavy burdens and grievous to be borne, and lay them on men’s shoulders; but they themselves will not move them with their finger.</font>
39:23:5: <font color="#CC0000">But all their works they do to be seen of men: for they make broad their phylacteries, and enlarge the borders of their garments,</font>
39:23:6: <font color="#CC0000">and love the chief place at feasts, and the chief seats in the synagogues,</font>
39:23:7: <font color="#CC0000">and the salutations in the marketplaces, and to be called of men, Rabbi.</font>
39:23:8: <font color="#CC0000">But be not ye called Rabbi: for one is your teacher, and all ye are brethren.</font>
39:23:9: <font color="#CC0000">And call no man your father on the earth: for one is your Father, even he who is in heaven.</font>
39:23:10: <font color="#CC0000">Neither be ye called masters: for one is your master, even the Christ.</font>
39:23:11: <font color="#CC0000">But he that is greatest among you shall be your servant.</font>
39:23:12: <font color="#CC0000">And whosoever shall exalt himself shall be humbled; and whosoever shall humble himself shall be exalted.</font>
39:23:13: <font color="#CC0000">But woe unto you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! because ye shut the kingdom of heaven against men: for ye enter not in yourselves, neither suffer ye them that are entering in to enter.</font>
39:23:15: <font color="#CC0000">Woe unto you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! for ye compass sea and land to make one proselyte; and when he is become so, ye make him twofold more a son of hell than yourselves.</font>
39:23:16: <font color="#CC0000">Woe unto you, ye blind guides, that say, Whosoever shall swear by the temple, it is nothing; but whosoever shall swear by the gold of the temple, he is a debtor.</font>
39:23:17: <font color="#CC0000">Ye fools and blind: for which is greater, the gold, or the temple that hath sanctified the gold?</font>
39:23:18: <font color="#CC0000">And, Whosoever shall swear by the altar, it is nothing; but whosoever shall swear by the gift that is upon it, he is a debtor.</font>
39:23:19: <font color="#CC0000">Ye blind: for which is greater, the gift, or the altar that sanctifieth the gift?</font>
39:23:20: <font color="#CC0000">He therefore that sweareth by the altar, sweareth by it, and by all things thereon.</font>
39:23:21: <font color="#CC0000">And he that sweareth by the temple, sweareth by it, and by him that dwelleth therein.</font>
39:23:22: <font color="#CC0000">And he that sweareth by the heaven, sweareth by the throne of God, and by him that sitteth thereon.</font>
39:23:23: <font color="#CC0000">Woe unto you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! for ye tithe mint and anise and cummin, and have left undone the weightier matters of the law, justice, and mercy, and faith: but these ye ought to have done, and not to have left the other undone.</font>
39:23:24: <font color="#CC0000">Ye blind guides, that strain out the gnat, and swallow the camel!</font>
39:23:25: <font color="#CC0000">Woe unto you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! for ye cleanse the outside of the cup and of the platter, but within they are full from extortion and excess.</font>
39:23:26: <font color="#CC0000">Thou blind Pharisee, cleanse first the inside of the cup and of the platter, that the outside thereof may become clean also.</font>
39:23:27: <font color="#CC0000">Woe unto you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! for ye are like unto whited sepulchres, which outwardly appear beautiful, but inwardly are full of dead men’s bones, and of all uncleanness.</font>
39:23:28: <font color="#CC0000">Even so ye also outwardly appear righteous unto men, but inwardly ye are full of hypocrisy and iniquity.</font>
39:23:29: <font color="#CC0000">Woe unto you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! for ye build the sepulchres of the prophets, and garnish the tombs of the righteous,</font>
39:23:30: <font color="#CC0000">and say, If we had been in the days of our fathers, we should not have been partakers with them in the blood of the prophets.</font>
39:23:31: <font color="#CC0000">Wherefore ye witness to yourselves, that ye are sons of them that slew the prophets.</font>
39:23:32: <font color="#CC0000">Fill ye up then the measure of your fathers.</font>
39:23:33: <font color="#CC0000">Ye serpents, ye offspring of vipers, how shall ye escape the judgment of hell?</font>
39:23:34: <font color="#CC0000">Therefore, behold, I send unto you prophets, and wise men, and scribes: some of them shall ye kill and crucify; and some of them shall ye scourge in your synagogues, and persecute from city to city:</font>
39:23:35: <font color="#CC0000">that upon you may come all the righteous blood shed on the earth, from the blood of Abel the righteous unto the blood of Zachariah son of Barachiah, whom ye slew between the sanctuary and the altar.</font>
