39:23:36: <font color="#CC0000">Verily I say unto you, All these things shall come upon this generation.</font>
39:23:37: <font color="#CC0000">O Jerusalem, Jerusalem, that killeth the prophets, and stoneth them that are sent unto her! how often would I have gathered thy children together, even as a hen gathereth her chickens under her wings, and ye would not!</font>
39:23:38: <font color="#CC0000">Behold, your house is left unto you desolate.</font>
39:23:39: <font color="#CC0000">For I say unto you, Ye shall not see me henceforth, till ye shall say, Blessed is he that cometh in the name of the Lord.</font>
39:24:2: But he answered and said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">See ye not all these things? verily I say unto you, There shall not be left here one stone upon another, that shall not be thrown down</font>.
39:24:4: And Jesus answered and said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Take heed that no man lead you</font> astray.
39:24:5: <font color="#CC0000">For many shall come in my name, saying, I am the Christ; and shall lead many astray.</font>
39:24:6: <font color="#CC0000">And ye shall hear of wars and rumors of wars; see that ye be not troubled: for these things must needs come to pass; but the end is not yet.</font>
39:24:7: <font color="#CC0000">For nation shall rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom; and there shall be famines and earthquakes in divers places.</font>
39:24:8: <font color="#CC0000">But all these things are the beginning of travail.</font>
39:24:9: <font color="#CC0000">Then shall they deliver you up unto tribulation, and shall kill you: and ye shall be hated of all the nations for my name’s sake.</font>
39:24:10: <font color="#CC0000">And then shall many stumble, and shall deliver up one another, and shall hate one another.</font>
39:24:11: <font color="#CC0000">And many false prophets shall arise, and shall lead many astray.</font>
39:24:12: <font color="#CC0000">And because iniquity shall be multiplied, the love of the many shall wax cold.</font>
39:24:13: <font color="#CC0000">But he that endureth to the end, the same shall be saved.</font>
39:24:14: <font color="#CC0000">And this gospel of the kingdom shall be preached in the whole world for a testimony unto all the nations; and then shall the end come.</font>
39:24:15: <font color="#CC0000">When therefore ye see the abomination of desolation, which was spoken of through Daniel the prophet, standing in the holy place (let him that readeth understand),</font>
39:24:16: <font color="#CC0000">then let them that are in Judæa flee unto the mountains:</font>
39:24:17: <font color="#CC0000">let him that is on the housetop not go down to take out the things that are in his house:</font>
39:24:18: <font color="#CC0000">and let him that is in the field not return back to take his cloak.</font>
39:24:19: <font color="#CC0000">But woe unto them that are with child and to them that give suck in those days!</font>
39:24:20: <font color="#CC0000">And pray ye that your flight be not in the winter, neither on a sabbath:</font>
39:24:21: <font color="#CC0000">for then shall be great tribulation, such as hath not been from the beginning of the world until now, no, nor ever shall be.</font>
39:24:22: <font color="#CC0000">And except those days had been shortened, no flesh would have been saved: but for the elect’s sake those days shall be shortened.</font>
39:24:23: <font color="#CC0000">Then if any man shall say unto you, Lo, here is the Christ, or, Here; believe it not.</font>
39:24:24: <font color="#CC0000">For there shall arise false Christs, and false prophets, and shall show great signs and wonders; so as to lead astray, if possible, even the elect.</font>
39:24:25: <font color="#CC0000">Behold, I have told you beforehand.</font>
39:24:26: <font color="#CC0000">If therefore they shall say unto you, Behold, he is in the wilderness; go not forth: Behold, he is in the inner chambers; believe it not.</font>
39:24:27: <font color="#CC0000">For as the lightning cometh forth from the east, and is seen even unto the west; so shall be the coming of the Son of man.</font>
39:24:28: <font color="#CC0000">Wheresoever the carcase is, there will the eagles be gathered together.</font>
39:24:29: <font color="#CC0000">But immediately after the tribulation of those days the sun shall be darkened, and the moon shall not give her light, and the stars shall fall from heaven, and the powers of the heavens shall be shaken:</font>
39:24:30: <font color="#CC0000">and then shall appear the sign of the Son of man in heaven: and then shall all the tribes of the earth mourn, and they shall see the Son of man coming on the clouds of heaven with power and great glory.</font>
39:24:31: <font color="#CC0000">And he shall send forth his angels with a great sound of a trumpet, and they shall gather together his elect from the four winds, from one end of heaven to the other.</font>
39:24:32: <font color="#CC0000">Now from the fig tree learn her parable: when her branch is now become tender, and putteth forth its leaves, ye know that the summer is nigh;</font>
39:24:33: <font color="#CC0000">even so ye also, when ye see all these things, know ye that he is nigh, even at the doors.</font>
39:24:34: <font color="#CC0000">Verily I say unto you, This generation shall not pass away, till all these things be accomplished.</font>
39:24:35: <font color="#CC0000">Heaven and earth shall pass away, but my words shall not pass away.</font>
39:24:36: <font color="#CC0000">But of that day and hour knoweth no one, not even the angels of heaven, neither the Son, but the Father only.</font>
39:24:37: <font color="#CC0000">And as were the days of Noah, so shall be the coming of the Son of man.</font>
39:24:38: <font color="#CC0000">For as in those days which were before the flood they were eating and drinking, marrying and giving in marriage, until the day that Noah entered into the ark,</font>
39:24:39: <font color="#CC0000">and they knew not until the flood came, and took them all away; so shall be the coming of the Son of man.</font>
39:24:40: <font color="#CC0000">Then shall two men be in the field; one is taken, and one is left:</font>
39:24:41: <font color="#CC0000">two women shall be grinding at the mill; one is taken, and one is left.</font>
39:24:42: <font color="#CC0000">Watch therefore: for ye know not on what day your Lord cometh.</font>
39:24:43: <font color="#CC0000">But know this, that if the master of the house had known in what watch the thief was coming, he would have watched, and would not have suffered his house to be broken through.</font>
39:24:44: <font color="#CC0000">Therefore be ye also ready; for in an hour that ye think not the Son of man cometh.</font>
39:24:45: <font color="#CC0000">Who then is the faithful and wise servant, whom his lord hath set over his household, to give them their food in due season?</font>
39:24:46: <font color="#CC0000">Blessed is that servant, whom his lord when he cometh shall find so doing.</font>
39:24:47: <font color="#CC0000">Verily I say unto you, that he will set him over all that he hath.</font>
39:24:48: <font color="#CC0000">But if that evil servant shall say in his heart, My lord tarrieth;</font>
39:24:49: <font color="#CC0000">and shall begin to beat his fellow-servants, and shall eat and drink with the drunken;</font>
39:24:50: <font color="#CC0000">the lord of that servant shall come in a day when he expecteth not, and in an hour when he knoweth not,</font>
39:24:51: <font color="#CC0000">and shall cut him asunder, and appoint his portion with the hypocrites: there shall be the weeping and the gnashing of teeth.</font>
39:25:1: <font color="#CC0000">Then shall the kingdom of heaven be likened unto ten virgins, who took their lamps, and went forth to meet the bridegroom.</font>
39:25:2: <font color="#CC0000">And five of them were foolish, and five were wise.</font>
39:25:3: <font color="#CC0000">For the foolish, when they took their lamps, took no oil with them:</font>
39:25:4: <font color="#CC0000">but the wise took oil in their vessels with their lamps.</font>
39:25:5: <font color="#CC0000">Now while the bridegroom tarried, they all slumbered and slept.</font>
39:25:6: <font color="#CC0000">But at midnight there is a cry, Behold, the bridegroom! Come ye forth to meet him.</font>
39:25:7: <font color="#CC0000">Then all those virgins arose, and trimmed their lamps.</font>
39:25:8: <font color="#CC0000">And the foolish said unto the wise, Give us of your oil; for our lamps are going out.</font>
39:25:9: <font color="#CC0000">But the wise answered, saying, Peradventure there will not be enough for us and you: go ye rather to them that sell, and buy for yourselves.</font>
39:25:10: <font color="#CC0000">And while they went away to buy, the bridegroom came; and they that were ready went in with him to the marriage feast: and the door was shut.</font>
39:25:11: <font color="#CC0000">Afterward came also the other virgins, saying, Lord, Lord, open to us.</font>
39:25:12: <font color="#CC0000">But he answered and said, Verily I say unto you, I know you not.</font>
39:25:13: <font color="#CC0000">Watch therefore, for ye know not the day nor the hour.</font>
39:25:14: <font color="#CC0000">Forit isaswhena man, going into another country, called his own servants, and delivered unto them his goods.</font>
39:25:15: <font color="#CC0000">And unto one he gave five talents, to another two, to another one; to each according to his several ability; and he went on his journey.</font>
39:25:16: <font color="#CC0000">Straightway he that received the five talents went and traded with them, and made other five talents.</font>
39:25:17: <font color="#CC0000">In like manner he also that received the two gained other two.</font>
39:25:18: <font color="#CC0000">But he that received the one went away and digged in the earth, and hid his lord’s money.</font>
39:25:19: <font color="#CC0000">Now after a long time the lord of those servants cometh, and maketh a reckoning with them.</font>
39:25:20: <font color="#CC0000">And he that received the five talents came and brought other five talents, saying, Lord, thou deliveredst unto me five talents: lo, I have gained other five talents.</font>
39:25:21: <font color="#CC0000">His lord said unto him, Well done, good and faithful servant: thou hast been faithful over a few things, I will set thee over many things; enter thou into the joy of thy lord.</font>
39:25:22: <font color="#CC0000">And he also that received the two talents came and said, Lord, thou deliveredst unto me two talents: lo, I have gained other two talents.</font>
39:25:23: <font color="#CC0000">His lord said unto him, Well done, good and faithful servant: thou hast been faithful over a few things, I will set thee over many things; enter thou into the joy of thy lord.</font>
39:25:24: <font color="#CC0000">And he also that had received the one talent came and said, Lord, I knew thee that thou art a hard man, reaping where thou didst not sow, and gathering where thou didst not scatter;</font>
39:25:25: <font color="#CC0000">and I was afraid, and went away and hid thy talent in the earth: lo, thou hast thine own.</font>
39:25:26: <font color="#CC0000">But his lord answered and said unto him, Thou wicked and slothful servant, thou knewest that I reap where I sowed not, and gather where I did not scatter;</font>
39:25:27: <font color="#CC0000">thou oughtest therefore to have put my money to the bankers, and at my coming I should have received back mine own with interest.</font>
39:25:28: <font color="#CC0000">Take ye away therefore the talent from him, and give it unto him that hath the ten talents.</font>
39:25:29: <font color="#CC0000">For unto every one that hath shall be given, and he shall have abundance: but from him that hath not, even that which he hath shall be taken away.</font>
39:25:30: <font color="#CC0000">And cast ye out the unprofitable servant into the outer darkness: there shall be the weeping and the gnashing of teeth.</font>
39:25:31: <font color="#CC0000">But when the Son of man shall come in his glory, and all the angels with him, then shall he sit on the throne of his glory:</font>
39:25:32: <font color="#CC0000">and before him shall be gathered all the nations: and he shall separate them one from another, as the shepherd separateth the sheep from the goats;</font>
39:25:33: <font color="#CC0000">and he shall set the sheep on his right hand, but the goats on the left.</font>
39:25:34: <font color="#CC0000">Then shall the King say unto them on his right hand, Come, ye blessed of my Father, inherit the kingdom prepared for you from the foundation of the world:</font>
39:25:35: <font color="#CC0000">for I was hungry, and ye gave me to eat; I was thirsty, and ye gave me drink; I was a stranger, and ye took me in;</font>
39:25:36: <font color="#CC0000">naked, and ye clothed me; I was sick, and ye visited me; I was in prison, and ye came unto me.</font>
39:25:37: <font color="#CC0000">Then shall the righteous answer him, saying, Lord, when saw we thee hungry, and fed thee? or athirst, and gave thee drink?</font>
39:25:38: <font color="#CC0000">And when saw we thee a stranger, and took thee in? or naked, and clothed thee?</font>
39:25:39: <font color="#CC0000">And when saw we thee sick, or in prison, and came unto thee?</font>
39:25:40: <font color="#CC0000">And the King shall answer and say unto them, Verily I say unto you, Inasmuch as ye did it unto one of these my brethren, even these least, ye did it unto me.</font>
39:25:41: <font color="#CC0000">Then shall he say also unto them on the left hand, Depart from me, ye cursed, into the eternal fire which is prepared for the devil and his angels:</font>
39:25:42: <font color="#CC0000">for I was hungry, and ye did not give me to eat; I was thirsty, and ye gave me no drink;</font>
39:25:43: <font color="#CC0000">I was a stranger, and ye took me not in; naked, and ye clothed me not; sick, and in prison, and ye visited me not.</font>
39:25:44: <font color="#CC0000">Then shall they also answer, saying, Lord, when saw we thee hungry, or athirst, or a stranger, or naked, or sick, or in prison, and did not minister unto thee?</font>
39:25:45: <font color="#CC0000">Then shall he answer them, saying, Verily I say unto you, Inasmuch as ye did it not unto one of these least, ye did it not unto me.</font>
39:25:46: <font color="#CC0000">And these shall go away into eternal punishment: but the righteous into eternal life.</font>
39:26:2: <font color="#CC0000">Ye know that after two days the passover cometh, and the Son of man is delivered up to be crucified.</font>
39:26:10: But Jesus perceiving it said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Why trouble ye the woman? for she hath wrought a good work upon me</font>.
39:26:11: <font color="#CC0000">For ye have the poor always with you; but me ye have not always.</font>
39:26:12: <font color="#CC0000">For in that she poured this ointment upon my body, she did it to prepare me for burial.</font>
39:26:13: <font color="#CC0000">Verily I say unto you, Wheresoever this gospel shall be preached in the whole world, that also which this woman hath done shall be spoken of for a memorial of her.</font>
39:26:18: And he said, <font color="#CC0000">Go into the city to such a man, and say unto him, The Teacher saith, My time is at hand; I keep the passover at thy house with my disciples</font>.
39:26:21: and as they were eating, he said, <font color="#CC0000">Verily I say unto you, that one of you shall betray me</font>.
39:26:23: And he answered and said, <font color="#CC0000">He that dipped his hand with me in the dish, the same shall betray me</font>.
39:26:24: <font color="#CC0000">The Son of man goeth, even as it is written of him: but woe unto that man through whom the Son of man is betrayed! good were it for that man if he had not been born.</font>
39:26:25: And Judas, who betrayed him, answered and said, Is it I, Rabbi? He saith unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Thou hast said</font>.
39:26:26: And as they were eating, Jesus took bread, and blessed, and brake it; and he gave to the disciples, and said, <font color="#CC0000">Take, eat; this is my body</font>.
39:26:27: And he took a cup, and gave thanks, and gave to them, saying, <font color="#CC0000">Drink ye all of it</font>;
39:26:28: <font color="#CC0000">for this is my blood of the covenant, which is poured out for many unto remission of sins.</font>
39:26:29: <font color="#CC0000">But I say unto you, I shall not drink henceforth of this fruit of the vine, until that day when I drink it new with you in my Father’s kingdom.</font>
39:26:31: Then saith Jesus unto them, <font color="#CC0000">All ye shall be offended in me this night: for it is written, I will smite the shepherd, and the sheep of the flock shall be scattered abroad</font>.
39:26:32: <font color="#CC0000">But after I am raised up, I will go before you into Galilee.</font>
39:26:34: Jesus said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Verily I say unto thee, that this night, before the cock crow, thou shalt deny me thrice</font>.
39:26:36: Then cometh Jesus with them unto a place called Gethsemane, and saith unto his disciples, <font color="#CC0000">Sit ye here, while I go yonder and pray</font>.
39:26:38: Then saith he unto them, <font color="#CC0000">My soul is exceeding sorrowful, even unto death: abide ye here, and watch with me</font>.
39:26:39: And he went forward a little, and fell on his face, and prayed, saying, <font color="#CC0000">My Father, if it be possible, let this cup pass away from me: nevertheless, not as I will, but as thou wilt</font>.
39:26:40: And he cometh unto the disciples, and findeth them sleeping, and saith unto Peter, <font color="#CC0000">What, could ye not watch with me one hour</font>?
39:26:41: <font color="#CC0000">Watch and pray, that ye enter not into temptation: the spirit indeed is willing, but the flesh is weak.</font>
39:26:42: Again a second time he went away, and prayed, saying, <font color="#CC0000">My Father, if this cannot pass away, except I drink it, thy will be done</font>.
39:26:45: Then cometh he to the disciples, and saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Sleep on now, and take your rest: behold, the hour is at hand, and the Son of man is betrayed into the hands of sinners</font>.
39:26:46: <font color="#CC0000">Arise, let us be going: behold, he is at hand that betrayeth me.</font>
39:26:50: And Jesus said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Friend, do that for which thou art come</font>. Then they came and laid hands on Jesus, and took him.
39:26:52: Then saith Jesus unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Put up again thy sword into its place: for all they that take the sword shall perish with the sword</font>.
39:26:53: <font color="#CC0000">Or thinkest thou that I cannot beseech my Father, and he shall even now send me more than twelve legions of angels?</font>
39:26:54: <font color="#CC0000">How then should the scriptures be fulfilled, that thus it must be?</font>
39:26:55: In that hour said Jesus to the multitudes, <font color="#CC0000">Are ye come out as against a robber with swords and staves to seize me? I sat daily in the temple teaching, and ye took me</font> not.
39:26:56: <font color="#CC0000">But all this is come to pass, that the scriptures of the prophets might be fulfilled</font>. Then all the disciples left him, and fled.
39:26:64: Jesus saith unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Thou hast said: nevertheless I say unto you, Henceforth ye shall see the Son of man sitting at the right hand of Power, and coming on the clouds of heaven</font>.
39:27:11: Now Jesus stood before the governor: and the governor asked him, saying, Art thou the King of the Jews? And Jesus said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Thou sayest</font>.
39:27:46: And about the ninth hour Jesus cried with a loud voice, saying, <font color="#CC0000">Eli, Eli, lama sabachthani</font>? that is, My God, my God, why hast thou forsaken me?
39:28:9: And behold, Jesus met them, saying, <font color="#CC0000">All hail</font>. And they came and took hold of his feet, and worshipped him.
39:28:10: Then saith Jesus unto them, Fear <font color="#CC0000">not: go tell my brethren that they depart into Galilee, and there shall they see me</font>.
39:28:18: And Jesus came to them and spake unto them, saying, <font color="#CC0000">All authority hath been given unto me in heaven and on earth</font>.
39:28:19: <font color="#CC0000">Go ye therefore, and make disciples of all the nations, baptizing them into the name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Spirit:</font>
39:28:20: <font color="#CC0000">teaching them to observe all things whatsoever I commanded you: and lo, I am with you always, even unto the end of the world</font>.
40:1:15: and saying, <font color="#CC0000">The time is fulfilled, and the kingdom of God is at hand: repent ye, and believe in the gospel</font>.
40:1:17: And Jesus said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Come ye after me, and I will make you to become fishers of men</font>.
40:1:25: And Jesus rebuked him, saying, <font color="#CC0000">Hold thy peace, and come out of him</font>.
40:1:38: And he saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Let us go elsewhere into the next towns, that I may preach there also; for to this end came I forth</font>.
40:1:41: And being moved with compassion, he stretched forth his hand, and touched him, and saith unto him, <font color="#CC0000">I will; be thou made clean</font>.
40:1:44: and saith unto him, <font color="#CC0000">See thou say nothing to any man: but go show thyself to the priest, and offer for thy cleansing the things which Moses commanded, for a testimony unto them</font>.
40:2:5: And Jesus seeing their faith saith unto the sick of the palsy, <font color="#CC0000">Son, thy sins are forgiven</font>.
40:2:8: And straightway Jesus, perceiving in his spirit that they so reasoned within themselves, saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Why reason ye these things in your hearts</font>?
40:2:9: <font color="#CC0000">Which is easier, to say to the sick of the palsy, Thy sins are forgiven; or to say, Arise, and take up thy bed, and walk?</font>
40:2:10: <font color="#CC0000">But that ye may know that the Son of man hath authority on earth to forgive sins</font> (he saith to the sick of the palsy),
40:2:11: <font color="#CC0000">I say unto thee, Arise, take up thy bed, and go unto thy house.</font>
40:2:14: And as he passed by, he saw Levi the son of Alphæus sitting at the place of toll, and he saith unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Follow me</font>. And he arose and followed him.
40:2:17: And when Jesus heard it, he saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">They that are whole have no need of a physician, but they that are sick: I came not to call the righteous, but sinners</font>.
40:2:19: And Jesus said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Can the sons of the bridechamber fast, while the bridegroom is with them? as long as they have the bridegroom with them, they cannot fast</font>.
40:2:20: <font color="#CC0000">But the days will come, when the bridegroom shall be taken away from them, and then will they fast in that day.</font>
40:2:21: <font color="#CC0000">No man seweth a piece of undressed cloth on an old garment: else that which should fill it up taketh from it, the new from the old, and a worse rent is made.</font>
40:2:22: <font color="#CC0000">And no man putteth new wine into old wine-skins; else the wine will burst the skins, and the wine perisheth, and the skins: but they put new wine into fresh wine-skins.</font>
40:2:25: And he said unto them, Did <font color="#CC0000">ye never read what David did, when he had need, and was hungry, he, and they that were with him</font>?
40:2:26: <font color="#CC0000">How he entered into the house of God when Abiathar was high priest, and ate the showbread, which it is not lawful to eat save for the priests, and gave also to them that were with him?</font>
40:2:27: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">The sabbath was made for man, and not man for the sabbath</font>:
40:2:28: <font color="#CC0000">so that the Son of man is lord even of the sabbath.</font>
40:3:3: And he saith unto the man that had his hand withered, <font color="#CC0000">Stand forth</font>.
40:3:4: And he saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Is it lawful on the sabbath day to do good, or to do harm? to save a life, or to kill</font>? But they held their peace.
40:3:5: And when he had looked round about on them with anger, being grieved at the hardening of their heart, he saith unto the man, <font color="#CC0000">Stretch forth thy hand</font>. And he stretched it forth; and his hand was restored.
40:3:23: And he called them unto him, and said unto them in parables, <font color="#CC0000">How can Satan cast out Satan</font>?
40:3:24: <font color="#CC0000">And if a kingdom be divided against itself, that kingdom cannot stand.</font>
40:3:25: <font color="#CC0000">And if a house be divided against itself, that house will not be able to stand.</font>
40:3:26: <font color="#CC0000">And if Satan hath risen up against himself, and is divided, he cannot stand, but hath an end.</font>
40:3:27: <font color="#CC0000">But no one can enter into the house of the strong man, and spoil his goods, except he first bind the strong man; and then he will spoil his house.</font>
40:3:28: <font color="#CC0000">Verily I say unto you, All their sins shall be forgiven unto the sons of men, and their blasphemies wherewith soever they shall blaspheme:</font>
40:3:29: <font color="#CC0000">but whosoever shall blaspheme against the Holy Spirit hath never forgiveness, but is guilty of an eternal sin:</font>
40:3:33: And he answereth them, and saith, <font color="#CC0000">Who is my mother and my brethren</font>?
40:3:34: And looking round on them that sat round about him, he saith, <font color="#CC0000">Behold, my mother and my brethren</font>!
40:3:35: <font color="#CC0000">For whosoever shall do the will of God, the same is my brother, and sister, and mother.</font>
40:4:3: <font color="#CC0000">Hearken: Behold, the sower went forth to sow:</font>
40:4:4: <font color="#CC0000">and it came to pass, as he sowed, some seed fell by the way side, and the birds came and devoured it.</font>
40:4:5: <font color="#CC0000">And other fell on the rocky ground, where it had not much earth; and straightway it sprang up, because it had no deepness of earth:</font>
40:4:6: <font color="#CC0000">and when the sun was risen, it was scorched; and because it had no root, it withered away.</font>
40:4:7: <font color="#CC0000">And other fell among the thorns, and the thorns grew up, and choked it, and it yielded no fruit.</font>
40:4:8: <font color="#CC0000">And others fell into the good ground, and yielded fruit, growing up and increasing; and brought forth, thirtyfold, and sixtyfold, and a hundredfold.</font>
40:4:9: And <font color="#CC0000">he said, Who hath ears to hear, let him hear</font>.
40:4:11: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Unto you is given the mystery of the kingdom of God: but unto them that are without, all things are done in parables</font>:
40:4:12: <font color="#CC0000">that seeing they may see, and not perceive; and hearing they may hear, and not understand; lest haply they should turn again, and it should be forgiven them.</font>
40:4:13: And he saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Know ye not this parable? and how shall ye know all the parables</font>?
40:4:15: <font color="#CC0000">And these are they by the way side, where the word is sown; and when they have heard, straightway cometh Satan, and taketh away the word which hath been sown in them.</font>
40:4:16: <font color="#CC0000">And these in like manner are they that are sown upon the rocky places, who, when they have heard the word, straightway receive it with joy;</font>
40:4:17: <font color="#CC0000">and they have no root in themselves, but endure for a while; then, when tribulation or persecution ariseth because of the word, straightway they stumble.</font>
40:4:18: <font color="#CC0000">And others are they that are sown among the thorns; these are they that have heard the word,</font>
40:4:19: <font color="#CC0000">and the cares of the world, and the deceitfulness of riches, and the lusts of other things entering in, choke the word, and it becometh unfruitful.</font>
40:4:20: <font color="#CC0000">And those are they that were sown upon the good ground; such as hear the word, and accept it, and bear fruit, thirtyfold, and sixtyfold, and a hundredfold.</font>
40:4:21: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Is the lamp brought to be put under the bushel, or under the bed, and not to be put on</font> the stand?
40:4:22: <font color="#CC0000">For there is nothing hid, save that it should be manifested; neither was anything made secret, but that it should come to light.</font>
40:4:23: <font color="#CC0000">If any man hath ears to hear, let him hear.</font>
40:4:24: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Take heed what ye hear: with what measure ye mete it shall be measured unto you; and more shall be given unto you</font>.
40:4:25: <font color="#CC0000">For he that hath, to him shall be given: and he that hath not, from him shall be taken away even that which he hath.</font>
40:4:26: And he said, <font color="#CC0000">So is the kingdom of God, as if a man should cast seed upon the</font> earth;
40:4:27: <font color="#CC0000">and should sleep and rise night and day, and the seed should spring up and grow, he knoweth not how.</font>
40:4:28: <font color="#CC0000">The earth beareth fruit of herself; first the blade, then the ear, then the full grain in the ear.</font>
40:4:29: <font color="#CC0000">But when the fruit is ripe, straightway he putteth forth the sickle, because the harvest is come.</font>
40:4:30: And he said, How <font color="#CC0000">shall we liken the kingdom of God? or in what parable shall we set it</font> forth?
40:4:31: <font color="#CC0000">It is like a grain of mustard seed, which, when it is sown upon the earth, though it be less than all the seeds that are upon the earth,</font>
40:4:32: <font color="#CC0000">yet when it is sown, groweth up, and becometh greater than all the herbs, and putteth out great branches; so that the birds of the heaven can lodge under the shadow thereof.</font>
40:4:35: And on that day, when even was come, he saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Let us go over unto the other side</font>.
40:4:39: And he awoke, and rebuked the wind, and said unto the sea, <font color="#CC0000">Peace, be still</font>. And the wind ceased, and there was a great calm.
40:4:40: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Why are ye fearful? have ye not yet faith</font>?
40:5:8: For he said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Come forth, thou unclean spirit, out of the man</font>.
40:5:9: And he asked him, <font color="#CC0000">What is thy name</font>? And he saith unto him, My name is Legion; for we are many.
40:5:19: And he suffered him not, but saith unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Go to thy house unto thy friends, and tell them how great things the Lord hath done for thee, and how he had mercy on thee</font>.
40:5:30: And straightway Jesus, perceiving in himself that the power proceeding from him had gone forth, turned him about in the crowd, and said, <font color="#CC0000">Who touched my</font> garments?
40:5:34: And he said unto her, <font color="#CC0000">Daughter, thy faith hath made thee whole; go in peace, and be whole of thy plague</font>.
40:5:36: But Jesus, <font color="#CC0000">not heeding the word spoken, saith unto the ruler of the synagogue, Fear not, only believe</font>.
40:5:39: And when he was entered in, he saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Why make ye a tumult, and weep? the child is not dead, but sleepeth</font>.
40:5:41: And taking the child by the hand, he saith unto her, <font color="#CC0000">Talitha cumi</font>; which is, being interpreted, Damsel, I say unto thee, Arise.
40:6:4: And Jesus said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">A prophet is not without honor, save in his own country, and among his own kin, and in his own house</font>.
40:6:10: And he said unto them, Wheresoever <font color="#CC0000">ye enter into a house, there abide till ye depart</font> thence.
40:6:11: <font color="#CC0000">And whatsoever place shall not receive you, and they hear you not, as ye go forth thence, shake off the dust that is under your feet for a testimony unto them.</font>
40:6:31: And he saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Come ye yourselves apart into a desert place, and rest a while</font>. For there were many coming and going, and they had no leisure so much as to eat.
40:6:37: But he answered and said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Give ye them to eat</font>. And they say unto him, Shall we go and buy two hundred shillings’ worth of bread, and give them to eat?
40:6:38: And he saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">How many loaves have ye? go and see</font>. And when they knew, they say, Five, and two fishes.
40:6:50: for they all saw him, and were troubled. But he straightway spake with them, and saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Be of good cheer: it is I; be not afraid</font>.
40:7:6: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Well did Isaiah prophesy of you hypocrites, as it is written,  This people honoreth me with their lips, But their heart is far from me</font>.
40:7:7: <font color="#CC0000">But in vain do they worship me, Teachingas theirdoctrines the precepts of men.</font>
40:7:8: <font color="#CC0000">Ye leave the commandment of God, and hold fast the tradition of men.</font>
40:7:9: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Full well do ye reject the commandment of God, that ye may keep your tradition</font>.
40:7:10: <font color="#CC0000">For Moses said, Honor thy father and thy mother; and, He that speaketh evil of father or mother, let him die the death:</font>
40:7:11: <font color="#CC0000">but ye say, If a man shall say to his father or his mother, That wherewith thou mightest have been profited by me is Corban, that is to say, Given to God;</font>
40:7:12: <font color="#CC0000">ye no longer suffer him to do aught for his father or his mother;</font>
40:7:13: <font color="#CC0000">making void the word of God by your tradition, which ye have delivered: and many such like things ye do.</font>
40:7:14: And he called to him the multitude again, and said <font color="#CC0000">unto them, Hear me all of you, and understand</font>:
40:7:15: <font color="#CC0000">there is nothing from without the man, that going into him can defile him; but the things which proceed out of the man are those that defile the man.</font>
40:7:18: And he saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Are ye so without understanding also? Perceive ye not, that whatsoever from without goeth into the man, it cannot defile him</font>;
40:7:19: <font color="#CC0000">because it goeth not into his heart, but into his belly, and goeth out into the draught? This he said, making all meats clean.</font>
40:7:20: And he said, <font color="#CC0000">That which proceedeth out of the man, that defileth the man</font>.
40:7:21: <font color="#CC0000">For from within, out of the heart of men, evil thoughts proceed, fornications, thefts, murders, adulteries,</font>
40:7:22: <font color="#CC0000">covetings, wickednesses, deceit, lasciviousness, an evil eye, railing, pride, foolishness:</font>
40:7:23: <font color="#CC0000">all these evil things proceed from within, and defile the man.</font>
40:7:27: And he said unto her, <font color="#CC0000">Let the children first be filled: for it is not meet to take the children’s bread and cast it to the dogs</font>.
40:7:29: And he said unto her, <font color="#CC0000">For this saying go thy way; the demon is gone out of thy daughter</font>.
40:7:34: and looking up to heaven, he sighed, and saith unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Ephphatha</font>, that is, Be opened.
40:8:2: <font color="#CC0000">I have compassion on the multitude, because they continue with me now three days, and have nothing to eat:</font>
40:8:3: <font color="#CC0000">and if I send them away fasting to their home, they will faint on the way; and some of them are come from far.</font>
40:8:5: And he asked them, <font color="#CC0000">How many loaves have ye</font>? And they said, Seven.
40:8:12: And he sighed deeply in his spirit, and saith, <font color="#CC0000">Why doth this generation seek a sign? verily I say unto you, There shall no sign be given unto this generation</font>.
40:8:15: And he charged them, saying, <font color="#CC0000">Take heed, beware of the leaven of the Pharisees and the leaven of Herod</font>.
40:8:17: And Jesus perceiving it saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Why reason ye, because ye have no bread? do ye not yet perceive, neither understand? have ye your heart hardened</font>?
40:8:18: <font color="#CC0000">Having eyes, see ye not? and having ears, hear ye not? and do ye not remember?</font>
40:8:19: <font color="#CC0000">When I brake the five loaves among the five thousand, how many baskets full of broken pieces took ye up</font>? They say unto him, Twelve.
40:8:20: <font color="#CC0000">And when the seven among the four thousand, how many basketfuls of broken pieces took ye up</font>? And they say unto him, Seven.
40:8:21: <font color="#CC0000">And he said unto them, Do ye not yet understand?</font>
40:8:26: <font color="#CC0000">And he sent him away to his home, saying, Do not even enter into the village.</font>
40:8:27: And Jesus went forth, and his disciples, into the villages of Cæsarea Philippi: and on the way he asked his disciples, saying unto them, Who <font color="#CC0000">do men say that I am</font>?
40:8:29: And he asked them, <font color="#CC0000">But who say ye that I am</font>? Peter answereth and saith unto him, Thou art the Christ.
40:8:33: But he turning about, and seeing his disciples, rebuked Peter, and saith, <font color="#CC0000">Get thee behind me, Satan; for thou mindest not the things of God, but the things of men</font>.
40:8:34: And he called unto him the multitude with his disciples, and said unto them, If any man would <font color="#CC0000">come after me, let him deny himself, and take up his cross, and follow me</font>.
40:8:35: <font color="#CC0000">For whosoever would save his life shall lose it; and whosoever shall lose his life for my sake and the gospel’s shall save it.</font>
40:8:36: <font color="#CC0000">For what doth it profit a man, to gain the whole world, and forfeit his life?</font>
40:8:37: <font color="#CC0000">For what should a man give in exchange for his life?</font>
40:8:38: <font color="#CC0000">For whosoever shall be ashamed of me and of my words in this adulterous and sinful generation, the Son of man also shall be ashamed of him, when he cometh in the glory of his Father with the holy angels.</font>
40:9:1: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Verily I say unto you, There are some here of them that stand by, who shall in no wise taste of death, till they see the kingdom of God come with power</font>.
40:9:12: And he said unto them, Elijah indeed <font color="#CC0000">cometh first, and restoreth all things: and how is it written of the Son of man, that he should suffer many things and be set at nought</font>?
40:9:13: <font color="#CC0000">But I say unto you, that Elijah is come, and they have also done unto him whatsoever they would, even as it is written of him.</font>
40:9:16: And he asked them, <font color="#CC0000">What question ye with them</font>?
40:9:19: And he answereth them and saith, <font color="#CC0000">O faithless generation, how long shall I be with you? how long shall I bear with you? bring him unto me</font>.
40:9:21: And he asked his father, <font color="#CC0000">How long time is it since this hath come unto him</font>? And he said, From a child.
40:9:23: And Jesus said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">If thou canst! All things are possible to him that believeth</font>.
40:9:25: And when Jesus saw that a multitude came running together, he rebuked the unclean spirit, saying unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Thou dumb and deaf spirit, I command thee, come out of him, and enter no more into him</font>.
40:9:29: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">This kind can come out by nothing, save by prayer</font>.
40:9:31: For he taught his disciples, and said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">The Son of man is delivered up into the hands of men, and they shall kill him; and when he is killed, after three days he shall rise</font> again.
40:9:33: <font color="#CC0000">And they came to Capernaum: and when he was in the house he asked them, What were ye reasoning on the way?</font>
40:9:35: And he sat down, and called the twelve; and he saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">If any man would be first, he shall be last of all, and servant of all</font>.
40:9:37: <font color="#CC0000">Whosoever shall receive one of such little children in my name, receiveth me: and whosoever receiveth me, receiveth not me, but him that sent me.</font>
40:9:39: But Jesus said, <font color="#CC0000">Forbid him not: for there is no man who shall do a mighty work in my name, and be able quickly to speak evil of me</font>.
40:9:40: <font color="#CC0000">For he that is not against us is for us.</font>
40:9:41: <font color="#CC0000">For whosoever shall give you a cup of water to drink, because ye are Christ’s, verily I say unto you, he shall in no wise lose his reward.</font>
40:9:42: <font color="#CC0000">And whosoever shall cause one of these little ones that believe on me to stumble, it were better for him if a great millstone were hanged about his neck, and he were cast into the sea.</font>
40:9:43: <font color="#CC0000">And if thy hand cause thee to stumble, cut it off: it is good for thee to enter into life maimed, rather than having thy two hands to go into hell, into the unquenchable fire.</font>
40:9:45: <font color="#CC0000">And if thy foot cause thee to stumble, cut it off: it is good for thee to enter into life halt, rather than having thy two feet to be cast into hell.</font>
40:9:47: <font color="#CC0000">And if thine eye cause thee to stumble, cast it out: it is good for thee to enter into the kingdom of God with one eye, rather than having two eyes to be cast into hell;</font>
40:9:48: <font color="#CC0000">where their worm dieth not, and the fire is not quenched.</font>
40:9:49: <font color="#CC0000">For every one shall be salted with fire.</font>
40:9:50: <font color="#CC0000">Salt is good: but if the salt have lost its saltness, wherewith will ye season it? Have salt in yourselves, and be at peace one with another.</font>
40:10:3: And he answered and said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">What did Moses command you</font>?
40:10:5: But Jesus said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">For your hardness of heart he wrote you this</font> commandment.
40:10:6: <font color="#CC0000">But from the beginning of the creation, Male and female made he them.</font>
40:10:7: <font color="#CC0000">For this cause shall a man leave his father and mother, and shall cleave to his wife;</font>
40:10:8: <font color="#CC0000">and the two shall become one flesh: so that they are no more two, but one flesh.</font>
40:10:9: <font color="#CC0000">What therefore God hath joined together, let not man put asunder.</font>
40:10:11: And he saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Whosoever shall put away his wife, and marry another, committeth adultery against her</font>:
40:10:12: <font color="#CC0000">and if she herself shall put away her husband, and marry another, she committeth adultery.</font>
40:10:14: But when Jesus saw it, he was moved with indignation, and said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Suffer the little children to come unto me; forbid them not: for to such belongeth the kingdom of God</font>.
40:10:15: <font color="#CC0000">Verily I say unto you, Whosoever shall not receive the kingdom of God as a little child, he shall in no wise enter therein.</font>
40:10:18: And Jesus said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Why callest thou me good? none is good save one, even God</font>.
40:10:19: <font color="#CC0000">Thou knowest the commandments, Do not kill, Do not commit adultery, Do not steal, Do not bear false witness, Do not defraud, Honor thy father and mother.</font>
40:10:21: And Jesus looking upon him loved him, and said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">One thing thou lackest: go, sell whatsoever thou hast, and give to the poor, and thou shalt have treasure in heaven: and come, follow me</font>.
40:10:23: And Jesus looked round about, and saith unto his disciples, <font color="#CC0000">How hardly shall they that have riches enter into the kingdom of God</font>!
40:10:24: And the disciples were amazed at his words. But Jesus answereth again, and saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Children, how hard is it for them that trust in riches to enter into the kingdom of God</font>!
40:10:25: <font color="#CC0000">It is easier for a camel to go through a needle’s eye, than for a rich man to enter into the kingdom of God.</font>
40:10:27: Jesus looking upon them saith, <font color="#CC0000">With men it is impossible, but not with God: for all things are possible</font> with God.
40:10:29: Jesus said, <font color="#CC0000">Verily I say unto you, There is no man that hath left house, or brethren, or sisters, or mother, or father, or children, or lands, for my sake, and for the</font> gospel’s sake,
40:10:30: <font color="#CC0000">but he shall receive a hundredfold now in this time, houses, and brethren, and sisters, and mothers, and children, and lands, with persecutions; and in the world to come eternal life.</font>
40:10:31: <font color="#CC0000">But many that are first shall be last; and the last first.</font>
40:10:33: saying, <font color="#CC0000">Behold, we go up to Jerusalem; and the Son of man shall be delivered unto the chief priests and the scribes; and they shall condemn him to death, and shall deliver him unto the Gentiles</font>:
40:10:34: <font color="#CC0000">and they shall mock him, and shall spit upon him, and shall scourge him, and shall kill him; and after three days he shall rise again.</font>
40:10:36: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">What would ye that I should do for you</font>?
40:10:38: But Jesus said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Ye know not what ye ask. Are ye able to drink the cup that I drink? or to be baptized with the baptism that I am baptized with</font>?
40:10:39: And they said unto him, We are able. And Jesus said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">The cup that I drink ye shall drink; and with the baptism that I am baptized withal shall ye be baptized</font>:
40:10:40: <font color="#CC0000">but to sit on my right hand or on my left hand is not mine to give; but it is for them for whom it hath been prepared.</font>
40:10:42: And Jesus called them to him, and saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Ye know that they who are accounted to rule over the Gentiles lord it over them; and their great ones exercise authority over them</font>.
40:10:43: <font color="#CC0000">But it is not so among you: but whosoever would become great among you, shall be your minister;</font>
40:10:44: <font color="#CC0000">and whosoever would be first among you, shall be servant of all.</font>
40:10:45: <font color="#CC0000">For the Son of man also came not to be ministered unto, but to minister, and to give his life a ransom for many.</font>
40:10:51: And Jesus answered him, and said, <font color="#CC0000">What wilt thou that I should do unto thee</font>? And the blind man said unto him, Rabboni, that I may receive my sight.
40:10:52: And Jesus said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Go thy way; thy faith hath made thee whole</font>. And straightway he received his sight, and followed him in the way.
40:11:2: and saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Go your way into the village that is over against you: and straightway as ye enter into it, ye shall find a colt tied, whereon no man ever yet sat; loose him, and bring him</font>.
40:11:3: <font color="#CC0000">And if any one say unto you, Why do ye this? say ye, The Lord hath need of him; and straightway he will send him back hither.</font>
40:11:14: And he answered and said unto it, <font color="#CC0000">No man eat fruit from thee henceforward for ever</font>. And his disciples heard it.
40:11:17: And he taught, and said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Is it not written, My house shall be called a house of prayer for all the nations? but ye have made it a den of</font> robbers.
40:11:22: And Jesus answering saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Have faith in God</font>.
40:11:23: <font color="#CC0000">Verily I say unto you, Whosoever shall say unto this mountain, Be thou taken up and cast into the sea; and shall not doubt in his heart, but shall believe that what he saith cometh to pass; he shall have it.</font>
40:11:24: <font color="#CC0000">Therefore I say unto you, All things whatsoever ye pray and ask for, believe that ye receive them, and ye shall have them.</font>
40:11:25: <font color="#CC0000">And whensoever ye stand praying, forgive, if ye have aught against any one; that your Father also who is in heaven may forgive you your trespasses.</font>
40:11:29: And Jesus said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">I will ask of you one question, and answer me, and I will tell you by what authority I do these things</font>.
40:11:30: <font color="#CC0000">The baptism of John, was it from heaven, or from men? answer me.</font>
40:11:33: And they answered Jesus and say, We know not. And Jesus saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Neither tell I you by what authority I do these things</font>.
40:12:1: And he began to speak unto them in parables. <font color="#CC0000">A man planted a vineyard, and set a hedge about it, and digged a pit for the winepress, and built a tower, and let it out to husbandmen, and went into another country</font>.
40:12:2: <font color="#CC0000">And at the season he sent to the husbandmen a servant, that he might receive from the husbandmen of the fruits of the vineyard.</font>
40:12:3: <font color="#CC0000">And they took him, and beat him, and sent him away empty.</font>
40:12:4: <font color="#CC0000">And again he sent unto them another servant; and him they wounded in the head, and handled shamefully.</font>
40:12:5: <font color="#CC0000">And he sent another; and him they killed: and many others; beating some, and killing some.</font>
40:12:6: <font color="#CC0000">He had yet one, a beloved son: he sent him last unto them, saying, They will reverence my son.</font>
40:12:7: <font color="#CC0000">But those husbandmen said among themselves, This is the heir; come, let us kill him, and the inheritance shall be ours.</font>
40:12:8: <font color="#CC0000">And they took him, and killed him, and cast him forth out of the vineyard.</font>
40:12:9: <font color="#CC0000">What therefore will the lord of the vineyard do? he will come and destroy the husbandmen, and will give the vineyard unto others.</font>
40:12:10: <font color="#CC0000">Have ye not read even this scripture:  The stone which the builders rejected, The same was made the head of the corner;</font>
40:12:11: <font color="#CC0000">This was from the Lord, And it is marvellous in our eyes?</font>
40:12:15: Shall we give, or shall we not give? But he, knowing their hypocrisy, said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Why make ye trial of me? bring me a denarius, that I may see it</font>.
40:12:16: And they brought it. And he saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Whose is this image and superscription</font>? And they said unto him, Cæsar’s.
40:12:17: And Jesus said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Render unto Cæsar the things that are Cæsar’s, and unto God the things that are</font> God’s. And they marvelled greatly at him.
40:12:24: Jesus said unto them, Is it not for this cause that <font color="#CC0000">ye err, that ye know not the scriptures, nor the power of God</font>?
40:12:25: <font color="#CC0000">For when they shall rise from the dead, they neither marry, nor are given in marriage; but are as angels in heaven.</font>
40:12:26: <font color="#CC0000">But as touching the dead, that they are raised; have ye not read in the book of Moses, in the place concerning the Bush, how God spake unto him, saying, I am the God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob?</font>
40:12:27: <font color="#CC0000">He is not the God of the dead, but of the living: ye do greatly err.</font>
40:12:29: Jesus answered, <font color="#CC0000">The first is, Hear, O Israel; The Lord our God, the Lord is one</font>:
40:12:30: <font color="#CC0000">and thou shalt love the Lord thy God with all thy heart, and with all thy soul, and with all thy mind, and with all thy strength.</font>
40:12:31: <font color="#CC0000">The second is this, Thou shalt love thy neighbor as thyself. There is none other commandment greater than these.</font>
40:12:34: And when Jesus saw that he answered discreetly, he said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Thou art not far from the kingdom of God</font>. And no man after that durst ask him any question.
40:12:35: And Jesus answered and said, as he taught in the temple, <font color="#CC0000">How say the scribes that the Christ is the son of David</font>?
40:12:36: <font color="#CC0000">David himself said in the Holy Spirit,  The Lord said unto my Lord, Sit thou on my right hand, Till I make thine enemies the footstool of thy feet.</font>
40:12:37: <font color="#CC0000">David himself calleth him Lord; and whence is he his son? And the common people heard him gladly.</font>
40:12:38: And in his teaching he said, <font color="#CC0000">Beware of the scribes, who desire to walk in long robes, andto have salutations in the marketplaces</font>,
40:12:39: <font color="#CC0000">and chief seats in the synagogues, and chief places at feasts:</font>
40:12:40: <font color="#CC0000">they that devour widows’ houses, and for a pretence make long prayers; these shall receive greater condemnation.</font>
40:12:43: And he called unto him his disciples, and said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Verily I say unto you, This poor widow cast in more than all they that are casting into the treasury</font>:
40:12:44: <font color="#CC0000">for they all did cast in of their superfluity; but she of her want did cast in all that she had, even all her living.</font>
40:13:2: And Jesus said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Seest thou these great buildings? there shall not be left here one stone upon another, which shall not be thrown down</font>.
40:13:5: And Jesus began to say unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Take heed that no man lead you</font> astray.
40:13:6: <font color="#CC0000">Many shall come in my name, saying, I am he; and shall lead many astray.</font>
40:13:7: <font color="#CC0000">And when ye shall hear of wars and rumors of wars, be not troubled: these things must needs come to pass; but the end is not yet.</font>
40:13:8: <font color="#CC0000">For nation shall rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom; there shall be earthquakes in divers places; there shall be famines: these things are the beginning of travail.</font>
40:13:9: <font color="#CC0000">But take ye heed to yourselves: for they shall deliver you up to councils; and in synagogues shall ye be beaten; and before governors and kings shall ye stand for my sake, for a testimony unto them.</font>
40:13:10: <font color="#CC0000">And the gospel must first be preached unto all the nations.</font>
40:13:11: <font color="#CC0000">And when they lead you to judgment, and deliver you up, be not anxious beforehand what ye shall speak: but whatsoever shall be given you in that hour, that speak ye; for it is not ye that speak, but the Holy Spirit.</font>
40:13:12: <font color="#CC0000">And brother shall deliver up brother to death, and the father his child; and children shall rise up against parents, and cause them to be put to death.</font>
40:13:13: <font color="#CC0000">And ye shall be hated of all men for my name’s sake: but he that endureth to the end, the same shall be saved.</font>
40:13:14: <font color="#CC0000">But when ye see the abomination of desolation standing where he ought not (let him that readeth understand), then let them that are in Judæa flee unto the mountains:</font>
40:13:15: <font color="#CC0000">and let him that is on the housetop not go down, nor enter in, to take anything out of his house:</font>
40:13:16: <font color="#CC0000">and let him that is in the field not return back to take his cloak.</font>
40:13:17: <font color="#CC0000">But woe unto them that are with child and to them that give suck in those days!</font>
40:13:18: <font color="#CC0000">And pray ye that it be not in the winter.</font>
40:13:19: <font color="#CC0000">For those days shall be tribulation, such as there hath not been the like from the beginning of the creation which God created until now, and never shall be.</font>
40:13:20: <font color="#CC0000">And except the Lord had shortened the days, no flesh would have been saved; but for the elect’s sake, whom he chose, he shortened the days.</font>
40:13:21: <font color="#CC0000">And then if any man shall say unto you, Lo, here is the Christ; or, Lo, there; believe it not:</font>
40:13:22: <font color="#CC0000">for there shall arise false Christs and false prophets, and shall show signs and wonders, that they may lead astray, if possible, the elect.</font>
40:13:23: <font color="#CC0000">But take ye heed: behold, I have told you all things beforehand.</font>

41:6:27: <font color="#CC0000">But I say unto you that hear, Love your enemies, do good to them that hate you,</font>
41:6:28: <font color="#CC0000">bless them that curse you, pray for them that despitefully use you.</font>

41:6:32: <font color="#CC0000">And if ye love them that love you, what thank have ye? for even sinners love those that love them.</font>
41:6:33: <font color="#CC0000">And if ye do good to them that do good to you, what thank have ye? for even sinners do the same.</font>

41:6:35: <font color="#CC0000">But love your enemies, and do them good, and lend, never despairing; and your reward shall be great, and ye shall be sons of the Most High: for he is kind toward the unthankful and evil.</font>

41:6:42: <font color="#CC0000">Or how canst thou say to thy brother, Brother, let me cast out the mote that is in thine eye, when thou thyself beholdest not the beam that is in thine own eye?
