41:9:18: And it came to pass, as he was praying apart, the disciples were with him: and he asked them, saying, Who do the multitudes <font color="#CC0000">say that I am</font>?
41:9:20: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">But who say ye that I am</font>? And Peter answering said, The Christ of God.
41:9:22: saying, <font color="#CC0000">The Son of man must suffer many things, and be rejected of the elders and chief priests and scribes, and be killed, and the third day</font> be raised up.
41:9:23: And he said unto all, <font color="#CC0000">If any man would come after me, let him deny himself, and take up his cross daily, and follow me</font>.
41:9:24: <font color="#CC0000">For whosoever would save his life shall lose it; but whosoever shall lose his life for my sake, the same shall save it.</font>
41:9:25: <font color="#CC0000">For what is a man profited, if he gain the whole world, and lose or forfeit his own self?</font>
41:9:26: <font color="#CC0000">For whosoever shall be ashamed of me and of my words, of him shall the Son of man be ashamed, when he cometh in his own glory, and the glory of the Father, and of the holy angels.</font>
41:9:27: <font color="#CC0000">But I tell you of a truth, There are some of them that stand here, who shall in no wise taste of death, till they see the kingdom of God.</font>
41:9:41: And Jesus answered and said, <font color="#CC0000">O faithless and perverse generation, how long shall I be with you, and bear with you? bring hither thy son</font>.
41:9:44: <font color="#CC0000">Let these words sink into your ears: for the Son of man shall be delivered up into the hands of men.</font>
41:9:48: and said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Whosoever shall receive this little child in my name receiveth me: and whosoever shall receive me receiveth him that sent me: for he that is least among you all, the same is great</font>.
41:9:50: But Jesus said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Forbid him not: for he that is not against you is for</font> you.
41:9:55: <font color="#CC0000">But he turned, and rebuked them.</font>
41:9:56: <font color="#CC0000">And they went to another village.</font>
41:9:58: And Jesus said unto him, The <font color="#CC0000">foxes have holes, and the birds of the heaven have nests; but the Son of man hath not where to lay his head</font>.
41:9:59: And he said unto another, <font color="#CC0000">Follow me</font>. But he said, Lord, suffer me first to go and bury my father.
41:9:60: But he said unto him, Leave <font color="#CC0000">the dead to bury their own dead; but go thou and publish abroad the kingdom of God</font>.
41:9:62: But Jesus said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">No man, having put his hand to the plow, and looking back, is fit for the kingdom of God</font>.
41:10:2: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">The harvest indeed is plenteous, but the laborers are few: pray ye therefore the Lord of the harvest, that he send forth laborers into his harvest</font>.
41:10:3: <font color="#CC0000">Go your ways; behold, I send you forth as lambs in the midst of wolves.</font>
41:10:4: <font color="#CC0000">Carry no purse, no wallet, no shoes; and salute no man on the way.</font>
41:10:5: <font color="#CC0000">And into whatsoever house ye shall enter, first say, Peace be to this house.</font>
41:10:6: <font color="#CC0000">And if a son of peace be there, your peace shall rest upon him: but if not, it shall turn to you again.</font>
41:10:7: <font color="#CC0000">And in that same house remain, eating and drinking such things as they give: for the laborer is worthy of his hire. Go not from house to house.</font>
41:10:8: <font color="#CC0000">And into whatsoever city ye enter, and they receive you, eat such things as are set before you:</font>
41:10:9: <font color="#CC0000">and heal the sick that are therein, and say unto them, The kingdom of God is come nigh unto you.</font>
41:10:10: <font color="#CC0000">But into whatsoever city ye shall enter, and they receive you not, go out into the streets thereof and say,</font>
41:10:11: <font color="#CC0000">Even the dust from your city, that cleaveth to our feet, we wipe off against you: nevertheless know this, that the kingdom of God is come nigh.</font>
41:10:12: <font color="#CC0000">I say unto you, It shall be more tolerable in that day for Sodom, than for that city.</font>
41:10:13: <font color="#CC0000">Woe unto thee, Chorazin! woe unto thee, Bethsaida! for if the mighty works had been done in Tyre and Sidon, which were done in you, they would have repented long ago, sitting in sackcloth and ashes.</font>
41:10:14: <font color="#CC0000">But it shall be more tolerable for Tyre and Sidon in the judgment, than for you.</font>
41:10:15: <font color="#CC0000">And thou, Capernaum, shalt thou be exalted unto heaven? thou shalt be brought down unto Hades.</font>
41:10:16: <font color="#CC0000">He that heareth you heareth me; and he that rejecteth you rejecteth me; and he that rejecteth me rejecteth him that sent me.</font>
41:10:18: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">I beheld Satan fallen as lightning from heaven</font>.
41:10:19: <font color="#CC0000">Behold, I have given you authority to tread upon serpents and scorpions, and over all the power of the enemy: and nothing shall in any wise hurt you.</font>
41:10:20: <font color="#CC0000">Nevertheless in this rejoice not, that the spirits are subject unto you; but rejoice that your names are written in heaven.</font>
41:10:21: In that same hour he rejoiced in the Holy Spirit, and said, <font color="#CC0000">I thank thee, O Father, Lord of heaven and earth, that thou didst hide these things from the wise and understanding, and didst reveal them unto babes: yea, Father; for so it was well-pleasing in thy sight</font>.
41:10:22: <font color="#CC0000">All things have been delivered unto me of my Father: and no one knoweth who the Son is, save the Father; and who the Father is, save the Son, and he to whomsoever the Son willeth to reveal him.</font>
41:10:23: And turning to the disciples, he said privately, <font color="#CC0000">Blessed are the eyes which see the things that ye see</font>:
41:10:24: <font color="#CC0000">for I say unto you, that many prophets and kings desired to see the things which ye see, and saw them not; and to hear the things which ye hear, and heard them not.</font>
41:10:26: And he said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">What is written in the law? how readest thou</font>?
41:10:28: And he said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Thou hast answered right: this do, and thou shalt live</font>.
41:10:30: Jesus made answer and said, <font color="#CC0000">A certain man was going down from Jerusalem to Jericho; and he fell among robbers, who both stripped him and beat him, and departed, leaving him half dead</font>.
41:10:31: <font color="#CC0000">And by chance a certain priest was going down that way: and when he saw him, he passed by on the other side.</font>
41:10:32: <font color="#CC0000">And in like manner a Levite also, when he came to the place, and saw him, passed by on the other side.</font>
41:10:33: <font color="#CC0000">But a certain Samaritan, as he journeyed, came where he was: and when he saw him, he was moved with compassion,</font>
41:10:34: <font color="#CC0000">and came to him, and bound up his wounds, pouring on them oil and wine; and he set him on his own beast, and brought him to an inn, and took care of him.</font>
41:10:35: <font color="#CC0000">And on the morrow he took out two shillings, and gave them to the host, and said, Take care of him; and whatsoever thou spendest more, I, when I come back again, will repay thee.</font>
41:10:36: <font color="#CC0000">Which of these three, thinkest thou, proved neighbor unto him that fell among the robbers?</font>
41:10:37: And he said, He that showed mercy on him. And Jesus said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Go, and do thou likewise</font>.
41:10:41: But the Lord answered and said unto her, <font color="#CC0000">Martha, Martha, thou art anxious and troubled about many things</font>:
41:10:42: <font color="#CC0000">but one thing is needful: for Mary hath chosen the good part, which shall not be taken away from her.</font>
41:11:2: <font color="#CC0000">And he said unto them, When ye pray, say, Father, Hallowed be thy name. Thy kingdom come.</font>
41:11:3: <font color="#CC0000">Give us day by day our daily bread.</font>
41:11:4: <font color="#CC0000">And forgive us our sins; for we ourselves also forgive every one that is indebted to us. And bring us not into temptation.</font>
41:11:5: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Which of you shall have a friend, and shall go unto him at midnight, and say to him, Friend, lend me three loaves</font>;
41:11:6: <font color="#CC0000">for a friend of mine is come to me from a journey, and I have nothing to set before him;</font>
41:11:7: <font color="#CC0000">and he from within shall answer and say, Trouble me not: the door is now shut, and my children are with me in bed; I cannot rise and give thee?</font>
41:11:8: <font color="#CC0000">I say unto you, Though he will not rise and give him because he is his friend, yet because of his importunity he will arise and give him as many as he needeth.</font>
41:11:9: <font color="#CC0000">And I say unto you, Ask, and it shall be given you; seek, and ye shall find; knock, and it shall be opened unto you.</font>
41:11:10: <font color="#CC0000">For every one that asketh receiveth; and he that seeketh findeth; and to him that knocketh it shall be opened.</font>
41:11:11: <font color="#CC0000">And of which of you that is a father shall his son ask a loaf, and he give him a stone? or a fish, and he for a fish give him a serpent?</font>
41:11:12: <font color="#CC0000">Or if he shall ask an egg, will he give him a scorpion?</font>
41:11:13: <font color="#CC0000">If ye then, being evil, know how to give good gifts unto your children, how much more shall your heavenly Father give the Holy Spirit to them that ask him?</font>
41:11:17: But he, knowing their thoughts, said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Every kingdom divided against itself is brought to desolation; and a house divided against a house falleth</font>.
41:11:18: <font color="#CC0000">And if Satan also is divided against himself, how shall his kingdom stand? because ye say that I cast out demons by Beelzebub.</font>
41:11:19: <font color="#CC0000">And if I by Beelzebub cast out demons, by whom do your sons cast them out? therefore shall they be your judges.</font>
41:11:20: <font color="#CC0000">But if I by the finger of God cast out demons, then is the kingdom of God come upon you.</font>
41:11:21: <font color="#CC0000">When the strong man fully armed guardeth his own court, his goods are in peace:</font>
41:11:22: <font color="#CC0000">but when a stronger than he shall come upon him, and overcome him, he taketh from him his whole armor wherein he trusted, and divideth his spoils.</font>
41:11:23: <font color="#CC0000">He that is not with me is against me; and he that gathereth not with me scattereth.</font>
41:11:24: <font color="#CC0000">The unclean spirit when he is gone out of the man, passeth through waterless places, seeking rest, and finding none, he saith, I will turn back unto my house whence I came out.</font>
41:11:25: <font color="#CC0000">And when he is come, he findeth it swept and garnished.</font>
41:11:26: <font color="#CC0000">Then goeth he, and taketh to him seven other spirits more evil than himself; and they enter in and dwell there: and the last state of that man becometh worse than the first.</font>
41:11:28: But he said, <font color="#CC0000">Yea rather, blessed are they that hear the word of God, and keep it</font>.
41:11:29: And when the multitudes were gathering together unto him, he began to say, <font color="#CC0000">This generation is an evil generation: it seeketh after a sign; and there shall no sign be given to it but the sign of</font> Jonah.
41:11:30: <font color="#CC0000">For even as Jonah became a sign unto the Ninevites, so shall also the Son of man be to this generation.</font>
41:11:31: <font color="#CC0000">The queen of the south shall rise up in the judgment with the men of this generation, and shall condemn them: for she came from the ends of the earth to hear the wisdom of Solomon; and behold, a greater than Solomon is here.</font>
41:11:32: <font color="#CC0000">The men of Nineveh shall stand up in the judgment with this generation, and shall condemn it: for they repented at the preaching of Jonah; and behold, a greater than Jonah is here.</font>
41:11:33: <font color="#CC0000">No man, when he hath lighted a lamp, putteth it in a cellar, neither under the bushel, but on the stand, that they which enter in may see the light.</font>
41:11:34: <font color="#CC0000">The lamp of thy body is thine eye: when thine eye is single, thy whole body also is full of light; but when it is evil, thy body also is full of darkness.</font>
41:11:35: <font color="#CC0000">Look therefore whether the light that is in thee be not darkness.</font>
41:11:36: <font color="#CC0000">If therefore thy whole body be full of light, having no part dark, it shall be wholly full of light, as when the lamp with its bright shining doth give thee light.</font>
41:11:39: And the Lord said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Now ye the Pharisees cleanse the outside of the cup and of the platter; but your inward part is full of extortion and wickedness</font>.
41:11:40: <font color="#CC0000">Ye foolish ones, did not he that made the outside make the inside also?</font>
41:11:41: <font color="#CC0000">But give for alms those things which are within; and behold, all things are clean unto you.</font>
41:11:42: <font color="#CC0000">But woe unto you Pharisees! for ye tithe mint and rue and every herb, and pass over justice and the love of God: but these ought ye to have done, and not to leave the other undone.</font>
41:11:43: <font color="#CC0000">Woe unto you Pharisees! for ye love the chief seats in the synagogues, and the salutations in the marketplaces.</font>
41:11:44: <font color="#CC0000">Woe unto you! for ye are as the tombs which appear not, and the men that walk over them know it not.</font>
41:11:46: And he said, <font color="#CC0000">Woe unto you lawyers also! for ye load men with burdens grievous to be borne, and ye yourselves touch not the burdens with one of your fingers</font>.
41:11:47: <font color="#CC0000">Woe unto you! for ye build the tombs of the prophets, and your fathers killed them.</font>
41:11:48: <font color="#CC0000">So ye are witnesses and consent unto the works of your fathers: for they killed them, and ye build their tombs.</font>
41:11:49: <font color="#CC0000">Therefore also said the wisdom of God, I will send unto them prophets and apostles; and some of them they shall kill and persecute;</font>
41:11:50: <font color="#CC0000">that the blood of all the prophets, which was shed from the foundation of the world, may be required of this generation;</font>
41:11:51: <font color="#CC0000">from the blood of Abel unto the blood of Zachariah, who perished between the altar and the sanctuary: yea, I say unto you, it shall be required of this generation.</font>
41:11:52: <font color="#CC0000">Woe unto you lawyers! for ye took away the key of knowledge: ye entered not in yourselves, and them that were entering in ye hindered.</font>
41:12:1: In the mean time, when the many thousands of the multitude were gathered together, insomuch that they trod one upon another, he began to say unto his disciples first of all, <font color="#CC0000">Beware ye of the leaven of the Pharisees, which is hypocrisy</font>.
41:12:2: <font color="#CC0000">But there is nothing covered up, that shall not be revealed; and hid, that shall not be known.</font>
41:12:3: <font color="#CC0000">Wherefore whatsoever ye have said in the darkness shall be heard in the light; and what ye have spoken in the ear in the inner chambers shall be proclaimed upon the housetops.</font>
41:12:4: <font color="#CC0000">And I say unto you my friends, Be not afraid of them that kill the body, and after that have no more that they can do.</font>
41:12:5: <font color="#CC0000">But I will warn you whom ye shall fear: Fear him, who after he hath killed hath power to cast into hell; yea, I say unto you, Fear him.</font>
41:12:6: <font color="#CC0000">Are not five sparrows sold for two pence? and not one of them is forgotten in the sight of God.</font>
41:12:7: <font color="#CC0000">But the very hairs of your head are all numbered. Fear not: ye are of more value than many sparrows.</font>
41:12:8: <font color="#CC0000">And I say unto you, Every one who shall confess me before men, him shall the Son of man also confess before the angels of God:</font>
41:12:9: <font color="#CC0000">but he that denieth me in the presence of men shall be denied in the presence of the angels of God.</font>
41:12:10: <font color="#CC0000">And every one who shall speak a word against the Son of man, it shall be forgiven him: but unto him that blasphemeth against the Holy Spirit it shall not be forgiven.</font>
41:12:11: <font color="#CC0000">And when they bring you before the synagogues, and the rulers, and the authorities, be not anxious how or what ye shall answer, or what ye shall say:</font>
41:12:12: <font color="#CC0000">for the Holy Spirit shall teach you in that very hour what ye ought to say.</font>
41:12:14: But he said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Man, who made me a judge or a divider over you</font>?
41:12:15: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Take heed, and keep yourselves from all covetousness: for a man’s life consisteth not in the abundance of the things which he possesseth</font>.
41:12:16: And he spake a parable unto them, saying, <font color="#CC0000">The ground of a certain rich man brought forth plentifully</font>:
41:12:17: <font color="#CC0000">and he reasoned within himself, saying, What shall I do, because I have not where to bestow my fruits?</font>
41:12:18: <font color="#CC0000">And he said, This will I do: I will pull down my barns, and build greater; and there will I bestow all my grain and my goods.</font>
41:12:19: <font color="#CC0000">And I will say to my soul, Soul, thou hast much goods laid up for many years; take thine ease, eat, drink, be merry.</font>
41:12:20: <font color="#CC0000">But God said unto him, Thou foolish one, this night is thy soul required of thee; and the things which thou hast prepared, whose shall they be?</font>
41:12:21: <font color="#CC0000">So is he that layeth up treasure for himself, and is not rich toward God.</font>
41:12:22: And he said unto his disciples, <font color="#CC0000">Therefore I say unto you, Be not anxious for your life, what ye shall eat; nor yet for your body, what ye shall put on</font>.
41:12:23: <font color="#CC0000">For the life is more than the food, and the body than the raiment.</font>
41:12:24: <font color="#CC0000">Consider the ravens, that they sow not, neither reap; which have no store-chamber nor barn; and God feedeth them: of how much more value are ye than the birds!</font>
41:12:25: <font color="#CC0000">And which of you by being anxious can add a cubit unto the measure of his life?</font>
41:12:26: <font color="#CC0000">If then ye are not able to do even that which is least, why are ye anxious concerning the rest?</font>
41:12:27: <font color="#CC0000">Consider the lilies, how they grow: they toil not, neither do they spin; yet I say unto you, Even Solomon in all his glory was not arrayed like one of these.</font>
41:12:28: <font color="#CC0000">But if God doth so clothe the grass in the field, which to-day is, and to-morrow is cast into the oven; how much more shall he clothe you, O ye of little faith?</font>
41:12:29: <font color="#CC0000">And seek not ye what ye shall eat, and what ye shall drink, neither be ye of doubtful mind.</font>
41:12:30: <font color="#CC0000">For all these things do the nations of the world seek after: but your Father knoweth that ye have need of these things.</font>
41:12:31: <font color="#CC0000">Yet seek ye his kingdom, and these things shall be added unto you.</font>
41:12:32: <font color="#CC0000">Fear not, little flock; for it is your Father’s good pleasure to give you the kingdom.</font>
41:12:33: <font color="#CC0000">Sell that which ye have, and give alms; make for yourselves purses which wax not old, a treasure in the heavens that faileth not, where no thief draweth near, neither moth destroyeth.</font>
41:12:34: <font color="#CC0000">For where your treasure is, there will your heart be also.</font>
41:12:35: <font color="#CC0000">Let your loins be girded about, and your lamps burning;</font>
41:12:36: <font color="#CC0000">and be ye yourselves like unto men looking for their lord, when he shall return from the marriage feast; that, when he cometh and knocketh, they may straightway open unto him.</font>
41:12:37: <font color="#CC0000">Blessed are those servants, whom the lord when he cometh shall find watching: verily I say unto you, that he shall gird himself, and make them sit down to meat, and shall come and serve them.</font>
41:12:38: <font color="#CC0000">And if he shall come in the second watch, and if in the third, and find them so, blessed are those servants.</font>
41:12:39: <font color="#CC0000">But know this, that if the master of the house had known in what hour the thief was coming, he would have watched, and not have left his house to be broken through.</font>
41:12:40: <font color="#CC0000">Be ye also ready: for in an hour that ye think not the Son of man cometh.</font>
41:12:42: And the Lord said, <font color="#CC0000">Who then is the faithful and wise steward, whom his lord shall set over his household, to give them their portion of food in due season</font>?
41:12:43: <font color="#CC0000">Blessed is that servant, whom his lord when he cometh shall find so doing.</font>
41:12:44: <font color="#CC0000">Of a truth I say unto you, that he will set him over all that he hath.</font>
41:12:45: <font color="#CC0000">But if that servant shall say in his heart, My lord delayeth his coming; and shall begin to beat the menservants and the maidservants, and to eat and drink, and to be drunken;</font>
41:12:46: <font color="#CC0000">the lord of that servant shall come in a day when he expecteth not, and in an hour when he knoweth not, and shall cut him asunder, and appoint his portion with the unfaithful.</font>
41:12:47: <font color="#CC0000">And that servant, who knew his lord’s will, and made not ready, nor did according to his will, shall be beaten with many stripes;</font>
41:12:48: <font color="#CC0000">but he that knew not, and did things worthy of stripes, shall be beaten with few stripes. And to whomsoever much is given, of him shall much be required: and to whom they commit much, of him will they ask the more.</font>
41:12:49: <font color="#CC0000">I came to cast fire upon the earth; and what do I desire, if it is already kindled?</font>
41:12:50: <font color="#CC0000">But I have a baptism to be baptized with; and how am I straitened till it be accomplished!</font>
41:12:51: <font color="#CC0000">Think ye that I am come to give peace in the earth? I tell you, Nay; but rather division:</font>
41:12:52: <font color="#CC0000">for there shall be from henceforth five in one house divided, three against two, and two against three.</font>
41:12:53: <font color="#CC0000">They shall be divided, father against son, and son against father; mother against daughter, and daughter against her mother; mother in law against her daughter in law, and daughter in law against her mother in law.</font>
41:12:54: And he said to the multitudes also, <font color="#CC0000">When ye see a cloud rising in the west, straightway ye say, There cometh a shower; and so it</font> cometh to pass.
41:12:55: <font color="#CC0000">And when ye see a south wind blowing, ye say, There will be a scorching heat; and it cometh to pass.</font>
41:12:56: <font color="#CC0000">Ye hypocrites, ye know how to interpret the face of the earth and the heaven; but how is it that ye know not how to interpret this time?</font>
41:12:57: <font color="#CC0000">And why even of yourselves judge ye not what is right?</font>
41:12:58: <font color="#CC0000">For as thou art going with thine adversary before the magistrate, on the way give diligence to be quit of him; lest haply he drag thee unto the judge, and the judge shall deliver thee to the officer, and the officer shall cast thee into prison.</font>
41:12:59: <font color="#CC0000">I say unto thee, Thou shalt by no means come out thence, till thou have paid the very last mite.</font>
41:13:2: And he answered and said unto them, Think <font color="#CC0000">ye that these Galilæans were sinners above all the Galilæans, because they have suffered these things</font>?
41:13:3: <font color="#CC0000">I tell you, Nay: but, except ye repent, ye shall all in like manner perish.</font>
41:13:4: <font color="#CC0000">Or those eighteen, upon whom the tower in Siloam fell, and killed them, think ye that they were offenders above all the men that dwell in Jerusalem?</font>
41:13:5: <font color="#CC0000">I tell you, Nay: but, except ye repent, ye shall all likewise perish.</font>
41:13:6: And he spake this parable; <font color="#CC0000">A certain man had a fig tree planted in his vineyard; and he came seeking fruit thereon, and found none</font>.
41:13:7: <font color="#CC0000">And he said unto the vinedresser, Behold, these three years I come seeking fruit on this fig tree, and find none: cut it down; why doth it also cumber the ground?</font>
41:13:8: <font color="#CC0000">And he answering saith unto him, Lord, let it alone this year also, till I shall dig about it, and dung it:</font>
41:13:9: <font color="#CC0000">and if it bear fruit thenceforth, well; but if not, thou shalt cut it down.</font>
41:13:12: And when Jesus saw her, he called her, and said to her, <font color="#CC0000">Woman, thou art loosed from thine infirmity</font>.
41:13:15: But the Lord answered him, and said, Ye hypocrites, <font color="#CC0000">doth not each one of you on the sabbath loose his ox or his ass from the stall, and lead him away to watering</font>?
41:13:16: <font color="#CC0000">And ought not this woman, being a daughter of Abraham, whom Satan had bound, lo, these eighteen years, to have been loosed from this bond on the day of the sabbath?</font>
41:13:18: He said therefore, <font color="#CC0000">Unto what is the kingdom of God like? and whereunto shall I liken it</font>?
41:13:19: <font color="#CC0000">It is like unto a grain of mustard seed, which a man took, and cast into his own garden; and it grew, and became a tree; and the birds of the heaven lodged in the branches thereof.</font>
41:13:20: And again he said, <font color="#CC0000">Whereunto shall I liken the kingdom of God</font>?
41:13:21: <font color="#CC0000">It is like unto leaven, which a woman took and hid in three measures of meal, till it was all leavened.</font>
41:13:24: <font color="#CC0000">Strive to enter in by the narrow door: for many, I say unto you, shall seek to enter in, and shall not be able.</font>
41:13:25: <font color="#CC0000">When once the master of the house is risen up, and hath shut to the door, and ye begin to stand without, and to knock at the door, saying, Lord, open to us; and he shall answer and say to you, I know you not whence ye are;</font>
41:13:26: <font color="#CC0000">then shall ye begin to say, We did eat and drink in thy presence, and thou didst teach in our streets;</font>
41:13:27: <font color="#CC0000">and he shall say, I tell you, I know not whence ye are; depart from me, all ye workers of iniquity.</font>
41:13:28: <font color="#CC0000">There shall be the weeping and the gnashing of teeth, when ye shall see Abraham, and Isaac, and Jacob, and all the prophets, in the kingdom of God, and yourselves cast forth without.</font>
41:13:29: <font color="#CC0000">And they shall come from the east and west, and from the north and south, and shall sit down in the kingdom of God.</font>
41:13:30: <font color="#CC0000">And behold, there are last who shall be first, and there are first who shall be last.</font>
41:13:32: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Go and say to that fox, Behold, I cast out demons and perform cures to-day and to-morrow, and the third day I am perfected</font>.
41:13:33: <font color="#CC0000">Nevertheless I must go on my way to-day and to-morrow and the day following: for it cannot be that a prophet perish out of Jerusalem.</font>
41:13:34: <font color="#CC0000">O Jerusalem, Jerusalem, that killeth the prophets, and stoneth them that are sent unto her! how often would I have gathered thy children together, even as a hen gathereth her own brood under her wings, and ye would not!</font>
41:13:35: <font color="#CC0000">Behold, your house is left unto you desolate: and I say unto you, Ye shall not see me, until ye shall say, Blessed is he that cometh in the name of the Lord.</font>
41:14:3: And Jesus answering spake unto the lawyers and Pharisees, saying, <font color="#CC0000">Is it lawful to heal on the sabbath</font>, or not?
41:14:5: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Which of you shall have an ass or an ox fallen into a well, and will not straightway draw him up on a sabbath day</font>?
41:14:8: <font color="#CC0000">When thou art bidden of any man to a marriage feast, sit not down in the chief seat; lest haply a more honorable man than thou be bidden of him,</font>
41:14:9: <font color="#CC0000">and he that bade thee and him shall come and say to thee, Give this man place; and then thou shalt begin with shame to take the lowest place.</font>
41:14:10: <font color="#CC0000">But when thou art bidden, go and sit down in the lowest place; that when he that hath bidden thee cometh, he may say to thee, Friend, go up higher: then shalt thou have glory in the presence of all that sit at meat with thee.</font>
41:14:11: <font color="#CC0000">For every one that exalteth himself shall be humbled; and he that humbleth himself shall be exalted.</font>
41:14:12: And he said to him also that had bidden him, <font color="#CC0000">When thou makest a dinner or a supper, call not thy friends, nor thy brethren, nor thy kinsmen, nor rich neighbors; lest haply they also bid thee again, and a recompense be made thee</font>.
41:14:13: <font color="#CC0000">But when thou makest a feast, bid the poor, the maimed, the lame, the blind:</font>
41:14:14: <font color="#CC0000">and thou shalt be blessed; because they have not wherewith to recompense thee: for thou shalt be recompensed in the resurrection of the just.</font>
41:14:16: But he said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">A certain man made a great supper; and he bade many</font>:
41:14:17: <font color="#CC0000">and he sent forth his servant at supper time to say to them that were bidden, Come; for all things are now ready.</font>
41:14:18: <font color="#CC0000">And they all with one consent began to make excuse. The first said unto him, I have bought a field, and I must needs go out and see it; I pray thee have me excused.</font>
41:14:19: <font color="#CC0000">And another said, I have bought five yoke of oxen, and I go to prove them; I pray thee have me excused.</font>
41:14:20: <font color="#CC0000">And another said, I have married a wife, and therefore I cannot come.</font>
41:14:21: <font color="#CC0000">And the servant came, and told his lord these things. Then the master of the house being angry said to his servant, Go out quickly into the streets and lanes of the city, and bring in hither the poor and maimed and blind and lame.</font>
41:14:22: <font color="#CC0000">And the servant said, Lord, what thou didst command is done, and yet there is room.</font>
41:14:23: <font color="#CC0000">And the lord said unto the servant, Go out into the highways and hedges, and constrain them to come in, that my house may be filled.</font>
41:14:24: <font color="#CC0000">For I say unto you, that none of those men that were bidden shall taste of my supper.</font>
41:14:26: <font color="#CC0000">If any man cometh unto me, and hateth not his own father, and mother, and wife, and children, and brethren, and sisters, yea, and his own life also, he cannot be my disciple.</font>
41:14:27: <font color="#CC0000">Whosoever doth not bear his own cross, and come after me, cannot be my disciple.</font>
41:14:28: <font color="#CC0000">For which of you, desiring to build a tower, doth not first sit down and count the cost, whether he have wherewith to complete it?</font>
41:14:29: <font color="#CC0000">Lest haply, when he hath laid a foundation, and is not able to finish, all that behold begin to mock him,</font>
41:14:30: <font color="#CC0000">saying, This man began to build, and was not able to finish.</font>
41:14:31: <font color="#CC0000">Or what king, as he goeth to encounter another king in war, will not sit down first and take counsel whether he is able with ten thousand to meet him that cometh against him with twenty thousand?</font>
41:14:32: <font color="#CC0000">Or else, while the other is yet a great way off, he sendeth an ambassage, and asketh conditions of peace.</font>
41:14:33: <font color="#CC0000">So therefore whosoever he be of you that renounceth not all that he hath, he cannot be my disciple.</font>
41:14:34: <font color="#CC0000">Salt therefore is good: but if even the salt have lost its savor, wherewith shall it be seasoned?</font>
41:14:35: <font color="#CC0000">It is fit neither for the land nor for the dunghill: men cast it out. He that hath ears to hear, let him hear.</font>
41:15:4: <font color="#CC0000">What man of you, having a hundred sheep, and having lost one of them, doth not leave the ninety and nine in the wilderness, and go after that which is lost, until he find it?</font>
41:15:5: <font color="#CC0000">And when he hath found it, he layeth it on his shoulders, rejoicing.</font>
41:15:6: <font color="#CC0000">And when he cometh home, he calleth together his friends and his neighbors, saying unto them, Rejoice with me, for I have found my sheep which was lost.</font>
41:15:7: <font color="#CC0000">I say unto you, that even so there shall be joy in heaven over one sinner that repenteth, more than over ninety and nine righteous persons, who need no repentance.</font>
41:15:8: <font color="#CC0000">Or what woman having ten pieces of silver, if she lose one piece, doth not light a lamp, and sweep the house, and seek diligently until she find it?</font>
41:15:9: <font color="#CC0000">And when she hath found it, she calleth together her friends and neighbors, saying, Rejoice with me, for I have found the piece which I had lost.</font>
41:15:10: <font color="#CC0000">Even so, I say unto you, there is joy in the presence of the angels of God over one sinner that repenteth.</font>
41:15:11: And he said, <font color="#CC0000">A certain man had two sons</font>:
41:15:12: <font color="#CC0000">and the younger of them said to his father, Father, give me the portion ofthy substance that falleth to me. And he divided unto them his living.</font>
41:15:13: <font color="#CC0000">And not many days after, the younger son gathered all together and took his journey into a far country; and there he wasted his substance with riotous living.</font>
41:15:14: <font color="#CC0000">And when he had spent all, there arose a mighty famine in that country; and he began to be in want.</font>
41:15:15: <font color="#CC0000">And he went and joined himself to one of the citizens of that country; and he sent him into his fields to feed swine.</font>
41:15:16: <font color="#CC0000">And he would fain have filled his belly with the husks that the swine did eat: and no man gave unto him.</font>
41:15:17: <font color="#CC0000">But when he came to himself he said, How many hired servants of my father’s have bread enough and to spare, and I perish here with hunger!</font>
41:15:18: <font color="#CC0000">I will arise and go to my father, and will say unto him, Father, I have sinned against heaven, and in thy sight:</font>
41:15:19: <font color="#CC0000">I am no more worthy to be called thy son: make me as one of thy hired servants.</font>
41:15:20: <font color="#CC0000">And he arose, and came to his father. But while he was yet afar off, his father saw him, and was moved with compassion, and ran, and fell on his neck, and kissed him.</font>
41:15:21: <font color="#CC0000">And the son said unto him, Father, I have sinned against heaven, and in thy sight: I am no more worthy to be called thy son.</font>
41:15:22: <font color="#CC0000">But the father said to his servants, Bring forth quickly the best robe, and put it on him; and put a ring on his hand, and shoes on his feet:</font>
41:15:23: <font color="#CC0000">and bring the fatted calf, and kill it, and let us eat, and make merry:</font>
41:15:24: <font color="#CC0000">for this my son was dead, and is alive again; he was lost, and is found. And they began to be merry.</font>
41:15:25: <font color="#CC0000">Now his elder son was in the field: and as he came and drew nigh to the house, he heard music and dancing.</font>
41:15:26: <font color="#CC0000">And he called to him one of the servants, and inquired what these things might be.</font>
41:15:27: <font color="#CC0000">And he said unto him, Thy brother is come; and thy father hath killed the fatted calf, because he hath received him safe and sound.</font>
41:15:28: <font color="#CC0000">But he was angry, and would not go in: and his father came out, and entreated him.</font>
41:15:29: <font color="#CC0000">But he answered and said to his father, Lo, these many years do I serve thee, and I never transgressed a commandment of thine; and yet thou never gavest me a kid, that I might make merry with my friends:</font>
41:15:30: <font color="#CC0000">but when this thy son came, who hath devoured thy living with harlots, thou killedst for him the fatted calf.</font>
41:15:31: <font color="#CC0000">And he said unto him, Son, thou art ever with me, and all that is mine is thine.</font>
41:15:32: <font color="#CC0000">But it was meet to make merry and be glad: for this thy brother was dead, and is alive again; and was lost, and is found.</font>
41:16:1: And he said also unto the disciples, <font color="#CC0000">There was a certain rich man, who had a steward; and the same was accused unto him that he was wasting his goods</font>.
41:16:2: <font color="#CC0000">And he called him, and said unto him, What is this that I hear of thee? render the account of thy stewardship; for thou canst be no longer steward.</font>
41:16:3: <font color="#CC0000">And the steward said within himself, What shall I do, seeing that my lord taketh away the stewardship from me? I have not strength to dig; to beg I am ashamed.</font>
41:16:4: <font color="#CC0000">I am resolved what to do, that, when I am put out of the stewardship, they may receive me into their houses.</font>
41:16:5: <font color="#CC0000">And calling to him each one of his lord’s debtors, he said to the first, How much owest thou unto my lord?</font>
41:16:6: <font color="#CC0000">And he said, A hundred measures of oil. And he said unto him, Take thy bond, and sit down quickly and write fifty.</font>
41:16:7: <font color="#CC0000">Then said he to another, And how much owest thou? And he said, A hundred measures of wheat. He saith unto him, Take thy bond, and write fourscore.</font>
41:16:8: <font color="#CC0000">And his lord commended the unrighteous steward because he had done wisely: for the sons of this world are for their own generation wiser than the sons of the light.</font>
41:16:9: <font color="#CC0000">And I say unto you, Make to yourselves friends by means of the mammon of unrighteousness; that, when it shall fail, they may receive you into the eternal tabernacles.</font>
41:16:10: <font color="#CC0000">He that is faithful in a very little is faithful also in much: and he that is unrighteous in a very little is unrighteous also in much.</font>
41:16:11: <font color="#CC0000">If therefore ye have not been faithful in the unrighteous mammon, who will commit to your trust the true riches?</font>
41:16:12: <font color="#CC0000">And if ye have not been faithful in that which is another’s, who will give you that which is your own?</font>
41:16:13: <font color="#CC0000">No servant can serve two masters: for either he will hate the one, and love the other; or else he will hold to one, and despise the other. Ye cannot serve God and mammon.</font>
41:16:15: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Ye are they that justify yourselves in the sight of men; but God knoweth your hearts: for that which is exalted among men is an abomination in the sight of God</font>.
41:16:16: <font color="#CC0000">The law and the prophets were until John: from that time the gospel of the kingdom of God is preached, and every man entereth violently into it.</font>
41:16:17: <font color="#CC0000">But it is easier for heaven and earth to pass away, than for one tittle of the law to fall.</font>
41:16:18: <font color="#CC0000">Every one that putteth away his wife, and marrieth another, committeth adultery: and he that marrieth one that is put away from a husband committeth adultery.</font>
41:16:19: <font color="#CC0000">Now there was a certain rich man, and he was clothed in purple and fine linen, faring sumptuously every day:</font>
41:16:20: <font color="#CC0000">and a certain beggar named Lazarus was laid at his gate, full of sores,</font>
41:16:21: <font color="#CC0000">and desiring to be fed with the crumbs that fell from the rich man’s table; yea, even the dogs came and licked his sores.</font>
41:16:22: <font color="#CC0000">And it came to pass, that the beggar died, and that he was carried away by the angels into Abraham’s bosom: and the rich man also died, and was buried.</font>
41:16:23: <font color="#CC0000">And in Hades he lifted up his eyes, being in torments, and seeth Abraham afar off, and Lazarus in his bosom.</font>
41:16:24: <font color="#CC0000">And he cried and said, Father Abraham, have mercy on me, and send Lazarus, that he may dip the tip of his finger in water, and cool my tongue; for I am in anguish in this flame.</font>
41:16:25: <font color="#CC0000">But Abraham said, Son, remember that thou in thy lifetime receivedst thy good things, and Lazarus in like manner evil things: but now here he is comforted, and thou art in anguish.</font>
41:16:26: <font color="#CC0000">And besides all this, between us and you there is a great gulf fixed, that they that would pass from hence to you may not be able, and that none may cross over from thence to us.</font>
41:16:27: <font color="#CC0000">And he said, I pray thee therefore, father, that thou wouldest send him to my father’s house;</font>
41:16:28: <font color="#CC0000">for I have five brethren; that he may testify unto them, lest they also come into this place of torment.</font>
41:16:29: <font color="#CC0000">But Abraham saith, They have Moses and the prophets; let them hear them.</font>
41:16:30: <font color="#CC0000">And he said, Nay, father Abraham: but if one go to them from the dead, they will repent.</font>
41:16:31: <font color="#CC0000">And he said unto him, If they hear not Moses and the prophets, neither will they be persuaded, if one rise from the dead.</font>
41:17:1: And he said unto his disciples, <font color="#CC0000">It is impossible but that occasions of stumbling should come; but woe unto him, through whom they come</font>!
41:17:2: <font color="#CC0000">It were well for him if a millstone were hanged about his neck, and he were thrown into the sea, rather than that he should cause one of these little ones to stumble.</font>
41:17:3: <font color="#CC0000">Take heed to yourselves: if thy brother sin, rebuke him; and if he repent, forgive him.</font>
41:17:4: <font color="#CC0000">And if he sin against thee seven times in the day, and seven times turn again to thee, saying, I repent; thou shalt forgive him.</font>
41:17:6: And the Lord said, <font color="#CC0000">If ye had faith as a grain of mustard seed, ye would say unto this sycamine tree, Be thou rooted up, and be thou planted in the sea; and it would obey you</font>.
41:17:7: <font color="#CC0000">But who is there of you, having a servant plowing or keeping sheep, that will say unto him, when he is come in from the field, Come straightway and sit down to meat;</font>
41:17:8: <font color="#CC0000">and will not rather say unto him, Make ready wherewith I may sup, and gird thyself, and serve me, till I have eaten and drunken; and afterward thou shalt eat and drink?</font>
41:17:9: <font color="#CC0000">Doth he thank the servant because he did the things that were commanded?</font>
41:17:10: <font color="#CC0000">Even so ye also, when ye shall have done all the things that are commanded you, say, We are unprofitable servants; we have done that which it was our duty to do.</font>
41:17:14: And when he saw them, he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Go and show yourselves unto the priests</font>. And it came to pass, as they went, they were cleansed.
41:17:17: And Jesus answering said, <font color="#CC0000">Were not the ten cleansed? but where are the nine</font>?
41:17:18: <font color="#CC0000">Were there none found that returned to give glory to God, save this stranger?</font>
41:17:19: And he said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Arise, and go thy way: thy faith hath made thee whole</font>.
41:17:20: And being asked by the Pharisees, when the kingdom of God cometh, he answered them and said, <font color="#CC0000">The kingdom of God cometh not with observation</font>:
41:17:21: <font color="#CC0000">neither shall they say, Lo, here! or, There! for lo, the kingdom of God is within you.</font>
41:17:22: And he said unto the disciples, <font color="#CC0000">The days will come, when ye shall desire to see one of the days of the Son of man, and ye shall not see it</font>.
41:17:23: <font color="#CC0000">And they shall say to you, Lo, there! Lo, here! go not away, nor follow after them:</font>
41:17:24: <font color="#CC0000">for as the lightning, when it lighteneth out of the one part under the heaven, shineth unto the other part under heaven; so shall the Son of man be in his day.</font>
41:17:25: <font color="#CC0000">But first must he suffer many things and be rejected of this generation.</font>
41:17:26: <font color="#CC0000">And as it came to pass in the days of Noah, even so shall it be also in the days of the Son of man.</font>
41:17:27: <font color="#CC0000">They ate, they drank, they married, they were given in marriage, until the day that Noah entered into the ark, and the flood came, and destroyed them all.</font>
41:17:28: <font color="#CC0000">Likewise even as it came to pass in the days of Lot; they ate, they drank, they bought, they sold, they planted, they builded;</font>
41:17:29: <font color="#CC0000">but in the day that Lot went out from Sodom it rained fire and brimstone from heaven, and destroyed them all:</font>
41:17:30: <font color="#CC0000">after the same manner shall it be in the day that the Son of man is revealed.</font>
41:17:31: <font color="#CC0000">In that day, he that shall be on the housetop, and his goods in the house, let him not go down to take them away: and let him that is in the field likewise not return back.</font>
41:17:33: <font color="#CC0000">Whosoever shall seek to gain his life shall lose it: but whosoever shall lose his life shall preserve it.</font>
41:17:34: <font color="#CC0000">I say unto you, In that night there shall be two men on one bed; the one shall be taken, and the other shall be left.</font>
41:17:35: <font color="#CC0000">There shall be two women grinding together; the one shall be taken, and the other shall be left.</font>
41:17:37: And they answering say unto him, Where, Lord? And he said unto them, Where <font color="#CC0000">the body is, thither will the eagles also be gathered together</font>.
41:18:2: saying, <font color="#CC0000">There was in a city a judge, who feared not God, and regarded not man</font>:
41:18:3: <font color="#CC0000">and there was a widow in that city; and she came oft unto him, saying, Avenge me of mine adversary.</font>
41:18:4: <font color="#CC0000">And he would not for a while: but afterward he said within himself, Though I fear not God, nor regard man;</font>
41:18:5: <font color="#CC0000">yet because this widow troubleth me, I will avenge her, lest she wear me out by her continual coming.</font>
41:18:6: And the Lord said, <font color="#CC0000">Hear what the unrighteous judge saith</font>.
41:18:7: <font color="#CC0000">And shall not God avenge his elect, that cry to him day and night, and yet he is longsuffering over them?</font>
41:18:8: <font color="#CC0000">I say unto you, that he will avenge them speedily. Nevertheless, when the Son of man cometh, shall he find faith on the earth?</font>
41:18:10: <font color="#CC0000">Two men went up into the temple to pray; the one a Pharisee, and the other a publican.</font>
41:18:11: <font color="#CC0000">The Pharisee stood and prayed thus with himself, God, I thank thee, that I am not as the rest of men, extortioners, unjust, adulterers, or even as this publican.</font>
41:18:12: <font color="#CC0000">I fast twice in the week; I give tithes of all that I get.</font>
41:18:13: <font color="#CC0000">But the publican, standing afar off, would not lift up so much as his eyes unto heaven, but smote his breast, saying, God, be thou merciful to me a sinner.</font>
41:18:14: <font color="#CC0000">I say unto you, This man went down to his house justified rather than the other: for every one that exalteth himself shall be humbled; but he that humbleth himself shall be exalted.</font>
41:18:16: But Jesus called them unto him, saying, <font color="#CC0000">Suffer the little children to come unto me, and forbid them not: for to such belongeth the kingdom of God</font>.
41:18:17: <font color="#CC0000">Verily I say unto you, Whosoever shall not receive the kingdom of God as a little child, he shall in no wise enter therein.</font>
41:18:19: And Jesus said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Why callest thou me good? none is good, save one, even God</font>.
41:18:20: <font color="#CC0000">Thou knowest the commandments, Do not commit adultery, Do not kill, Do not steal, Do not bear false witness, Honor thy father and mother.</font>
41:18:22: And when Jesus heard it, he said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">One thing thou lackest yet: sell all that thou hast, and distribute unto the poor, and thou shalt have treasure in heaven: and come, follow me</font>.
41:18:24: And Jesus seeing him said, <font color="#CC0000">How hardly shall they that have riches enter into the kingdom of God</font>!
41:18:25: <font color="#CC0000">For it is easier for a camel to enter in through a needle’s eye, than for a rich man to enter into the kingdom of God.</font>
41:18:27: But he said, <font color="#CC0000">The things which are impossible with men are possible with God</font>.
41:18:29: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Verily I say unto you, There is no man that hath left house, or wife, or brethren, or parents, or children, for the kingdom of God’s sake</font>,
41:18:30: <font color="#CC0000">who shall not receive manifold more in this time, and in the world to come eternal life.</font>
41:18:31: And he took unto him the twelve, and said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Behold, we go up to Jerusalem, and all the things that are written through the prophets shall be accomplished unto the Son of man</font>.
41:18:32: <font color="#CC0000">For he shall be delivered up unto the Gentiles, and shall be mocked, and shamefully treated, and spit upon:</font>
41:18:33: <font color="#CC0000">and they shall scourge and kill him: and the third day he shall rise again.</font>
41:18:41: <font color="#CC0000">What wilt thou that I should do unto thee</font>? And he said, Lord, that I may receive my sight.
41:18:42: And Jesus said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Receive thy sight: thy faith hath made thee</font> whole.
41:19:5: And when Jesus came to the place, he looked up, and said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Zacchæus, make haste, and come down; for to-day I must abide at thy house</font>.
41:19:9: And Jesus said unto him, To-day <font color="#CC0000">is salvation come to this house, forasmuch as he also is a son of Abraham</font>.
41:19:10: <font color="#CC0000">For the Son of man came to seek and to save that which was lost.</font>
41:19:12: He said therefore, <font color="#CC0000">A certain nobleman went into a far country, to receive for himself a kingdom, and to return</font>.
41:19:13: <font color="#CC0000">And he called ten servants of his, and gave them ten pounds, and said unto them, Trade ye herewith till I come.</font>
41:19:14: <font color="#CC0000">But his citizens hated him, and sent an ambassage after him, saying, We will not that this man reign over us.</font>
41:19:15: <font color="#CC0000">And it came to pass, when he was come back again, having received the kingdom, that he commanded these servants, unto whom he had given the money, to be called to him, that he might know what they had gained by trading.</font>
41:19:16: <font color="#CC0000">And the first came before him, saying, Lord, thy pound hath made ten pounds more.</font>
41:19:17: <font color="#CC0000">And he said unto him, Well done, thou good servant: because thou wast found faithful in a very little, have thou authority over ten cities.</font>
41:19:18: <font color="#CC0000">And the second came, saying, Thy pound, Lord, hath made five pounds.</font>
41:19:19: <font color="#CC0000">And he said unto him also, Be thou also over five cities.</font>
41:19:20: <font color="#CC0000">And another came, saying, Lord, behold, here is thy pound, which I kept laid up in a napkin:</font>
41:19:21: <font color="#CC0000">for I feared thee, because thou art an austere man: thou takest up that which thou layedst not down, and reapest that which thou didst not sow.</font>
41:19:22: <font color="#CC0000">He saith unto him, Out of thine own mouth will I judge thee, thou wicked servant. Thou knewest that I am an austere man, taking up that which I laid not down, and reaping that which I did not sow;</font>
41:19:23: <font color="#CC0000">then wherefore gavest thou not my money into the bank, and I at my coming should have required it with interest?</font>
41:19:24: <font color="#CC0000">And he said unto them that stood by, Take away from him the pound, and give it unto him that hath the ten pounds.</font>
41:19:25: <font color="#CC0000">And they said unto him, Lord, he hath ten pounds.</font>
41:19:26: <font color="#CC0000">I say unto you, that unto every one that hath shall be given; but from him that hath not, even that which he hath shall be taken away from him.</font>
41:19:27: <font color="#CC0000">But these mine enemies, that would not that I should reign over them, bring hither, and slay them before me.</font>
41:19:30: saying, <font color="#CC0000">Go your way into the village over against you; in which as ye enter ye shall find a colt tied, whereon no man ever yet sat: loose him, and bring him</font>.

41:19:40: And he answered and said, <font color="#CC0000">I tell you that, if these shall hold their peace, the stones will cry out</font>.
41:19:42: saying, <font color="#CC0000">If thou hadst known in this day, even thou, the things which belong unto peace! but now they are hid from thine eyes</font>.
41:19:43: <font color="#CC0000">For the days shall come upon thee, when thine enemies shall cast up a bank about thee, and compass thee round, and keep thee in on every side,</font>
41:19:44: <font color="#CC0000">and shall dash thee to the ground, and thy children within thee; and they shall not leave in thee one stone upon another; because thou knewest not the time of thy visitation.</font>
41:19:46: saying unto them, <font color="#CC0000">It is written, And my house shall be a house of prayer: but ye have made it a den of</font> robbers.
41:20:3: And he answered and said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">I also will ask you a question; and tell me</font>:

41:20:9: And he began to speak unto the people this parable: <font color="#CC0000">A man planted a vineyard, and let it out to husbandmen, and went into another country for a long time</font>.
41:20:10: <font color="#CC0000">And at the season he sent unto the husbandmen a servant, that they should give him of the fruit of the vineyard: but the husbandmen beat him, and sent him away empty.</font>
41:20:11: <font color="#CC0000">And he sent yet another servant: and him also they beat, and handled him shamefully, and sent him away empty.</font>
41:20:12: <font color="#CC0000">And he sent yet a third: and him also they wounded, and cast him forth.</font>
41:20:13: <font color="#CC0000">And the lord of the vineyard said, What shall I do? I will send my beloved son; it may be they will reverence him.</font>
41:20:14: <font color="#CC0000">But when the husbandmen saw him, they reasoned one with another, saying, This is the heir; let us kill him, that the inheritance may be ours.</font>
41:20:15: <font color="#CC0000">And they cast him forth out of the vineyard, and killed him. What therefore will the lord of the vineyard do unto them?</font>
41:20:16: <font color="#CC0000">He will come and destroy these husbandmen, and will give the vineyard unto others</font>. And when they heard it, they said, God forbid.
41:20:17: But he looked upon them, and said, <font color="#CC0000">What then is this that is written,  The stone which the builders rejected, The same was made the head of the corner</font>?
41:20:18: <font color="#CC0000">Every one that falleth on that stone shall be broken to pieces; but on whomsoever it shall fall, it will scatter him as dust.</font>
41:20:23: <font color="#CC0000">But he perceived their craftiness, and said unto them,</font>
41:20:24: Show <font color="#CC0000">me a denarius. Whose image and superscription hath it</font>? And they said, Cæsar’s.
41:20:25: And he said unto them, Then <font color="#CC0000">render unto Cæsar the things that are Cæsar’s, and unto God the things</font> that are God’s.
41:20:34: And Jesus said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">The sons of this world marry, and are given in marriage</font>:
41:20:35: <font color="#CC0000">but they that are accounted worthy to attain to that world, and the resurrection from the dead, neither marry, nor are given in marriage:</font>
41:20:36: <font color="#CC0000">for neither can they die any more: for they are equal unto the angels; and are sons of God, being sons of the resurrection.</font>
41:20:37: <font color="#CC0000">But that the dead are raised, even Moses showed, in the place concerning the Bush, when he calleth the Lord the God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob.</font>

41:20:41: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">How say they that the Christ is David’s son</font>?
41:20:42: <font color="#CC0000">For David himself saith in the book of Psalms,  The Lord said unto my Lord, Sit thou on my right hand,</font>
41:20:43: <font color="#CC0000">Till I make thine enemies the footstool of thy feet.</font>
41:20:44: <font color="#CC0000">David therefore calleth him Lord, and how is he his son?</font>
41:20:46: <font color="#CC0000">Beware of the scribes, who desire to walk in long robes, and love salutations in the marketplaces, and chief seats in the synagogues, and chief places at feasts;</font>

41:21:3: And he said, <font color="#CC0000">Of a truth I say unto you, This poor widow cast in more than they all</font>:
41:21:4: <font color="#CC0000">for all these did of their superfluity cast in unto the gifts; but she of her want did cast in all the living that she had.</font>
41:21:6: <font color="#CC0000">As for these things which ye behold, the days will come, in which there shall not be left here one stone upon another, that shall not be thrown down.</font>
41:21:8: And he said, <font color="#CC0000">Take heed that ye be not led astray: for many shall come in my name, saying, I am he; and, The time is at hand: go ye not after them</font>.
41:21:9: <font color="#CC0000">And when ye shall hear of wars and tumults, be not terrified: for these things must needs come to pass first; but the end is not immediately.</font>
41:21:10: Then said he unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Nation shall rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom</font>;
41:21:11: <font color="#CC0000">and there shall be great earthquakes, and in divers places famines and pestilences; and there shall be terrors and great signs from heaven.</font>
41:21:12: <font color="#CC0000">But before all these things, they shall lay their hands on you, and shall persecute you, delivering you up to the synagogues and prisons, bringing you before kings and governors for my name’s sake.</font>
41:21:13: <font color="#CC0000">It shall turn out unto you for a testimony.</font>
41:21:14: <font color="#CC0000">Settle it therefore in your hearts, not to meditate beforehand how to answer:</font>
41:21:15: <font color="#CC0000">for I will give you a mouth and wisdom, which all your adversaries shall not be able to withstand or to gainsay.</font>
41:21:16: <font color="#CC0000">But ye shall be delivered up even by parents, and brethren, and kinsfolk, and friends; and some of you shall they cause to be put to death.</font>
41:21:17: <font color="#CC0000">And ye shall be hated of all men for my name’s sake.</font>
41:21:18: <font color="#CC0000">And not a hair of your head shall perish.</font>
41:21:19: <font color="#CC0000">In your patience ye shall win your souls.</font>
41:21:20: <font color="#CC0000">But when ye see Jerusalem compassed with armies, then know that her desolation is at hand.</font>
41:21:21: <font color="#CC0000">Then let them that are in Judæa flee unto the mountains; and let them that are in the midst of her depart out; and let not them that are in the country enter therein.</font>
41:21:22: <font color="#CC0000">For these are days of vengeance, that all things which are written may be fulfilled.</font>
41:21:23: <font color="#CC0000">Woe unto them that are with child and to them that give suck in those days! for there shall be great distress upon the land, and wrath unto this people.</font>
41:21:24: <font color="#CC0000">And they shall fall by the edge of the sword, and shall be led captive into all the nations: and Jerusalem shall be trodden down of the Gentiles, until the times of the Gentiles be fulfilled.</font>
41:21:25: <font color="#CC0000">And there shall be signs in sun and moon and stars; and upon the earth distress of nations, in perplexity for the roaring of the sea and the billows;</font>
41:21:26: <font color="#CC0000">men fainting for fear, and for expectation of the things which are coming on the world: for the powers of the heavens shall be shaken.</font>
41:21:27: <font color="#CC0000">And then shall they see the Son of man coming in a cloud with power and great glory.</font>
41:21:28: <font color="#CC0000">But when these things begin to come to pass, look up, and lift up your heads; because your redemption draweth nigh.</font>
41:21:29: And he spake to them a parable: <font color="#CC0000">Behold the fig tree, and all the trees</font>:
41:21:30: <font color="#CC0000">when they now shoot forth, ye see it and know of your own selves that the summer is now nigh.</font>
41:21:31: <font color="#CC0000">Even so ye also, when ye see these things coming to pass, know ye that the kingdom of God is nigh.</font>
41:21:32: <font color="#CC0000">Verily I say unto you, This generation shall not pass away, till all things be accomplished.</font>
41:21:33: <font color="#CC0000">Heaven and earth shall pass away: but my words shall not pass away.</font>
41:21:34: <font color="#CC0000">But take heed to yourselves, lest haply your hearts be overcharged with surfeiting, and drunkenness, and cares of this life, and that day come on you suddenly as a snare:</font>
41:21:35: <font color="#CC0000">for so shall it come upon all them that dwell on the face of all the earth.</font>
41:21:36: <font color="#CC0000">But watch ye at every season, making supplication, that ye may prevail to escape all these things that shall come to pass, and to stand before the Son of man.</font>
41:22:8: And he sent Peter and John, saying, <font color="#CC0000">Go and make ready for us the passover, that we may eat</font>.

41:22:11: <font color="#CC0000">And ye shall say unto the master of the house, The Teacher saith unto thee, Where is the guest-chamber, where I shall eat the passover with my disciples?</font>
41:22:12: <font color="#CC0000">And he will show you a large upper room furnished: there make ready.</font>
41:22:15: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">With desire I have desired to eat this passover with you before I suffer</font>:
41:22:16: <font color="#CC0000">for I say unto you, I shall not eat it, until it be fulfilled in the kingdom of God.</font>
41:22:17: And he received a cup, and when he had given thanks, he said, <font color="#CC0000">Take this, and divide it among yourselves</font>:
41:22:18: <font color="#CC0000">for I say unto you, I shall not drink from henceforth of the fruit of the vine, until the kingdom of God shall come.</font>
41:22:19: And he took bread, and when he had given thanks, he brake it, and gave to them, saying, <font color="#CC0000">This is my body which is given for you: this do in remembrance of me</font>.
41:22:20: And the cup in like manner after supper, saying, <font color="#CC0000">This cup is the new covenant in my blood, even that which is poured out for you</font>.
41:22:21: <font color="#CC0000">But behold, the hand of him that betrayeth me is with me on the table.</font>
41:22:22: <font color="#CC0000">For the Son of man indeed goeth, as it hath been determined: but woe unto that man through whom he is betrayed!</font>
41:22:25: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">The kings of the Gentiles have lordship over them; and they that have authority over them are called Benefactors</font>.
41:22:26: <font color="#CC0000">But ye shall not be so: but he that is the greater among you, let him become as the younger; and he that is chief, as he that doth serve.</font>
41:22:27: <font color="#CC0000">For which is greater, he that sitteth at meat, or he that serveth? is not he that sitteth at meat? but I am in the midst of you as he that serveth.</font>
41:22:28: <font color="#CC0000">But ye are they that have continued with me in my temptations;</font>
41:22:29: <font color="#CC0000">and I appoint unto you a kingdom, even as my Father appointed unto me,</font>
41:22:30: <font color="#CC0000">that ye may eat and drink at my table in my kingdom; and ye shall sit on thrones judging the twelve tribes of Israel.</font>
41:22:31: <font color="#CC0000">Simon, Simon, behold, Satan asked to have you, that he might sift you as wheat</font>:
41:22:32: <font color="#CC0000">but I made supplication for thee, that thy faith fail not; and do thou, when once thou hast turned again, establish thy brethren.</font>
41:22:34: And he said, <font color="#CC0000">I tell thee, Peter, the cock shall not crow this day, until thou shalt thrice deny that thou knowest me</font>.
41:22:35: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">When I sent you forth without purse, and wallet, and shoes, lacked ye</font> anything? And they said, Nothing.
41:22:36: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">But now, he that hath a purse, let him take it, and likewise a wallet; and he that hath none, let him sell his cloak, and buy</font> a sword.
41:22:37: <font color="#CC0000">For I say unto you, that this which is written must be fulfilled in me, And he was reckoned with transgressors: for that which concerneth me hath fulfilment.</font>
41:22:38: And they said, Lord, behold, here are two swords. And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">It is enough</font>.
41:22:40: And when he was at the place, he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Pray that ye enter not into temptation</font>.

41:23:3: And Pilate asked him, saying, Art thou the King of the Jews? And he answered him and said, <font color="#CC0000">Thou sayest</font>.

41:23:29: <font color="#CC0000">For behold, the days are coming, in which they shall say, Blessed are the barren, and the wombs that never bare, and the breasts that never gave suck.</font>
41:23:30: <font color="#CC0000">Then shall they begin to say to the mountains, Fall on us; and to the hills, Cover us.</font>

41:24:17: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">What communications are these that ye have one with another, as ye walk? And they stood still, looking sad</font>.

41:24:25: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">O foolish men, and slow of heart to believe in all that the prophets have spoken</font>!
41:24:26: <font color="#CC0000">Behooved it not the Christ to suffer these things, and to enter into his glory?</font>

41:24:48: <font color="#CC0000">Ye are witnesses of these things.</font>
41:24:49: <font color="#CC0000">And behold, I send forth the promise of my Father upon you: but tarry ye in the city, until ye be clothed with power from on high.</font>
42:1:38: And Jesus turned, and beheld them following, and saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">What seek ye</font>? And they said unto him, Rabbi (which is to say, being interpreted, Teacher), where abidest thou?

42:3:11: <font color="#CC0000">Verily, verily, I say unto thee, We speak that which we know, and bear witness of that which we have seen; and ye receive not our witness.</font>
42:3:12: <font color="#CC0000">If I told you earthly things and ye believe not, how shall ye believe if I tell you heavenly things?</font>
42:3:13: <font color="#CC0000">And no one hath ascended into heaven, but he that descended out of heaven, even the Son of man, who is in heaven.</font>
42:3:14: <font color="#CC0000">And as Moses lifted up the serpent in the wilderness, even so must the Son of man be lifted up;</font>
42:3:15: <font color="#CC0000">that whosoever believeth may in him have eternal life.</font>
42:3:16: <font color="#CC0000">For God so loved the world, that he gave his only begotten Son, that whosoever believeth on him should not perish, but have eternal life.</font>
42:3:17: <font color="#CC0000">For God sent not the Son into the world to judge the world; but that the world should be saved through him.</font>
42:3:18: <font color="#CC0000">He that believeth on him is not judged: he that believeth not hath been judged already, because he hath not believed on the name of the only begotten Son of God.</font>

42:3:20: <font color="#CC0000">For every one that doeth evil hateth the light, and cometh not to the light, lest his works should be reproved.</font>

42:4:7: There cometh a woman of Samaria to draw water: Jesus saith unto her, <font color="#CC0000">Give me to drink</font>.

42:4:14: <font color="#CC0000">but whosoever drinketh of the water that I shall give him shall never thirst; but the water that I shall give him shall become in him a well of water springing up unto eternal life.</font>
42:4:16: Jesus saith unto her, <font color="#CC0000">Go, call thy husband, and come hither</font>.
42:4:17: The woman answered and <font color="#CC0000">said unto him, I have no husband</font>. Jesus saith unto her, Thou saidst well, I have no husband:
42:4:18: <font color="#CC0000">for thou hast had five husbands; and he whom thou now hast is not thy husband: this hast thou said truly.</font>

42:4:37: <font color="#CC0000">For herein is the saying true, One soweth, and another reapeth.</font>
