42:5:19: Jesus therefore answered and said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Verily, verily, I say unto you, The Son can do nothing of himself, but what he seeth the Father doing: for what things soever he doeth, these the Son also doeth</font> in like manner.
42:5:20: <font color="#CC0000">For the Father loveth the Son, and showeth him all things that himself doeth: and greater works than these will he show him, that ye may marvel.</font>
42:5:21: <font color="#CC0000">For as the Father raiseth the dead and giveth them life, even so the Son also giveth life to whom he will.</font>
42:5:22: <font color="#CC0000">For neither doth the Father judge any man, but he hath given all judgment unto the Son;</font>
42:5:23: <font color="#CC0000">that all may honor the Son, even as they honor the Father. He that honoreth not the Son honoreth not the Father that sent him.</font>
42:5:24: <font color="#CC0000">Verily, verily, I say unto you, He that heareth my word, and believeth him that sent me, hath eternal life, and cometh not into judgment, but hath passed out of death into life.</font>
42:5:25: <font color="#CC0000">Verily, verily, I say unto you, The hour cometh, and now is, when the dead shall hear the voice of the Son of God; and they that hear shall live.</font>
42:5:26: <font color="#CC0000">For as the Father hath life in himself, even so gave he to the Son also to have life in himself:</font>
42:5:27: <font color="#CC0000">and he gave him authority to execute judgment, because he is a son of man.</font>
42:5:28: <font color="#CC0000">Marvel not at this: for the hour cometh, in which all that are in the tombs shall hear his voice,</font>
42:5:29: <font color="#CC0000">and shall come forth; they that have done good, unto the resurrection of life; and they that have done evil, unto the resurrection of judgment.</font>
42:5:30: <font color="#CC0000">I can of myself do nothing: as I hear, I judge: and my judgment is righteous; because I seek not mine own will, but the will of him that sent me.</font>
42:5:31: <font color="#CC0000">If I bear witness of myself, my witness is not true.</font>
42:5:32: <font color="#CC0000">It is another that beareth witness of me; and I know that the witness which he witnesseth of me is true.</font>
42:5:33: <font color="#CC0000">Ye have sent unto John, and he hath borne witness unto the truth.</font>
42:5:34: <font color="#CC0000">But the witness which I receive is not from man: howbeit I say these things, that ye may be saved.</font>
42:5:35: <font color="#CC0000">He was the lamp that burneth and shineth; and ye were willing to rejoice for a season in his light.</font>
42:5:36: <font color="#CC0000">But the witness which I have is greater than that of John; for the works which the Father hath given me to accomplish, the very works that I do, bear witness of me, that the Father hath sent me.</font>
42:5:37: <font color="#CC0000">And the Father that sent me, he hath borne witness of me. Ye have neither heard his voice at any time, nor seen his form.</font>
42:5:38: <font color="#CC0000">And ye have not his word abiding in you: for whom he sent, him ye believe not.</font>
42:5:39: <font color="#CC0000">Ye search the scriptures, because ye think that in them ye have eternal life; and these are they which bear witness of me;</font>
42:5:40: <font color="#CC0000">and ye will not come to me, that ye may have life.</font>
42:5:41: <font color="#CC0000">I receive not glory from men.</font>
42:5:42: <font color="#CC0000">But I know you, that ye have not the love of God in yourselves.</font>
42:5:43: <font color="#CC0000">I am come in my Father’s name, and ye receive me not: if another shall come in his own name, him ye will receive.</font>
42:5:44: <font color="#CC0000">How can ye believe, who receive glory one of another, and the glory that cometh from the only God ye seek not?</font>
42:5:45: <font color="#CC0000">Think not that I will accuse you to the Father: there is one that accuseth you, even Moses, on whom ye have set your hope.</font>
42:5:46: <font color="#CC0000">For if ye believed Moses, ye would believe me; for he wrote of me.</font>
42:5:47: <font color="#CC0000">But if ye believe not his writings, how shall ye believe my words?</font>
42:6:5: Jesus therefore lifting up his eyes, and seeing that a great multitude cometh unto him, saith unto Philip, <font color="#CC0000">Whence are we to buy bread, that these may eat</font>?
42:6:10: Jesus said, <font color="#CC0000">Make the people sit down</font>. Now there was much grass in the place. So the men sat down, in number about five thousand.
42:6:12: And when they were filled, he saith unto his disciples, <font color="#CC0000">Gather up the broken pieces which remain over, that nothing be lost</font>.
42:6:20: But he saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">It is I; be not afraid</font>.
42:6:26: Jesus answered them and said, <font color="#CC0000">Verily, verily, I say unto you, Ye seek me, not because ye saw signs, but because ye ate of the loaves, and were filled</font>.
42:6:27: <font color="#CC0000">Work not for the food which perisheth, but for the food which abideth unto eternal life, which the Son of man shall give unto you: for him the Father, even God, hath sealed.</font>
42:6:29: Jesus answered and said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">This is the work of God, that ye believe on him whom he hath sent</font>.
42:6:32: Jesus therefore said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Verily, verily, I say unto you, It was not Moses that gave you the bread out of heaven; but my Father giveth you the true bread out of heaven</font>.
42:6:33: <font color="#CC0000">For the bread of God is that which cometh down out of heaven, and giveth life unto the world.</font>
42:6:35: Jesus said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">I am the bread of life: he that cometh to me shall not hunger, and he that believeth on me shall never thirst</font>.
42:6:36: <font color="#CC0000">But I said unto you, that ye have seen me, and yet believe not.</font>
42:6:37: <font color="#CC0000">All that which the Father giveth me shall come unto me; and him that cometh to me I will in no wise cast out.</font>
42:6:38: <font color="#CC0000">For I am come down from heaven, not to do mine own will, but the will of him that sent me.</font>
42:6:39: <font color="#CC0000">And this is the will of him that sent me, that of all that which he hath given me I should lose nothing, but should raise it up at the last day.</font>
42:6:40: <font color="#CC0000">For this is the will of my Father, that every one that beholdeth the Son, and believeth on him, should have eternal life; and I will raise him up at the last day.</font>
42:6:43: Jesus answered and said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Murmur not among yourselves</font>.
42:6:44: <font color="#CC0000">No man can come to me, except the Father that sent me draw him: and I will raise him up in the last day.</font>
42:6:45: <font color="#CC0000">It is written in the prophets, And they shall all be taught of God. Every one that hath heard from the Father, and hath learned, cometh unto me.</font>
42:6:46: <font color="#CC0000">Not that any man hath seen the Father, save he that is from God, he hath seen the Father.</font>
42:6:47: <font color="#CC0000">Verily, verily, I say unto you, He that believeth hath eternal life.</font>
42:6:48: <font color="#CC0000">I am the bread of life.</font>
42:6:49: <font color="#CC0000">Your fathers ate the manna in the wilderness, and they died.</font>
42:6:50: <font color="#CC0000">This is the bread which cometh down out of heaven, that a man may eat thereof, and not die.</font>
42:6:51: <font color="#CC0000">I am the living bread which came down out of heaven: if any man eat of this bread, he shall live for ever: yea and the bread which I will give is my flesh, for the life of the world.</font>
42:6:53: Jesus therefore said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Verily, verily, I say unto you, Except ye eat the flesh of the Son of man and drink his blood, ye have not life in</font> yourselves.
42:6:54: <font color="#CC0000">He that eateth my flesh and drinketh my blood hath eternal life; and I will raise him up at the last day.</font>
42:6:55: <font color="#CC0000">For my flesh is meat indeed, and my blood is drink indeed.</font>
42:6:56: <font color="#CC0000">He that eateth my flesh and drinketh my blood abideth in me, and I in him.</font>
42:6:57: <font color="#CC0000">As the living Father sent me, and I live because of the Father; so he that eateth me, he also shall live because of me.</font>
42:6:58: <font color="#CC0000">This is the bread which came down out of heaven: not as the fathers ate, and died; he that eateth this bread shall live for ever.</font>
42:6:61: But Jesus knowing in himself that his disciples murmured at this, said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Doth this cause you</font> to stumble?
42:6:62: <font color="#CC0000">What then if ye should behold the Son of man ascending where he was before?</font>
42:6:63: <font color="#CC0000">It is the spirit that giveth life; the flesh profiteth nothing: the words that I have spoken unto you are spirit, and are life.</font>
42:6:64: <font color="#CC0000">But there are some of you that believe not</font>. For Jesus knew from the beginning who they were that believed not, and who it was that should betray him.
42:6:65: And he <font color="#CC0000">said, For this cause have I said unto you, that no man can come unto me, except it be given unto him of the Father</font>.
42:6:67: Jesus said therefore unto the twelve, Would <font color="#CC0000">ye also go away</font>?
42:6:70: Jesus answered them, Did <font color="#CC0000">not I choose you the twelve, and one of you is a devil</font>?
42:7:6: Jesus therefore saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">My time is not yet come; but your time is always ready</font>.
42:7:7: <font color="#CC0000">The world cannot hate you; but me it hateth, because I testify of it, that its works are evil.</font>
42:7:8: <font color="#CC0000">Go ye up unto the feast: I go not up unto this feast; because my time is not yet fulfilled.</font>
42:7:16: Jesus therefore answered them, and said, <font color="#CC0000">My teaching is not mine, but his that sent me</font>.
42:7:17: <font color="#CC0000">If any man willeth to do his will, he shall know of the teaching, whether it is of God, or whether I speak from myself.</font>
42:7:18: <font color="#CC0000">He that speaketh from himself seeketh his own glory: but he that seeketh the glory of him that sent him, the same is true, and no unrighteousness is in him.</font>
42:7:19: <font color="#CC0000">Did not Moses give you the law, and yet none of you doeth the law? Why seek ye to kill me?</font>
42:7:21: Jesus answered and said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">I did one work, and ye all marvel</font> because thereof.
42:7:22: <font color="#CC0000">Moses hath given you circumcision (not that it is of Moses, but of the fathers); and on the sabbath ye circumcise a man.</font>
42:7:23: <font color="#CC0000">If a man receiveth circumcision on the sabbath, that the law of Moses may not be broken; are ye wroth with me, because I made a man every whit whole on the sabbath?</font>
42:7:24: <font color="#CC0000">Judge not according to appearance, but judge righteous judgment.</font>
42:7:28: Jesus therefore cried in the temple, teaching and saying, <font color="#CC0000">Ye both know me, and know whence I am; and I am not come of myself, but he that sent me is true, whom ye know not</font>.
42:7:29: <font color="#CC0000">I know him; because I am from him, and he sent me.</font>
42:7:33: Jesus therefore said, <font color="#CC0000">Yet a little while am I with you, and I go unto him that sent me</font>.
42:7:34: <font color="#CC0000">Ye shall seek me, and shall not find me: and where I am, ye cannot come.</font>
42:7:37: Now on the last day, the greatdayof the feast, Jesus stood and cried, saying, <font color="#CC0000">If any man thirst, let him come unto me and drink</font>.
42:7:38: <font color="#CC0000">He that believeth on me, as the scripture hath said, from within him shall flow rivers of living water.</font>
42:8:7: But when they continued asking him, he lifted up himself, and said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">He that is without sin among you, let him first cast a stone at her</font>.
42:8:10: And Jesus lifted up himself, and said unto her, <font color="#CC0000">Woman, where are they? did no man condemn thee</font>?
42:8:11: And she said, No man, Lord. And Jesus said, <font color="#CC0000">Neither do I condemn thee: go thy way; from henceforth sin no more</font>.]
42:8:12: Again therefore Jesus spake unto them, saying, <font color="#CC0000">I am the light of the world: he that followeth me shall not walk in the darkness, but shall have the light of life</font>.
42:8:14: Jesus answered and said unto them, Even if <font color="#CC0000">I bear witness of myself, my witness is true; for I know whence I came, and whither I go; but ye know not whence I come, or whither I go</font>.
42:8:15: <font color="#CC0000">Ye judge after the flesh; I judge no man.</font>
42:8:16: <font color="#CC0000">Yea and if I judge, my judgment is true; for I am not alone, but I and the Father that sent me.</font>
42:8:17: <font color="#CC0000">Yea and in your law it is written, that the witness of two men is true.</font>
42:8:18: <font color="#CC0000">I am he that beareth witness of myself, and the Father that sent me beareth witness of me.</font>
42:8:19: They said therefore unto him, Where is thy Father? Jesus answered, <font color="#CC0000">Ye know neither me, nor my Father: if ye knew me, ye would know my Father also</font>.
42:8:21: He said therefore again unto them, <font color="#CC0000">I go away, and ye shall seek me, and shall die in your sin: whither I go, ye cannot come</font>.
42:8:23: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Ye are from beneath; I am from above: ye are of this world; I am not of this world</font>.
42:8:24: <font color="#CC0000">I said therefore unto you, that ye shall die in your sins: for except ye believe that I am he, ye shall die in your sins.</font>
42:8:25: They said therefore unto him, Who art thou? Jesus said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Even that which I have also spoken unto you from the beginning</font>.
42:8:26: <font color="#CC0000">I have many things to speak and to judge concerning you: howbeit he that sent me is true; and the things which I heard from him, these speak I unto the world.</font>
42:8:28: Jesus therefore said, <font color="#CC0000">When ye have lifted up the Son of man, then shall ye know that I am he, and that I do nothing of myself, but as the Father taught me, I speak these things</font>.
42:8:29: <font color="#CC0000">And he that sent me is with me; he hath not left me alone; for I do always the things that are pleasing to him.</font>
42:8:31: Jesus therefore said to those Jews that had believed him, <font color="#CC0000">If ye abide in my word, then are ye truly my disciples</font>;
42:8:32: <font color="#CC0000">and ye shall know the truth, and the truth shall make you free.</font>
42:8:34: Jesus answered them, <font color="#CC0000">Verily, verily, I say unto you, Every one that committeth sin is the bondservant of sin</font>.
42:8:35: <font color="#CC0000">And the bondservant abideth not in the house for ever: the son abideth for ever.</font>
42:8:36: <font color="#CC0000">If therefore the Son shall make you free, ye shall be free indeed.</font>
42:8:37: <font color="#CC0000">I know that ye are Abraham’s seed; yet ye seek to kill me, because my word hath not free course in you.</font>
42:8:38: <font color="#CC0000">I speak the things which I have seen with my Father: and ye also do the things which ye heard from your father.</font>
42:8:39: They answered and said unto him, Our father is Abraham. Jesus saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">If ye were Abraham’s children, ye would do the works of Abraham</font>.
42:8:40: <font color="#CC0000">But now ye seek to kill me, a man that hath told you the truth, which I heard from God: this did not Abraham.</font>
42:8:41: <font color="#CC0000">Ye do the works of your father</font>. They said unto him, We were not born of fornication; we have one Father, even God.
42:8:42: Jesus said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">If God were your Father, ye would love me: for I came forth and am come from God; for neither have I come of myself, but he sent me</font>.
42:8:43: <font color="#CC0000">Why do ye not understand my speech? Even because ye cannot hear my word.</font>
42:8:44: <font color="#CC0000">Ye are of your father the devil, and the lusts of your father it is your will to do. He was a murderer from the beginning, and standeth not in the truth, because there is no truth in him. When he speaketh a lie, he speaketh of his own: for he is a liar, and the father thereof.</font>
42:8:45: <font color="#CC0000">But because I say the truth, ye believe me not.</font>
42:8:46: <font color="#CC0000">Which of you convicteth me of sin? If I say truth, why do ye not believe me?</font>
42:8:47: <font color="#CC0000">He that is of God heareth the words of God: for this cause ye hear them not, because ye are not of God.</font>
42:8:49: Jesus answered, <font color="#CC0000">I have not a demon; but I honor my Father, and ye dishonor me</font>.
42:8:50: <font color="#CC0000">But I seek not mine own glory: there is one that seeketh and judgeth.</font>
42:8:51: <font color="#CC0000">Verily, verily, I say unto you, If a man keep my word, he shall never see death.</font>
42:8:54: Jesus answered, <font color="#CC0000">If I glorify myself, my glory is nothing: it is my Father that glorifieth me; of whom ye say, that he is your God</font>;
42:8:55: <font color="#CC0000">and ye have not known him: but I know him; and if I should say, I know him not, I shall be like unto you, a liar: but I know him, and keep his word.</font>
42:8:56: <font color="#CC0000">Your father Abraham rejoiced to see my day; and he saw it, and was glad.</font>
42:8:58: Jesus said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Verily, verily, I say unto you, Before Abraham was born, I am</font>.
42:9:3: Jesus answered, <font color="#CC0000">Neither did this man sin, nor his parents: but that the works of God should be made manifest in him</font>.
42:9:4: <font color="#CC0000">We must work the works of him that sent me, while it is day: the night cometh, when no man can work.</font>
42:9:5: <font color="#CC0000">When I am in the world, I am the light of the world.</font>
42:9:7: and said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Go, wash in the pool of Siloam</font> (which is by interpretation, Sent). He went away therefore, and washed, and came seeing.
42:9:35: Jesus heard that they had cast him out; and finding him, he said, <font color="#CC0000">Dost thou believe on the Son of God</font>?
42:9:37: Jesus said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Thou hast both seen him, and he it is that speaketh with thee</font>.
42:9:39: And Jesus said, <font color="#CC0000">For judgment came I into this world, that they that see not may see; and that they that see may become blind</font>.
42:9:41: Jesus said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">If ye were blind, ye would have no sin: but now ye say, We see: your sin remaineth</font>.
42:10:1: <font color="#CC0000">Verily, verily, I say unto you, He that entereth not by the door into the fold of the sheep, but climbeth up some other way, the same is a thief and a robber.</font>
42:10:2: <font color="#CC0000">But he that entereth in by the door is the shepherd of the sheep.</font>
42:10:3: <font color="#CC0000">To him the porter openeth; and the sheep hear his voice: and he calleth his own sheep by name, and leadeth them out.</font>
42:10:4: <font color="#CC0000">When he hath put forth all his own, he goeth before them, and the sheep follow him: for they know his voice.</font>
42:10:5: <font color="#CC0000">And a stranger will they not follow, but will flee from him: for they know not the voice of strangers.</font>
42:10:7: Jesus therefore said unto them again, <font color="#CC0000">Verily, verily, I say unto you, I am the door of the sheep</font>.
42:10:8: <font color="#CC0000">All that came before me are thieves and robbers: but the sheep did not hear them.</font>
42:10:9: <font color="#CC0000">I am the door; by me if any man enter in, he shall be saved, and shall go in and go out, and shall find pasture.</font>
42:10:10: <font color="#CC0000">The thief cometh not, but that he may steal, and kill, and destroy: I came that they may have life, and may have it abundantly.</font>
42:10:11: <font color="#CC0000">I am the good shepherd: the good shepherd layeth down his life for the sheep.</font>
42:10:12: <font color="#CC0000">He that is a hireling, and not a shepherd, whose own the sheep are not, beholdeth the wolf coming, and leaveth the sheep, and fleeth, and the wolf snatcheth them, and scattereth them:</font>
42:10:13: <font color="#CC0000">he fleeth because he is a hireling, and careth not for the sheep.</font>
42:10:14: <font color="#CC0000">I am the good shepherd; and I know mine own, and mine own know me,</font>
42:10:15: <font color="#CC0000">even as the Father knoweth me, and I know the Father; and I lay down my life for the sheep.</font>
42:10:16: <font color="#CC0000">And other sheep I have, which are not of this fold: them also I must bring, and they shall hear my voice; and they shall become one flock, one shepherd.</font>
42:10:17: <font color="#CC0000">Therefore doth the Father love me, because I lay down my life, that I may take it again.</font>
42:10:18: <font color="#CC0000">No one taketh it away from me, but I lay it down of myself. I have power to lay it down, and I have power to take it again. This commandment received I from my Father.</font>
42:10:25: Jesus answered them, <font color="#CC0000">I told you, and ye believe not: the works that I do in my Father’s name, these bear witness of me</font>.
42:10:26: <font color="#CC0000">But ye believe not, because ye are not of my sheep.</font>
42:10:27: <font color="#CC0000">My sheep hear my voice, and I know them, and they follow me:</font>
42:10:28: <font color="#CC0000">and I give unto them eternal life; and they shall never perish, and no one shall snatch them out of my hand.</font>
42:10:29: <font color="#CC0000">My Father, who hath given them unto me, is greater than all; and no one is able to snatch them out of the Father’s hand.</font>
42:10:30: <font color="#CC0000">I and the Father are one.</font>
42:10:32: Jesus answered them, <font color="#CC0000">Many good works have I showed you from the Father; for which of those works do ye stone me</font>?
42:10:34: Jesus answered them, <font color="#CC0000">Is it not written in your law, I said, Ye are gods</font>?
42:10:35: <font color="#CC0000">If he called them gods, unto whom the word of God came (and the scripture cannot be broken),</font>
42:10:36: <font color="#CC0000">say ye of him, whom the Father sanctified and sent into the world, Thou blasphemest; because I said, I am the Son of God?</font>
42:10:37: <font color="#CC0000">If I do not the works of my Father, believe me not.</font>
42:10:38: <font color="#CC0000">But if I do them, though ye believe not me, believe the works: that ye may know and understand that the Father is in me, and I in the Father.</font>
42:11:4: But when Jesus heard it, he said, <font color="#CC0000">This sickness is not unto death, but for the glory of God, that the Son of God may be glorified thereby</font>.
42:11:7: Then after this he saith to the disciples, <font color="#CC0000">Let us go into Judæa again</font>.
42:11:9: Jesus answered, <font color="#CC0000">Are there not twelve hours in the day? If a man walk in the day, he stumbleth not, because he seeth the light of this world</font>.
42:11:10: <font color="#CC0000">But if a man walk in the night, he stumbleth, because the light is not in him.</font>
42:11:11: These things spake he: and after this he saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Our friend Lazarus is fallen asleep; but I go, that I may awake him out of sleep</font>.
42:11:14: Then Jesus therefore said unto them plainly, <font color="#CC0000">Lazarus is dead</font>.
42:11:15: <font color="#CC0000">And I am glad for your sakes that I was not there, to the intent ye may believe; nevertheless let us go unto him.</font>
42:11:23: Jesus saith unto her, <font color="#CC0000">Thy brother shall rise again</font>.
42:11:25: Jesus said unto her, <font color="#CC0000">I am the resurrection, and the life: he that believeth on me, though he die, yet shall he live</font>;
42:11:26: <font color="#CC0000">and whosoever liveth and believeth on me shall never die. Believest thou this?</font>
42:11:34: and said, <font color="#CC0000">Where have ye laid him</font>? They say unto him, Lord, come and see.
42:11:39: Jesus saith, <font color="#CC0000">Take ye away the stone</font>. Martha, the sister of him that was dead, saith unto him, Lord, by this time the body decayeth; for he hath been dead four days.
42:11:40: Jesus saith unto her, <font color="#CC0000">Said I not unto thee, that, if thou believedst, thou shouldest see the glory of God</font>?
42:11:41: So they took away the stone. And Jesus lifted up his eyes, and said, <font color="#CC0000">Father, I thank thee that thou heardest me</font>.
42:11:42: <font color="#CC0000">And I knew that thou hearest me always: but because of the multitude that standeth around I said it, that they may believe that thou didst send me.</font>
42:11:43: And when he had thus spoken, he cried with a loud voice, <font color="#CC0000">Lazarus, come forth</font>.
42:11:44: He that was dead came forth, bound hand and foot with grave-clothes; and his face was bound about with a napkin. Jesus saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Loose him, and let him go</font>.
42:12:7: Jesus therefore said, Suffer <font color="#CC0000">her to keep it against the day of my burying</font>.
42:12:8: <font color="#CC0000">For the poor ye have always with you; but me ye have not always.</font>
42:12:23: And Jesus answereth them, saying, <font color="#CC0000">The hour is come, that the Son of man should be glorified</font>.
42:12:24: <font color="#CC0000">Verily, verily, I say unto you, Except a grain of wheat fall into the earth and die, it abideth by itself alone; but if it die, it beareth much fruit.</font>
42:12:25: <font color="#CC0000">He that loveth his life loseth it; and he that hateth his life in this world shall keep it unto life eternal.</font>
42:12:26: <font color="#CC0000">If any man serve me, let him follow me; and where I am, there shall also my servant be: if any man serve me, him will the Father honor.</font>
42:12:27: <font color="#CC0000">Now is my soul troubled; and what shall I say? Father, save me from this hour. But for this cause came I unto this hour.</font>
42:12:28: <font color="#CC0000">Father, glorify thy name</font>. There came therefore a voice out of heaven, saying, I have both glorified it, and will glorify it again.
42:12:30: Jesus answered and said, <font color="#CC0000">This voice hath not come for my sake, but for your sakes</font>.
42:12:31: <font color="#CC0000">Now is the judgment of this world: now shall the prince of this world be cast out.</font>
42:12:32: <font color="#CC0000">And I, if I be lifted up from the earth, will draw all men unto myself.</font>
42:12:35: Jesus therefore said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Yet a little while is the light among you. Walk while ye have the light, that darkness overtake you not: and he that walketh in the darkness knoweth not whither he goeth</font>.
42:12:36: <font color="#CC0000">While ye have the light, believe on the light, that ye may become sons of light</font>. These things spake Jesus, and he departed and hid himself from them.
42:12:44: And Jesus cried and said, <font color="#CC0000">He that believeth on me, believeth not on me, but on him that sent me</font>.
42:12:45: <font color="#CC0000">And he that beholdeth me beholdeth him that sent me.</font>
42:12:46: <font color="#CC0000">I am come a light into the world, that whosoever believeth on me may not abide in the darkness.</font>
42:12:47: <font color="#CC0000">And if any man hear my sayings, and keep them not, I judge him not: for I came not to judge the world, but to save the world.</font>
42:12:48: <font color="#CC0000">He that rejecteth me, and receiveth not my sayings, hath one that judgeth him: the word that I spake, the same shall judge him in the last day.</font>
42:12:49: <font color="#CC0000">For I spake not from myself; but the Father that sent me, he hath given me a commandment, what I should say, and what I should speak.</font>
42:12:50: <font color="#CC0000">And I know that his commandment is life eternal; the things therefore which I speak, even as the Father hath said unto me, so I speak.</font>
42:13:7: Jesus answered and said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">What I do thou knowest not now; but thou shalt understand hereafter</font>.
42:13:8: Peter saith unto him, Thou shalt never wash my feet. Jesus answered him, <font color="#CC0000">If I wash thee not, thou hast no part with me</font>.
42:13:10: Jesus saith to him, <font color="#CC0000">He that is bathed needeth not save to wash his feet, but is clean every whit: and ye are clean, but not all</font>.
42:13:12: So when he had washed their feet, and taken his garments, and sat down again, he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Know ye what I have done to you</font>?
42:13:13: <font color="#CC0000">Ye call me, Teacher, and, Lord: and ye say well; for so I am.</font>
42:13:14: <font color="#CC0000">If I then, the Lord and the Teacher, have washed your feet, ye also ought to wash one another’s feet.</font>
42:13:15: <font color="#CC0000">For I have given you an example, that ye also should do as I have done to you.</font>
42:13:16: <font color="#CC0000">Verily, verily, I say unto you, A servant is not greater than his lord; neither one that is sent greater than he that sent him.</font>
42:13:17: <font color="#CC0000">If ye know these things, blessed are ye if ye do them.</font>
42:13:18: <font color="#CC0000">I speak not of you all: I know whom I have chosen: but that the scripture may be fulfilled, He that eateth my bread lifted up his heel against me.</font>
42:13:19: <font color="#CC0000">From henceforth I tell you before it come to pass, that, when it is come to pass, ye may believe that I am he.</font>
42:13:20: <font color="#CC0000">Verily, verily, I say unto you, He that receiveth whomsoever I send receiveth me; and he that receiveth me receiveth him that sent me.</font>
42:13:21: When Jesus had thus said, he was troubled in the spirit, and testified, and said, <font color="#CC0000">Verily, verily, I say unto you, that one of you shall betray me</font>.
42:13:26: Jesus therefore answereth, <font color="#CC0000">He it is, for whom I shall dip the sop, and give it him. So when he had dipped the sop, he taketh and giveth it</font> to Judas, the son of Simon Iscariot.
42:13:27: And after the sop, then entered Satan into him. Jesus therefore saith unto him, What <font color="#CC0000">thou doest, do quickly</font>.
42:13:31: When therefore he was gone out, Jesus saith, <font color="#CC0000">Now is the Son of man glorified, and God is glorified in him</font>;
42:13:32: <font color="#CC0000">and God shall glorify him in himself, and straightway shall he glorify him.</font>
42:13:33: <font color="#CC0000">Little children, yet a little while I am with you. Ye shall seek me: and as I said unto the Jews, Whither I go, ye cannot come; so now I say unto you.</font>
42:13:34: <font color="#CC0000">A new commandment I give unto you, that ye love one another; even as I have loved you, that ye also love one another.</font>
42:13:35: <font color="#CC0000">By this shall all men know that ye are my disciples, if ye have love one to another.</font>
42:13:36: Simon Peter saith unto him, Lord, whither goest thou? Jesus answered, <font color="#CC0000">Whither I go, thou canst not follow me now; but thou shalt follow afterwards</font>.
42:13:38: Jesus answereth, <font color="#CC0000">Wilt thou lay down thy life for me? Verily, verily, I say unto thee, The cock shall not crow, till thou hast denied me thrice</font>.
42:14:1: <font color="#CC0000">Let not your heart be troubled: believe in God, believe also in me.</font>
42:14:2: <font color="#CC0000">In my Father’s house are many mansions; if it were not so, I would have told you; for I go to prepare a place for you.</font>
42:14:3: <font color="#CC0000">And if I go and prepare a place for you, I come again, and will receive you unto myself; that where I am, there ye may be also.</font>
42:14:4: <font color="#CC0000">And whither I go, ye know the way.</font>
42:14:6: Jesus saith unto him, <font color="#CC0000">I am the way, and the truth, and the life: no one cometh unto the Father, but by me</font>.
42:14:7: <font color="#CC0000">If ye had known me, ye would have known my Father also: from henceforth ye know him, and have seen him.</font>
42:14:9: Jesus saith unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Have I been so long time with you, and dost thou not know me, Philip? he that hath seen me hath seen the Father; how sayest thou, Show us the Father</font>?
42:14:10: <font color="#CC0000">Believest thou not that I am in the Father, and the Father in me? the words that I say unto you I speak not from myself: but the Father abiding in me doeth his works.</font>
42:14:11: <font color="#CC0000">Believe me that I am in the Father, and the Father in me: or else believe me for the very works’ sake.</font>
42:14:12: <font color="#CC0000">Verily, verily, I say unto you, He that believeth on me, the works that I do shall he do also; and greater works than these shall he do; because I go unto the Father.</font>
42:14:13: <font color="#CC0000">And whatsoever ye shall ask in my name, that will I do, that the Father may be glorified in the Son.</font>
42:14:14: <font color="#CC0000">If ye shall ask anything in my name, that will I do.</font>
42:14:15: <font color="#CC0000">If ye love me, ye will keep my commandments.</font>
42:14:16: <font color="#CC0000">And I will pray the Father, and he shall give you another Comforter, that he may be with you for ever,</font>
42:14:17: <font color="#CC0000">even the Spirit of truth: whom the world cannot receive; for it beholdeth him not, neither knoweth him: ye know him; for he abideth with you, and shall be in you.</font>
42:14:18: <font color="#CC0000">I will not leave you desolate: I come unto you.</font>
42:14:19: <font color="#CC0000">Yet a little while, and the world beholdeth me no more; but ye behold me: because I live, ye shall live also.</font>
42:14:20: <font color="#CC0000">In that day ye shall know that I am in my Father, and ye in me, and I in you.</font>
42:14:21: <font color="#CC0000">He that hath my commandments, and keepeth them, he it is that loveth me: and he that loveth me shall be loved of my Father, and I will love him, and will manifest myself unto him.</font>
42:14:23: Jesus answered and said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">If a man love me, he will keep my word: and my Father will love him, and we will come unto him, and make our abode with him</font>.
42:14:24: <font color="#CC0000">He that loveth me not keepeth not my words: and the word which ye hear is not mine, but the Father’s who sent me.</font>
42:14:25: <font color="#CC0000">These things have I spoken unto you, while yet abiding with you.</font>
42:14:26: <font color="#CC0000">But the Comforter, even the Holy Spirit, whom the Father will send in my name, he shall teach you all things, and bring to your remembrance all that I said unto you.</font>
42:14:27: <font color="#CC0000">Peace I leave with you; my peace I give unto you: not as the world giveth, give I unto you. Let not your heart be troubled, neither let it be fearful.</font>
42:14:28: <font color="#CC0000">Ye heard how I said to you, I go away, and I come unto you. If ye loved me, ye would have rejoiced, because I go unto the Father: for the Father is greater than I.</font>
42:14:29: <font color="#CC0000">And now I have told you before it come to pass, that, when it is come to pass, ye may believe.</font>
42:14:30: <font color="#CC0000">I will no more speak much with you, for the prince of the world cometh: and he hath nothing in me;</font>
42:14:31: <font color="#CC0000">but that the world may know that I love the Father, and as the Father gave me commandment, even so I do. Arise, let us go hence.</font>
42:15:1: <font color="#CC0000">I am the true vine, and my Father is the husbandman.</font>
42:15:2: <font color="#CC0000">Every branch in me that beareth not fruit, he taketh it away: and every branch that beareth fruit, he cleanseth it, that it may bear more fruit.</font>
42:15:3: <font color="#CC0000">Already ye are clean because of the word which I have spoken unto you.</font>
42:15:4: <font color="#CC0000">Abide in me, and I in you. As the branch cannot bear fruit of itself, except it abide in the vine; so neither can ye, except ye abide in me.</font>
42:15:5: <font color="#CC0000">I am the vine, ye are the branches: He that abideth in me, and I in him, the same beareth much fruit: for apart from me ye can do nothing.</font>
42:15:6: <font color="#CC0000">If a man abide not in me, he is cast forth as a branch, and is withered; and they gather them, and cast them into the fire, and they are burned.</font>
42:15:7: <font color="#CC0000">If ye abide in me, and my words abide in you, ask whatsoever ye will, and it shall be done unto you.</font>
42:15:8: <font color="#CC0000">Herein is my Father glorified, that ye bear much fruit; and so shall ye be my disciples.</font>
42:15:9: <font color="#CC0000">Even as the Father hath loved me, I also have loved you: abide ye in my love.</font>
42:15:10: <font color="#CC0000">If ye keep my commandments, ye shall abide in my love; even as I have kept my Father’s commandments, and abide in his love.</font>
42:15:11: <font color="#CC0000">These things have I spoken unto you, that my joy may be in you, and that your joy may be made full.</font>
42:15:12: <font color="#CC0000">This is my commandment, that ye love one another, even as I have loved you.</font>
42:15:13: <font color="#CC0000">Greater love hath no man than this, that a man lay down his life for his friends.</font>
42:15:14: <font color="#CC0000">Ye are my friends, if ye do the things which I command you.</font>
42:15:15: <font color="#CC0000">No longer do I call you servants; for the servant knoweth not what his lord doeth: but I have called you friends; for all things that I heard from my Father I have made known unto you.</font>
42:15:16: <font color="#CC0000">Ye did not choose me, but I chose you, and appointed you, that ye should go and bear fruit, and that your fruit should abide: that whatsoever ye shall ask of the Father in my name, he may give it you.</font>
42:15:17: <font color="#CC0000">These things I command you, that ye may love one another.</font>
42:15:18: <font color="#CC0000">If the world hateth you, ye know that it hath hated me before it hated you.</font>
42:15:19: <font color="#CC0000">If ye were of the world, the world would love its own: but because ye are not of the world, but I chose you out of the world, therefore the world hateth you.</font>
42:15:20: <font color="#CC0000">Remember the word that I said unto you, A servant is not greater than his lord. If they persecuted me, they will also persecute you; if they kept my word, they will keep yours also.</font>
42:15:21: <font color="#CC0000">But all these things will they do unto you for my name’s sake, because they know not him that sent me.</font>
42:15:22: <font color="#CC0000">If I had not come and spoken unto them, they had not had sin: but now they have no excuse for their sin.</font>
42:15:23: <font color="#CC0000">He that hateth me hateth my Father also.</font>
42:15:24: <font color="#CC0000">If I had not done among them the works which none other did, they had not had sin: but now have they both seen and hated both me and my Father.</font>
42:15:25: <font color="#CC0000">But this cometh to pass, that the word may be fulfilled that is written in their law, They hated me without a cause.</font>
42:15:26: <font color="#CC0000">But when the Comforter is come, whom I will send unto you from the Father, even the Spirit of truth, which proceedeth from the Father, he shall bear witness of me:</font>
42:15:27: <font color="#CC0000">and ye also bear witness, because ye have been with me from the beginning.</font>
42:16:1: <font color="#CC0000">These things have I spoken unto you, that ye should not be caused to stumble.</font>
42:16:2: <font color="#CC0000">They shall put you out of the synagogues: yea, the hour cometh, that whosoever killeth you shall think that he offereth service unto God.</font>
42:16:3: <font color="#CC0000">And these things will they do, because they have not known the Father, nor me.</font>
42:16:4: <font color="#CC0000">But these things have I spoken unto you, that when their hour is come, ye may remember them, how that I told you. And these things I said not unto you from the beginning, because I was with you.</font>
42:16:5: <font color="#CC0000">But now I go unto him that sent me; and none of you asketh me, Whither goest thou?</font>
42:16:6: <font color="#CC0000">But because I have spoken these things unto you, sorrow hath filled your heart.</font>
42:16:7: <font color="#CC0000">Nevertheless I tell you the truth: It is expedient for you that I go away; for if I go not away, the Comforter will not come unto you; but if I go, I will send him unto you.</font>
42:16:8: <font color="#CC0000">And he, when he is come, will convict the world in respect of sin, and of righteousness, and of judgment:</font>
42:16:9: <font color="#CC0000">of sin, because they believe not on me;</font>
42:16:10: <font color="#CC0000">of righteousness, because I go to the Father, and ye behold me no more;</font>
42:16:11: <font color="#CC0000">of judgment, because the prince of this world hath been judged.</font>
42:16:12: <font color="#CC0000">I have yet many things to say unto you, but ye cannot bear them now.</font>
42:16:13: <font color="#CC0000">Howbeit when he, the Spirit of truth, is come, he shall guide you into all the truth: for he shall not speak from himself; but what things soever he shall hear, these shall he speak: and he shall declare unto you the things that are to come.</font>
42:16:14: <font color="#CC0000">He shall glorify me: for he shall take of mine, and shall declare it unto you.</font>
42:16:15: <font color="#CC0000">All things whatsoever the Father hath are mine: therefore said I, that he taketh of mine, and shall declare it unto you.</font>
42:16:16: <font color="#CC0000">A little while, and ye behold me no more; and again a little while, and ye shall see me.</font>
42:16:19: Jesus perceived that they were desirous to ask him, and he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Do ye inquire among yourselves concerning this, that I said, A little while, and ye behold me not, and again a little while, and ye shall see me</font>?
42:16:20: <font color="#CC0000">Verily, verily, I say unto you, that ye shall weep and lament, but the world shall rejoice: ye shall be sorrowful, but your sorrow shall be turned into joy.</font>
42:16:21: <font color="#CC0000">A woman when she is in travail hath sorrow, because her hour is come: but when she is delivered of the child, she remembereth no more the anguish, for the joy that a man is born into the world.</font>
42:16:22: <font color="#CC0000">And ye therefore now have sorrow: but I will see you again, and your heart shall rejoice, and your joy no one taketh away from you.</font>
42:16:23: <font color="#CC0000">And in that day ye shall ask me no question. Verily, verily, I say unto you, If ye shall ask anything of the Father, he will give it you in my name.</font>
42:16:24: <font color="#CC0000">Hitherto have ye asked nothing in my name: ask, and ye shall receive, that your joy may be made full.</font>
42:16:25: <font color="#CC0000">These things have I spoken unto you in dark sayings: the hour cometh, when I shall no more speak unto you in dark sayings, but shall tell you plainly of the Father.</font>
42:16:26: <font color="#CC0000">In that day ye shall ask in my name: and I say not unto you, that I will pray the Father for you;</font>
42:16:27: <font color="#CC0000">for the Father himself loveth you, because ye have loved me, and have believed that I came forth from the Father.</font>
42:16:28: <font color="#CC0000">I came out from the Father, and am come into the world: again, I leave the world, and go unto the Father.</font>
42:16:31: Jesus answered them, <font color="#CC0000">Do ye now believe</font>?
42:16:32: <font color="#CC0000">Behold, the hour cometh, yea, is come, that ye shall be scattered, every man to his own, and shall leave me alone: and yet I am not alone, because the Father is with me.</font>
42:16:33: <font color="#CC0000">These things have I spoken unto you, that in me ye may have peace. In the world ye have tribulation: but be of good cheer; I have overcome the world.</font>
42:17:1: These things spake Jesus; and lifting up his eyes to heaven, he said, <font color="#CC0000">Father, the hour is come; glorify thy Son, that the Son may glorify thee</font>:
42:17:2: <font color="#CC0000">even as thou gavest him authority over all flesh, that to all whom thou hast given him, he should give eternal life.</font>
42:17:3: <font color="#CC0000">And this is life eternal, that they should know thee the only true God, and him whom thou didst send, even Jesus Christ.</font>
42:17:4: <font color="#CC0000">I glorified thee on the earth, having accomplished the work which thou hast given me to do.</font>
42:17:5: <font color="#CC0000">And now, Father, glorify thou me with thine own self with the glory which I had with thee before the world was.</font>
42:17:6: <font color="#CC0000">I manifested thy name unto the men whom thou gavest me out of the world: thine they were, and thou gavest them to me; and they have kept thy word.</font>
42:17:7: <font color="#CC0000">Now they know that all things whatsoever thou hast given me are from thee:</font>
42:17:8: <font color="#CC0000">for the words which thou gavest me I have given unto them; and they received them, and knew of a truth that I came forth from thee, and they believed that thou didst send me.</font>
42:17:9: <font color="#CC0000">I pray for them: I pray not for the world, but for those whom thou hast given me; for they are thine:</font>
42:17:10: <font color="#CC0000">and all things that are mine are thine, and thine are mine: and I am glorified in them.</font>
42:17:11: <font color="#CC0000">And I am no more in the world, and these are in the world, and I come to thee. Holy Father, keep them in thy name which thou hast given me, that they may be one, even as we are.</font>
42:17:12: <font color="#CC0000">While I was with them, I kept them in thy name which thou hast given me: and I guarded them, and not one of them perished, but the son of perdition; that the scripture might be fulfilled.</font>
42:17:13: <font color="#CC0000">But now I come to thee; and these things I speak in the world, that they may have my joy made full in themselves.</font>
42:17:14: <font color="#CC0000">I have given them thy word; and the world hated them, because they are not of the world, even as I am not of the world.</font>
42:17:15: <font color="#CC0000">I pray not that thou shouldest take them from the world, but that thou shouldest keep them from the evil one.</font>
42:17:16: <font color="#CC0000">They are not of the world, even as I am not of the world.</font>
42:17:17: <font color="#CC0000">Sanctify them in the truth: thy word is truth.</font>
42:17:18: <font color="#CC0000">As thou didst send me into the world, even so sent I them into the world.</font>
42:17:19: <font color="#CC0000">And for their sakes I sanctify myself, that they themselves also may be sanctified in truth.</font>
42:17:20: <font color="#CC0000">Neither for these only do I pray, but for them also that believe on me through their word;</font>
42:17:21: <font color="#CC0000">that they may all be one; even as thou, Father, art in me, and I in thee, that they also may be in us: that the world may believe that thou didst send me.</font>
42:17:22: <font color="#CC0000">And the glory which thou hast given me I have given unto them; that they may be one, even as we are one;</font>
42:17:23: <font color="#CC0000">I in them, and thou in me, that they may be perfected into one; that the world may know that thou didst send me, and lovedst them, even as thou lovedst me.</font>
42:17:24: <font color="#CC0000">Father, I desire that they also whom thou hast given me be with me where I am, that they may behold my glory, which thou hast given me: for thou lovedst me before the foundation of the world.</font>
42:17:25: <font color="#CC0000">O righteous Father, the world knew thee not, but I knew thee; and these knew that thou didst send me;</font>
42:17:26: <font color="#CC0000">and I made known unto them thy name, and will make it known; that the love wherewith thou lovedst me may be in them, and I in them.</font>
42:18:4: Jesus therefore, knowing all the things that were coming upon him, went forth, and saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Whom seek ye</font>?
42:18:5: They answered him, Jesus of Nazareth. Jesus saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">I am he</font>. And Judas also, who betrayed him, was standing with them.
42:18:7: Again therefore he asked them, <font color="#CC0000">Whom seek ye</font>? And they said, Jesus of Nazareth.
42:18:8: Jesus answered, <font color="#CC0000">I told you that I am he; if therefore ye seek me, let these go their way</font>:
42:18:11: Jesus therefore said unto Peter, <font color="#CC0000">Put up the sword into the sheath: the cup which the Father hath given me, shall I not drink it</font>?
42:18:20: Jesus answered him, <font color="#CC0000">I have spoken openly to the world; I ever taught in synagogues, and in the temple, where all the Jews come together; and in secret spake I nothing</font>.
42:18:21: <font color="#CC0000">Why askest thou me? ask them that have heard me, what I spake unto them: behold, these know the things which I said.</font>
42:18:23: Jesus answered him, <font color="#CC0000">If I have spoken evil, bear witness of the evil: but if well, why smitest thou me</font>?
42:18:34: Jesus answered, <font color="#CC0000">Sayest thou this of thyself, or did others tell it thee concerning me</font>?
42:18:36: Jesus answered, <font color="#CC0000">My kingdom is not of this world: if my kingdom were of this world, then would my servants fight, that I should not be delivered to the Jews: but now is my kingdom not from hence</font>.
42:18:37: Pilate therefore said unto him, Art thou a king then? Jesus answered, <font color="#CC0000">Thou sayest that I am a king. To this end have I been born, and to this end am I come into the world, that I should bear witness unto the truth. Every one that is of the truth heareth my voice</font>.
42:19:11: Jesus answered him, <font color="#CC0000">Thou wouldest have no power against me, except it were given thee from above: therefore he that delivered me unto thee hath greater sin</font>.
42:19:26: When Jesus therefore saw his mother, and the disciple standing by whom he loved, he saith unto his mother, <font color="#CC0000">Woman, behold, thy son</font>!
42:19:27: Then saith he to the disciple, <font color="#CC0000">Behold, thy mother</font>! And from that hour the disciple took her unto his own home.
42:19:28: After this Jesus, knowing that all things are now finished, that the scripture might be accomplished, saith, <font color="#CC0000">I thirst</font>.
42:19:30: When Jesus therefore had received the vinegar, he said, <font color="#CC0000">It is finished</font>: and he bowed his head, and gave up his spirit.
42:20:15: Jesus saith unto her, <font color="#CC0000">Woman, why weepest thou? whom seekest thou</font>? She, supposing him to be the gardener, saith unto him, Sir, if thou hast borne him hence, tell me where thou hast laid him, and I will take him away.
42:20:16: Jesus saith unto her, <font color="#CC0000">Mary</font>. She turneth herself, and saith unto him in Hebrew, Rabboni; which is to say, Teacher.
42:20:17: Jesus saith to her, <font color="#CC0000">Touch me not; for I am not yet ascended unto the Father: but go unto my brethren, and say to them, I ascend unto my Father and your Father, and my God and your God</font>.
42:20:19: When therefore it was evening, on that day, the firstdayof the week, and when the doors were shut where the disciples were, for fear of the Jews, Jesus came and stood in the midst, and saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Peace be unto you</font>.
42:20:21: Jesus therefore said to them again, <font color="#CC0000">Peace be unto you: as the Father hath sent me, even so send I you</font>.
42:20:22: And when he had said this, he breathed on them, and saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Receive ye the Holy</font> Spirit:
42:20:23: <font color="#CC0000">whose soever sins ye forgive, they are forgiven unto them; whose soever sins ye retain, they are retained.</font>
42:20:26: And after eight days again his disciples were within, and Thomas with them. Jesus cometh, the doors being shut, and stood in the midst, and said, <font color="#CC0000">Peace be unto you</font>.
42:20:27: Then saith he to Thomas, <font color="#CC0000">Reach hither thy finger, and see my hands; and reach hither thy hand, and put it into my side: and be not faithless, but believing</font>.
42:20:29: Jesus saith unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Because thou hast seen me, thou hast believed: blessed are they that have not seen, and yet have believed</font>.
42:21:5: Jesus therefore saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Children, have ye</font> aught to eat? They answered him, No.
42:21:6: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Cast the net on the right side of the boat, and ye shall find</font>. They cast therefore, and now they were not able to draw it for the multitude of fishes.
42:21:10: Jesus saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Bring of the fish which ye have now</font> taken.
42:21:12: Jesus saith unto them, <font color="#CC0000">Come and</font> break your fast. And none of the disciples durst inquire of him, Who art thou? knowing that it was the Lord.
42:21:15: So when they had broken their fast, Jesus saith to Simon Peter, <font color="#CC0000">Simon, son of John, lovest thou me more than these</font>? He saith unto him, Yea, Lord; thou knowest that I love thee. He saith unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Feed my lambs</font>.
42:21:16: He saith to him again a second time, <font color="#CC0000">Simon, son of John, lovest thou me</font>? He saith unto him, Yea, Lord; thou knowest that I love thee. He saith unto him, Tend <font color="#CC0000">my sheep</font>.
42:21:17: He saith unto him the third time, <font color="#CC0000">Simon, son of John, lovest thou me</font>? Peter was grieved because he said unto him the third time, Lovest thou me? And he said unto him, Lord, thou knowest all things; thou knowest that I love thee. Jesus saith unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Feed my sheep</font>.
42:21:18: <font color="#CC0000">Verily, verily, I say unto thee, When thou wast young, thou girdedst thyself, and walkedst whither thou wouldest: but when thou shalt be old, thou shalt stretch forth thy hands, and another shall gird thee, and carry thee whither thou wouldest not.</font>
42:21:19: Now this he spake, signifying by what manner of death he should glorify God. And when he had spoken this, he saith unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Follow me</font>.
42:21:22: Jesus saith unto him, <font color="#CC0000">If I will that he tarry till I come, what is that to thee? follow thou me</font>.
43:1:4: and, being assembled together with them, he charged them not to depart from Jerusalem, <font color="#CC0000">but to wait for the promise of the Father, which</font>, said he, <font color="#CC0000">ye heard from me</font>:
43:1:5: <font color="#CC0000">for John indeed baptized with water; but ye shall be baptized in the Holy Spirit not many days hence.</font>
43:1:7: And he said unto them, <font color="#CC0000">It is not for you to know times or seasons, which the Father hath set within his own</font> authority.
43:1:8: <font color="#CC0000">But ye shall receive power, when the Holy Spirit is come upon you: and ye shall be my witnesses both in Jerusalem, and in all Judæa and Samaria, and unto the uttermost part of the earth.</font>
43:9:4: and he fell upon the earth, and heard a voice saying unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Saul, Saul, why persecutest thou me</font>?
43:9:5: <font color="#CC0000">And he said, Who art thou, Lord? And he said, I am Jesus whom thou persecutest:</font>
43:9:6: but rise, <font color="#CC0000">and enter into the city, and it shall be told thee what thou must do</font>.
43:9:10: Now there was a certain disciple at Damascus, named <font color="#CC0000">Ananias</font>; and the Lord said unto him in a vision, Ananias. And he said, Behold, I am here, Lord.
43:9:11: And the Lord said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Arise, and go to the street which is called Straight, and inquire in the house of Judas for one named Saul, a man of Tarsus: for behold, he prayeth</font>;
43:9:12: <font color="#CC0000">and he hath seen a man named Ananias coming in, and laying his hands on him, that he might receive his sight.</font>
43:9:15: But the Lord said unto him, <font color="#CC0000">Go thy way: for he is a chosen vessel unto me, to bear my name before the Gentiles and kings, and the children of Israel</font>:
43:9:16: <font color="#CC0000">for I will show him how many things he must suffer for my name’s sake.</font>
43:11:16: And I remembered the word of the Lord, how he said, <font color="#CC0000">John indeed baptized with water; but ye shall be baptized in the Holy</font> Spirit.
43:18:9: And the Lord said unto Paul in the night by a vision, <font color="#CC0000">Be not afraid, but speak and hold not thy peace</font>:
43:18:10: <font color="#CC0000">for I am with thee, and no man shall set on thee to harm thee: for I have much people in this city.</font>
43:20:35: In all things I gave you an example, that so laboring ye ought to help the weak, and to remember the words of the Lord Jesus, that he himself said, <font color="#CC0000">It is more blessed to give than to receive</font>.
43:22:7: And I fell unto the ground, and heard a voice saying unto me, <font color="#CC0000">Saul, Saul, why persecutest thou me</font>?
43:22:8: And I answered, Who art thou, Lord? And he said unto me, <font color="#CC0000">I am Jesus of Nazareth, whom thou persecutest</font>.
43:22:10: And I said, What shall I do, Lord? And the Lord said unto me, <font color="#CC0000">Arise, and go into Damascus; and there it shall be told thee of all things which are appointed for thee to do</font>.
43:22:18: and saw him saying unto me, <font color="#CC0000">Make haste, and get thee quickly out of Jerusalem; because they will not receive of thee testimony concerning me</font>.
43:22:21: And he said unto me, <font color="#CC0000">Depart: for I will send thee forth far hence unto the Gentiles</font>.
43:23:11: And the night following the Lord stood by him, and said, <font color="#CC0000">Be of good cheer: for as thou hast testified concerning me at Jerusalem, so must thou bear witness also at Rome</font>.
43:26:14: And when we were all fallen to the earth, I heard a voice saying unto me in the Hebrew language, <font color="#CC0000">Saul, Saul, why persecutest thou me? it is hard for thee to kick against the</font> goad.
43:26:15: And I said, Who art thou, Lord? And the Lord said, <font color="#CC0000">I am Jesus whom thou persecutest</font>.
43:26:16: <font color="#CC0000">But arise, and stand upon thy feet: for to this end have I appeared unto thee, to appoint thee a minister and a witness both of the things wherein thou hast seen me, and of the things wherein I will appear unto thee;</font>
43:26:17: <font color="#CC0000">delivering thee from the people, and from the Gentiles, unto whom I send thee,</font>
43:26:18: <font color="#CC0000">to open their eyes, that they may turn from darkness to light and from the power of Satan unto God, that they may receive remission of sins and an inheritance among them that are sanctified by faith in me.</font>
45:11:24: and when he had given thanks, he brake it, and said, <font color="#CC0000">This is my body, which is for you: this do in remembrance of me</font>.
45:11:25: In like manner also the cup, after supper, saying, <font color="#CC0000">This cup is the new covenant in my blood: this do, as often as ye drink it, in remembrance of me</font>.
46:12:9: And he hath said unto me, <font color="#CC0000">My grace is sufficient for thee: for my power is made perfect in weakness</font>. Most gladly therefore will I rather glory in my weaknesses, that the power of Christ may rest upon me.
65:1:8: <font color="#CC0000">I am the Alpha and the Omega, saith the Lord God, who is and who was and who is to come, the</font> Almighty.
65:1:11: <font color="#CC0000">saying, What thou seest, write in a book and send it to the seven churches: unto Ephesus, and unto Smyrna, and unto Pergamum, and unto Thyatira, and unto Sardis, and unto Philadelphia, and unto Laodicea.</font>
65:1:17: And when I saw him, I fell at his feet as one dead. And he laid his right hand upon me, saying, <font color="#CC0000">Fear not; I am the first and the last</font>,
65:1:18: <font color="#CC0000">and the Living one; and I was dead, and behold, I am alive for evermore, and I have the keys of death and of Hades.</font>
65:1:19: <font color="#CC0000">Write therefore the things which thou sawest, and the things which are, and the things which shall come to pass hereafter;</font>
65:1:20: <font color="#CC0000">the mystery of the seven stars which thou sawest in my right hand, and the seven golden candlesticks. The seven stars are the angels of the seven churches: and the seven candlesticks are seven churches.</font>
65:2:1: <font color="#CC0000">To the angel of the church in Ephesus write: These things saith he that holdeth the seven stars in his right hand, he that walketh in the midst of the seven golden candlesticks:</font>
65:2:2: <font color="#CC0000">I know thy works, and thy toil and patience, and that thou canst not bear evil men, and didst try them that call themselves apostles, and they are not, and didst find them false;</font>
65:2:3: <font color="#CC0000">and thou hast patience and didst bear for my name’s sake, and hast not grown weary.</font>
65:2:4: <font color="#CC0000">But I have this against thee, that thou didst leave thy first love.</font>
65:2:5: <font color="#CC0000">Remember therefore whence thou art fallen, and repent and do the first works; or else I come to thee, and will move thy candlestick out of its place, except thou repent.</font>
65:2:6: <font color="#CC0000">But this thou hast, that thou hatest the works of the Nicolaitans, which I also hate.</font>
65:2:7: <font color="#CC0000">He that hath an ear, let him hear what the Spirit saith to the churches. To him that overcometh, to him will I give to eat of the tree of life, which is in the Paradise of God.</font>
65:2:8: <font color="#CC0000">And to the angel of the church in Smyrna write: These things saith the first and the last, who was dead, and lived again:</font>
65:2:9: <font color="#CC0000">I know thy tribulation, and thy poverty (but thou art rich), and the blasphemy of them that say they are Jews, and they are not, but are a synagogue of Satan.</font>
65:2:10: <font color="#CC0000">Fear not the things which thou art about to suffer: behold, the devil is about to cast some of you into prison, that ye may be tried; and ye shall have tribulation ten days. Be thou faithful unto death, and I will give thee the crown of life.</font>
65:2:11: <font color="#CC0000">He that hath an ear, let him hear what the Spirit saith to the churches. He that overcometh shall not be hurt of the second death.</font>
65:2:12: <font color="#CC0000">And to the angel of the church in Pergamum write: These things saith he that hath the sharp two-edged sword:</font>
65:2:13: <font color="#CC0000">I know where thou dwellest, even where Satan’s throne is; and thou holdest fast my name, and didst not deny my faith, even in the days of Antipas my witness, my faithful one, who was killed among you, where Satan dwelleth.</font>
65:2:14: <font color="#CC0000">But I have a few things against thee, because thou hast there some that hold the teaching of Balaam, who taught Balak to cast a stumblingblock before the children of Israel, to eat things sacrificed to idols, and to commit fornication.</font>
65:2:15: <font color="#CC0000">So hast thou also some that hold the teaching of the Nicolaitans in like manner.</font>
65:2:16: <font color="#CC0000">Repent therefore; or else I come to thee quickly, and I will make war against them with the sword of my mouth.</font>
65:2:17: <font color="#CC0000">He that hath an ear, let him hear what the Spirit saith to the churches. To him that overcometh, to him will I give of the hidden manna, and I will give him a white stone, and upon the stone a new name written, which no one knoweth but he that receiveth it.</font>
65:2:18: <font color="#CC0000">And to the angel of the church in Thyatira write: These things saith the Son of God, who hath his eyes like a flame of fire, and his feet are like unto burnished brass:</font>
65:2:19: <font color="#CC0000">I know thy works, and thy love and faith and ministry and patience, and that thy last works are more than the first.</font>
65:2:20: <font color="#CC0000">But I have this against thee, that thou sufferest the woman Jezebel, who calleth herself a prophetess; and she teacheth and seduceth my servants to commit fornication, and to eat things sacrificed to idols.</font>
65:2:21: <font color="#CC0000">And I gave her time that she should repent; and she willeth not to repent of her fornication.</font>
65:2:22: <font color="#CC0000">Behold, I cast her into a bed, and them that commit adultery with her into great tribulation, except they repent of her works.</font>
65:2:23: <font color="#CC0000">And I will kill her children with death; and all the churches shall know that I am he that searcheth the reins and hearts: and I will give unto each one of you according to your works.</font>
65:2:24: <font color="#CC0000">But to you I say, to the rest that are in Thyatira, as many as have not this teaching, who know not the deep things of Satan, as they are wont to say; I cast upon you none other burden.</font>
65:2:25: <font color="#CC0000">Nevertheless that which ye have, hold fast till I come.</font>
65:2:26: <font color="#CC0000">And he that overcometh, and he that keepeth my works unto the end, to him will I give authority over the nations:</font>
65:2:27: <font color="#CC0000">and he shall rule them with a rod of iron, as the vessels of the potter are broken to shivers; as I also have received of my Father:</font>
65:2:28: <font color="#CC0000">and I will give him the morning star.</font>
65:2:29: <font color="#CC0000">He that hath an ear, let him hear what the Spirit saith to the churches.</font>
65:3:1: <font color="#CC0000">And to the angel of the church in Sardis write: These things saith he that hath the seven Spirits of God, and the seven stars: I know thy works, that thou hast a name that thou livest, and thou art dead.</font>
65:3:2: <font color="#CC0000">Be thou watchful, and establish the things that remain, which were ready to die: for I have found no works of thine perfected before my God.</font>
65:3:3: <font color="#CC0000">Remember therefore how thou hast received and didst hear; and keep it, and repent. If therefore thou shalt not watch, I will come as a thief, and thou shalt not know what hour I will come upon thee.</font>
65:3:4: <font color="#CC0000">But thou hast a few names in Sardis that did not defile their garments: and they shall walk with me in white; for they are worthy.</font>
65:3:5: <font color="#CC0000">He that overcometh shall thus be arrayed in white garments; and I will in no wise blot his name out of the book of life, and I will confess his name before my Father, and before his angels.</font>
65:3:6: <font color="#CC0000">He that hath an ear, let him hear what the Spirit saith to the churches.</font>
65:3:7: <font color="#CC0000">And to the angel of the church in Philadelphia write: These things saith he that is holy, he that is true, he that hath the key of David, he that openeth and none shall shut, and that shutteth and none openeth:</font>
65:3:8: <font color="#CC0000">I know thy works (behold, I have set before thee a door opened, which none can shut), that thou hast a little power, and didst keep my word, and didst not deny my name.</font>
65:3:9: <font color="#CC0000">Behold, I give of the synagogue of Satan, of them that say they are Jews, and they are not, but do lie; behold, I will make them to come and worship before thy feet, and to know that I have loved thee.</font>
65:3:10: <font color="#CC0000">Because thou didst keep the word of my patience, I also will keep thee from the hour of trial, that hour which is to come upon the whole world, to try them that dwell upon the earth.</font>
65:3:11: <font color="#CC0000">I come quickly: hold fast that which thou hast, that no one take thy crown.</font>
65:3:12: <font color="#CC0000">He that overcometh, I will make him a pillar in the temple of my God, and he shall go out thence no more: and I will write upon him the name of my God, and the name of the city of my God, the new Jerusalem, which cometh down out of heaven from my God, and mine own new name.</font>
65:3:13: <font color="#CC0000">He that hath an ear, let him hear what the Spirit saith to the churches.</font>
65:3:14: <font color="#CC0000">And to the angel of the church in Laodicea write: These things saith the Amen, the faithful and true witness, the beginning of the creation of God:</font>
65:3:15: <font color="#CC0000">I know thy works, that thou art neither cold nor hot: I would thou wert cold or hot.</font>
65:3:16: <font color="#CC0000">So because thou art lukewarm, and neither hot nor cold, I will spew thee out of my mouth.</font>
65:3:17: <font color="#CC0000">Because thou sayest, I am rich, and have gotten riches, and have need of nothing; and knowest not that thou art the wretched one and miserable and poor and blind and naked:</font>
65:3:18: <font color="#CC0000">I counsel thee to buy of me gold refined by fire, that thou mayest become rich; and white garments, that thou mayest clothe thyself, and that the shame of thy nakedness be not made manifest; and eyesalve to anoint thine eyes, that thou mayest see.</font>
65:3:19: <font color="#CC0000">As many as I love, I reprove and chasten: be zealous therefore, and repent.</font>
65:3:20: <font color="#CC0000">Behold, I stand at the door and knock: if any man hear my voice and open the door, I will come in to him, and will sup with him, and he with me.</font>
65:3:21: <font color="#CC0000">He that overcometh, I will give to him to sit down with me in my throne, as I also overcame, and sat down with my Father in his throne.</font>
65:3:22: <font color="#CC0000">He that hath an ear, let him hear what the Spirit saith to the churches.</font>
65:22:7: <font color="#CC0000">And behold, I come quickly. Blessed is he that keepeth the words of the prophecy of this book.</font>
65:22:12: <font color="#CC0000">Behold, I come quickly; and my reward is with me, to render to each man according as his work is.</font>
65:22:13: <font color="#CC0000">I am the Alpha and the Omega, the first and the last, the beginning and the end.</font>
65:22:16: <font color="#CC0000">I Jesus have sent mine angel to testify unto you these things for the churches. I am the root and the offspring of David, the bright, the morning star.</font>
65:22:20: He who testifieth these things saith, Yea: <font color="#CC0000">I come quickly</font>. Amen: come, Lord Jesus.
